jobs.title: SENIOR ENVIRONMENTAL SCIENTIST (SUPERVISORY) JC-522366
jobs.description:
Department Information

At the California Department of Public Health (CDPH), equity, diversity, and inclusion are at the core of our mission to advance the health and well-being of California’s diverse people and communities. We are genuinely and strongly committed to cultivating and preserving a culture of inclusion and connectedness where we can grow and learn together with a diverse team of employees. In recruiting for team members, we welcome the unique contributions that you can bring to us and the work we do.

The Food and Drug Branch’s (FDB) mission is to protect and enhance the public health and well-being for all through sound science and enforcement of laws and regulations pertaining to food, cosmetics, drugs, medical devices, and industrial hemp products.

“The salary information listed on this advertisement may not reflect recent salary increases from salary adjustments or changes in the fiscal year.”

“CDPH is an E-Verify Employer”

Department Website: https://www.cdph.ca.gov

Job Description And Duties

THERE IS ONE VACANCY BEING ADVERTISED ACROSS MULTIPLE COUNTIES. THE INCUMBENT WILL BE HEADQUARTERED IN THE COUNTY NEAREST THE RESIDENCE OF THE SELECTED CANDIDATE. THE COUNTIES ARE: * Sacramento, Contra Costa, Fresno, Los Angeles, San Bernardino, and Orange County.*

This position supports the California Department of Public Health’s (CDPH) mission and strategic plan by promoting health and wellness and improving state health outcomes by advancing protective measures and reducing risks.

The Senior Environmental Scientist (Supervisory) is responsible for planning, organizing, directing, overseeing, and determining the effectiveness of the scientific, legal, educational, complaint, and inspection activities in the Standardization and Continuous Improvement Unit (SCIU). The incumbent supervises program staff, evaluates their performance, provides subject matter expertise on complaints, conducts staff training, and supports technical and enforcement programs within the Food and Drug Branch (FDB).

The incumbent works under the Chief, Food and Drug Section of the Food and Drug Branch, in the Food Investigations and Technical Support Section (FITSS), SCIU.

Please let us know how you heard about our position by taking this brief survey:

https://www.surveymonkey.com/r/CDPHRecruitment

You will find additional information about the job in the Duty Statement
.

jobs.title: Data Analyst
jobs.description:
Pay Rate: $28.85-34.00 per hour
Status: Non-Exempt; Full-time (40 hours per week)
Program Name: CalAIM #580 (CITED 4)
Reports to: CalAIM Director
Location: Remote or Hybrid
Funded until: 12/31/2026

﻿ JOB SUMMARY:

Under direct supervision of the Program Director(s), the Data Analyst will play a critical role in helping the organization define, implement, and operationalize meaningful performance metrics for CalAIM programs. This role plays a critical part in ensuring program performance, compliance with Department of Health Care Services (DHCS) and Medi-Cal Managed Care Plan (MCP) requirements, and data-driven decision-making to improve member outcomes. The analyst works closely with program leadership, care managers, and compliance teams. This role combines analytical expertise with programmatic understanding and change management skills.
The ideal candidate will help leadership decide what KPIs matter, translate those KPIs into practical data workflows, and support staff in using data to improve outcomes, contract performance, and reporting accuracy.
ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS:

Help leadership and program staff identify and define key performance indicators (KPIs) for CalAIM programs
Analyze case management and program data to track performance and outcomes
Create simple reports and dashboards for internal use and external reporting
Support reporting for CalAIM programs: Enhanced Care Management (ECM), Community Supports, or other delegated services
Improve data collection, data quality, and documentation processes
Train staff on how to use reports and understand KPIs
Track program performance metrics, KPIs, and contract deliverables (e.g., engagement rates, care plan completion, outcomes)
Provide ad hoc data support for program planning and quality improvement
Other duties as assigned

QUALIFICATION REQUIREMENTS

To perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to fulfill each essential duty satisfactorily. The qualifying requirements listed below are representative of the knowledge, skill, judgment, and/or abilities required. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

REQUIRED EDUCTION AND/OR EXPERIENCE:

Bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience
2+ years of experience working with data in healthcare, public health, or social services
Strong Excel skills and experience analyzing datasets
Familiarity with Power Pivot and Power Query
Ability to explain data clearly to non-technical staff
Ability to communicate effectively in both written and oral form.
Strong attention to detail, organization, and follow-through
Comfortable working in a fast-changing, resource-limited environment

LANGUAGE SKILLS

Must read and write fluently in English, with good communication and writing skills.

MATHEMATICAL SKILLS

The ability to perform simple arithmetic calculations is required to establish totals for timecards, supply requests, etc.

REASONING ABILITY

Ability to work independently, exercise initiative and good judgment and solve practical problems within established limits and guidelines. Ability to recognize instances in which consultation with a Senior Director and other project staff should be sought before a decision is made.

WORK ENVIRONMENT   

This position is primarily a remote position with hybrid option. When working remotely the employee will need an appropriate workspace (a quiet, private space) with a secure internet connection. Attendance at virtual meetings will be required and the employee may be required to travel for occasional in-person meetings or trainings. 

Physical demands will include sitting, bending, lifting, driving, and walking.

jobs.title: GIS Specialist
jobs.description:
Why You’ll Love Working Here

At ERRG, we believe that each of our projects are significant and the most critical ingredient for success is our people. Our people provide the talent, experience, and passion that lies at the heart of every one of our projects. That is why we can provide the challenges and inspiration to help our people grow professionally and personally.

Some of the great benefits we offer for eligible roles
, may include competitive compensation, hands-on training, educational assistance, and opportunities for career growth and advancement.

About the Role

GIS Specialist will compile and organize data from maps, databases, field surveys, and other sources to generate maps for various environmental projects. This position will predominantly be office GIS assignments but may include data collection related field work tasks on occasion.

What You'll Do

Assist with performing environmental work involving collection of environmental data from site investigations and oversight of construction activities.
Assist with spatial data creation, maintenance and dissemination, end-user support and the generation of maps and other GIS information and products.
Assist in preparing topographical and relief maps in ArcGIS using survey data.
Compile and organize GIS data from maps, databases, and other sources.
Frequently interact with technical staff members (e.g., engineers, geologists, and scientists) to assess their GIS/mapping needs and update them on the status of their map generation requests.
Provide support to (backstops) others during peak workload events.
Performs quality control on GIS data.
Accept responsibility for meeting standards of quality required by ERRG.
Meet internal deadlines.
Work effectively, both independently and as part of a dynamic team.
Assist in development and maintenance of company-wide templates and mapping standards.
Communicate effectively with others; ask appropriate questions to clarify expectations and promote learning.
Adhere to project-specific and corporate-wide safety policies.
Learn and follow appropriate data standards requested by clients, such as the Department of Defense’s Spatial Data Standards for Facilities, Infrastructure, and Environment (SDSFIE) and the Federal Geographic Data Committee (FGDC) Standards for metadata.

What You Bring

Bachelor’s degree from a 4-year college or university.
5+ years of industry-related experience.
Familiarity with ArcGIS Online and apps.
Proficiency using ArcGIS Pro and ArcMap.
Basic knowledge of SQL and/or Python programming languages.
Familiarity with Trimble Pathfinder Office software, Trimble GPS units and field data collectors.
Proficiency with Microsoft software (Excel, Access and/or SQL Server).
Ability to work on multiple projects simultaneously.
Understanding basic geographic principles, including projections and transformations.
Excellent written and oral communication skills.

California Salary Range: $90,000 USD - $100,000 USD

Employment is contingent upon successful completion of a background check and drug screening.

ERRG is committed to providing a safe work environment and to fostering the health and well-being of its employees. As part of the company's commitment to safeguard the wellbeing of its employees and to provide a safe environment for everyone, ERRG maintains a drug free workplace and complies with the Drug-Free Workplace Act.

ERRG is proud to be an affirmative action / equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration without regard to race, creed, gender, marital status, sexual orientation, citizenship status, color, religion, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, or any other status protected under local, state, or federal laws.

jobs.title: Program Coordinator, Forestry
jobs.description:
Company Overview

SCS Global Services is a pioneer and leader in the field of sustainability standards and third-party certification, working across the economy in the natural resources, built environment, food and agriculture, consumer products, and climate sectors for over 40 years. As a Benefit Corporation, we are committed to socially and environmentally responsible business practices, and through the application of sound science, we are driving positive change.

Job Overview

The Program Coordinator, Forestry is a member of the Forest Certification Program team, a growing and evolving program dedicated to delivering third-party certification services that promote sustainable forest management and responsible sourcing practices. This position primarily supports the Technical Director, Forestry, while also providing coordination and operational support to the broader Forestry leadership team and program staff as needed to ensure the effective delivery of program activities and client services.

Relevant Standards/Schemes

American Tree Farm System

Forest Stewardship Council (FSC)

PEFC Chain of Custody

Responsible Wood Certification

Sustainable Forestry Initiative (SFI)

Sustainable Biomass Program (SBP) Certification

Others, as determined

Essential Duties And Responsibilities

Program and Project Coordination

Coordinate day-to-day program operations, ensuring tasks, schedules, and deliverables are completed accurately and on time
Maintain program calendars, track project milestones, and prepare routine progress updates and reports
Assist with the management of program projects aimed at improving processes, meeting accreditation requirements, and streamlining operations
Support resource planning and logistics for meetings, events, training sessions, and other program activities
Assist in coordinating cross-functional teams to ensure effective communication and workflow alignment
Track program expenses and reconcile against budgeted amounts
Review invoices from auditors and scheme owners, submit them for approval, monitor invoice status, and identify potential cost savings and operational efficiencies
Support the collection and analysis of data for financial and operational reporting
Assist program management in organizing team meetings, events, and special initiatives
Manage new transaction verification projects as requested by Accreditation Services International (ASI)
Ensure program activities comply with organizational policies, accreditation requirements, and quality standards

Client Support and Communication

Provide exemplary customer service in response to requests and inquiries
Provide information to clients regarding the certification process and relevant program requirements
Manage daily communications with clients, SCS program personnel, contract auditors, accreditation bodies, certification schemes, and other stakeholders
Support the program through effective client relationship management within a collaborative team environment
Manage and coordinate responses to conformance-related inquiries from accreditation bodies, certification schemes, and stakeholders
Draft, review, and distribute program-related communications, updates, and guidance materials

Administrative and Operational Support

Maintain and organize program documentation, records, databases, and management systems to ensure accuracy, accessibility, and version control
Support internal administrative processes, including document control, contract management, procurement, and tracking activities
Maintain and update Work Instructions, templates, guides, and other operational tools and reference materials
Coordinate meeting logistics, agendas, minutes, and follow-up actions
Draft and distribute program communications, updates, and training materials as needed
Facilitate document translations and support the preparation of presentations, outreach materials, and other program resources
Support managers and team members with training activities and the implementation of program processes and tools
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure consistent execution of program activities and operational goals

Minimum Qualifications

Associate's Degree in Natural Resources, Business Administration, or a related field or
Equivalent combination of education and/or relevant work experience and
2+ years of professional experience
Proficient in Microsoft Office applications including Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and Outlook (Advanced proficiency)
Exceptional database management skills with ability to rapidly learn new systems as necessary
Ability to prioritize multiple tasks and meet deadlines
Ability to manage time, work well independently, and work in team-oriented situations
Ability to provide excellent customer service and maintain a professional demeanor at all times
Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, customer-service oriented, collaborative team environment
Proficiency in English with excellent writing and verbal communication

Preferred Qualifications

Bachelor's Degree in a related field
2+ years of experience in certification of relevant forestry standards (FSC, PEFC, SFI, SBP)

The above description is intended to describe the general nature and level of work being performed. It is not intended to be an exhaustive list of all responsibilities, duties, and skills required. Additional duties outside of normal responsibilities may be required from time to time as assigned.

Working Remotely

At this time, SCS Global Services is recruiting all open roles to be remote; allowing our employees the ability to work flexibly and allowing SCS as a company to diversify the experience and perspectives of our growing workforce. This role will be based out of your home office.

Estimated Hourly Salary

$20.40 - $26.44

EEOC Statement

SCS provides equal employment opportunities (EEO) to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, national origin, age, disability, genetic information, marital status, amnesty, or status as a covered veteran in accordance with applicable federal, state and local laws. SCS complies with applicable state and local laws governing nondiscrimination in employment in every location in which the company has facilities. This policy applies to all terms and conditions of employment, including, but not limited to, hiring, placement, promotion, termination, layoff, recall, transfer, leaves of absence, compensation, and training.

jobs.title: Project Coordinator
jobs.description:
About Pilot R&D
We are known for tackling the biggest challenges in food and turning ambitious ideas into reality. To make things happen, we navigate a wide range of relationships — from our clients to ingredient suppliers to co-packers and beyond — while doing rigorous product development work in our Berkeley kitchen and lab. We are a small, talented team that takes the work seriously and treats one another with respect.
​
Role Mission
Own the operational coordination of all active Pilot R&D client projects — scheduling, tracking, logistics, and documentation — so that the Relationship Manager can manage a larger portfolio of engagements without working at overcapacity, and the founding partners have zero involvement in day-to-day project operations.
​
Outcomes: What Must Be True at 12 Months
1. Project momentum is owned end-to-end
. No active project sits idle for more than 48 business hours without a documented next action and an owner. The coordinator tracks every open item across all active projects in a shared system — not email — and updates it daily. The Relationship Manager and founding partners can see the current status of every project at any time without having to ask.
​
2. The Relationship Manager's coordination burden is measurably reduced
. She can point to specific tasks she no longer touches — scheduling, milestone tracking, sample logistics, document prep — that this person now owns reliably. Target: she recaptures at least 8 hours/week of time currently spent on tasks a coordinator could handle. (target: by month 6)

3. 15+ concurrent projects tracked in real time with no gaps
. The project tracker reflects current status, next steps, and outstanding items for every active engagement at all times. The Relationship Manager never discovers a status she didn't know about or a deadline that wasn't flagged. (target: by month 4)

4. Client tastings are supported such that one rather than 2 founders need to attend
. Project Coordinator is able to attend all client tastings, in-person and remote, to assess client reactions and needs, take notes, capture next steps, answer questions, and ensure smooth run-of-show such that only one founder need to be present to lead tastings vs. two. (target: by month 9)

5. Average project duration is improved by 10% with path to 20%
. Project Coordinator is ushering projects to completion and keeps things moving during the highest risk points in the projects, namely when third-parties have greater influence on timeline. The Project Coordinator is also preparing Final Deliverables timely upon completion of the trial run to ensure Final Deliverables are packaged and presented to clients within 2 weeks of the scope being completed.

6. Every client-facing meeting has a prep package delivered 24 hours in advance — unprompted
. Agendas, prior session notes, open items, and any relevant documents ready before every call. Zero instances of the team walking into a client meeting underprepared due to coordination failure.

7. Sample logistics run without founder or Relationship Manager involvement
. All Client sample requests, shipment tracking, and follow-ups for all active projects are owned and executed by this person from day one of ownership (target: by day 60).

8. Five or more core coordination SOPs documented
. The five most repeatable coordination workflows (e.g., new project kickoff, client status update cycle, sample request process, proposal handoff, project closeout) are written up as clear SOPs that any future coordinator could follow without shadowing.

9. Client communication is professional and reflects well on Pilot
. All routine written communication to clients (meeting confirmations, document requests, status check-ins) sent on behalf of the team is clear, warm, and accurate. Zero instances of a client complaint about responsiveness or communication quality originating from this role.

What You Bring
Required
:
1–3 years of professional experience in a coordination, project support, operations, or client-facing administrative role
Exceptional organizational skills — you use a system, not your memory, to track tasks and deadlines
Proactive communication style — you send updates before people have to ask, and you follow up without being reminded
Comfortable managing external relationships with vendors, suppliers, or clients — including when they're unresponsive
Strong written communication — clear, professional, and typo-free
Experience managing multiple concurrent priorities without things slipping
An adventurous palate; a passion for food!
Ability to work from our office at least several days a week, including for client tastings

Preferred:

Experience in a professional services, consulting, or agency environment
Exposure to food and beverage, CPG, manufacturing, or supply chain (helpful but not required)
Familiarity with project management tools and Google Suite, including Google Sheets

Competencies

Proactive follow-through: Doesn't wait to be asked. Sends reminders unprompted. Tracks every open item and chases it.
Project coordination discipline: Uses a system — not memory — to manage tasks, deadlines, and handoffs across multiple active projects.
Relationship tenacity: Comfortable calling a co-packer or supplier for the fifth time. Knows when to escalate vs. when to push harder.
Written & verbal communication: Writes clear, professional client and vendor emails. Communicates status without being asked.
Accountability ownership: "My job is to make the project move" — doesn't hide behind excuses, unresponsive third parties, or unclear instructions.
Learning agility: No F&B background required, but must have a passion for food, absorb technical context quickly, and use it to have credible conversations with co-packers and suppliers.
Organizational precision: Typo-free, deadline-aware, detail-oriented. Catches things others miss because they pay attention.
Energy and initiative: Sees a stalled project as a problem to fix, not someone else's fault. Acts, then reports.

What We Offer
Full benefits: medical, dental, vision, 401k with match
A small, talented team that takes the work seriously, but not themselves. We respect each other and laughter is a regular feature of our day
The opportunity to taste all manner of delicious and weird food and beverages
Real ownership from day one — you'll matter immediately, not after a two-year ramp up

jobs.title: Project Coordinator (Architecture)
jobs.description:
About the Role:

The Crowd is excited to be partnering with a highly respected architecture and design practice known for delivering impactful projects across a diverse range of sectors. With a collaborative culture and a strong reputation for design excellence, this firm is seeking a Project Coordinator to join its San Francisco team in a full-time capacity.

This position is ideal for a highly organized professional who enjoys keeping projects, teams, and stakeholders aligned behind the scenes. Working closely with senior leadership and project teams, you will play a key role in supporting project operations, coordinating documentation and schedules, managing administrative processes, and helping ensure projects remain on track from start to finish. Experience within architecture, design, engineering, or construction is a plus.

This is a hybrid opportunity offering strong benefits, professional development opportunities, and long-term growth potential.

Key Responsibilities:

Provide day-to-day coordination and administrative support across multiple active projects and project teams
Maintain project schedules, milestone trackers, action logs, and project-related documentation to ensure information remains accurate and up to date
Coordinate meetings, prepare agendas, document key takeaways, and track follow-up actions to support effective communication among stakeholders
Assist with project financial administration, including billing support, invoice tracking, consultant coordination, and contract-related documentation
Support senior leadership with project reporting, resource planning, operational initiatives, and other activities that contribute to successful project delivery

Key Skills / Requirements:

2–5+ years of experience in project coordination, project administration, operations support, executive assistance, or a similar role
Exceptional organizational and time-management skills, with the ability to manage multiple priorities and deadlines simultaneously
Strong written and verbal communication skills, along with a proactive and solutions-oriented approach
Proficiency with Microsoft Office Suite, Google Workspace, spreadsheets, and project tracking tools
Experience within architecture, design, engineering, construction, or real estate environments is a plus

To apply for this position please click on the apply button to attach your resume (and portfolio for design positions).

By applying for this role, you are confirming that you agree to our Privacy Policy (full details are shown at the bottom of our website), and that all details submitted by you are correct and to the best of your knowledge.

The Crowd is an equal opportunities employer and agency.

jobs.title: Junior Geologist, Blue Moon
jobs.description:
Geologist, Junior - Blue Moon

Mariposa County, California

The Blue Moon Mine is seeking a motivated and detail-oriented Junior Geologist
to join our dynamic team in Mariposa County, California. The successful candidate will support geological operations, contribute to exploration, logging and modeling activities, and gain hands-on experience in a cutting-edge mining environment focused on responsible resource development.

The Junior Geologist is accountable for Supervising at least 2 geology technicians.

Key Responsibilities

Exploration:
Assist with discovery focused exploration on specific projects within the Blue Moon project through:

Increased geological understanding of the project and wider region
Developing good exploration targets with rigorous, but cost-effective testing and evaluation
Implement a variety of field exploration programs
Communicating results, observations and recommendations to management
Ensure all operations are conducted in a safe and environmentally sensitive manner with due regard to local community concerns
Expected to fully participate in geological mapping, drill core or chip logging, surface sampling, drill program execution, in addition to any supervisory responsibilities

Safety

Demonstrate personal commitment in regard to safety and environmental and Social Responsibility practices.
Ensure that agreed procedures, safeguards and controls are applied and used.
Ensure all activities are conducted safely and in accordance with Company standards and procedures
Ensure hazard management plans are observed.
Keep statutory and internal reporting as required.
Active participation to the Health & Safety system development

People

Supervise and review the work of direct reports and others and provide feedback to the Senior Geologist
Overview, provide direction and act as a technical reference point and mentor for staff while ensuring work outputs are in line with exploration program.
Be responsible for implementing and ongoing monitoring of performance and development review plans for all direct reports.
Ensure personnel have the appropriate equipment, licenses, skills, induction and training to safely undertake their tasks.
Encourage and facilitate effective and supportive communication between all team members and related department.
Be an active team member of the exploration group and company as a whole but also be proactive in individual lateral and forward thinking.

Key Performance Indicators

Safety

Assist with constant improvements of the Company’s safety systems
Achieve zero LTI’s

Production

Complete a minimum of 5,000m of exploration drilling for the financial year
Assist with maintaining daily/weekly/monthly quality control and exploration deliverable targets
Assist with preparation of daily and weekly reports by the due date
Participate actively in staff development and training

Behavioural Competencies

Problem Solving
Interpersonal Skills
Demonstrates strong conceptual skills and be capable of developing lateral and practical solutions.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Ability to respond to a changing work environment.
The ability to manage multiple projects and tasks at the same time.
Active driver

Qualifications

Bachelor of Science (Geology) or related tertiary qualification.
M.sc Degree (desirable)

Technical Capabilities

Advanced use of various company & departmental systems
Computer knowledge: achieve proficient knowledge and constant self-improvement of all aspects of geological and reporting software required
Relevant field geology, technical and academic experience
Knowledge of the local exploration and mining regulations
Good time management and organizational skills

Role Challenges

Aid in the discovery of additional high-grade, economic ore deposits through systematic exploration and lateral thinking
Create and maintain harmony and efficiency within the geological team, and the Company as a whole
Manage, execute and report on results from exploration programs and geological projects
Work in collaboration with the Senior Geologist to gather and interpret critical geological, geochemical and geophysical datasets to identify the most prospective targets that have the potential to yield a significant ore discovery.

Pay Range And Compensation Package

Compensation details will be discussed during the interview process.

Equal Opportunity Statement

We are committed to diversity and inclusivity in our hiring practices and encourage applications from all qualified individuals.

Please note: Our U.S. business entity is Keystone Mines Inc.

jobs.title: Data Analyst (MS Technologies)
jobs.description:
Determining compensation for this role (and others) at Vaco/Highspring depends upon a wide array of factors including but not limited to the individual's skill sets, experience and training, licensure and certifications, office location and other geographic considerations, as well as other business and organizational needs. With that said, as required by local law in geographies that require salary range disclosure, Vaco/Highspring notes the salary range for the role is noted in this job posting. The individual may also be eligible for discretionary bonuses, and can participate in medical, dental, and vision benefits as well as the company's 401(k) retirement plan.

Additional disclaimer:

Unless otherwise noted in the job description, the position Vaco/Highspring is filing for is occupied. Please note, however, that Vaco/Highspring is regularly asked to provide talent to other organizations. By submitting to this position, you are agreeing to be included in our talent pool for future hiring for similarly qualified positions. Submissions to this position are subject to the use of AI to perform preliminary candidate screenings, focused on ensuring minimum job requirements noted in the position are satisfied. Further assessment of candidates beyond this initial phase within Vaco/Highspring will be otherwise assessed by recruiters and hiring managers. Vaco/Highspring does not have knowledge of the tools used by its clients in making final hiring decisions and cannot opine on their use of AI products.

Desired Skills and Experience

What You'll Do

Design, develop, and maintain Power BI dashboards and reporting solutions used across the organization.
Write and optimize SQL queries to analyze and transform data from multiple sources.
Create scalable data models and calculations using DAX and the Microsoft data stack.
Partner with stakeholders across manufacturing, finance, logistics, marketing, and executive teams to understand reporting needs and deliver actionable insights.
Support analytics initiatives using tools including Power BI, Azure Analysis Services, Databricks, and SQL.
Assist with automation and advanced analytics efforts using Python when appropriate.
Help expand reporting capabilities and improve how information is delivered throughout the business.
Participate in emerging AI initiatives and future-focused analytics projects as the organization continues to invest in new technologies.
What We're Looking For

Experience building reports and dashboards with Power BI.
Strong SQL skills for data extraction, analysis, and manipulation.
Experience with DAX and data modeling.
Familiarity with the Microsoft data ecosystem.
Strong communication skills and the ability to work effectively with both technical and business stakeholders.
A collaborative mindset and desire to continuously learn and grow.
Nice to Have

Experience with Databricks.
Exposure to Python for data analysis or automation.
Manufacturing industry experience.

Equal Opportunity Notice

jobs.title: Senior Data Analyst - 3056-02
jobs.description:
Delaware Nation Industries is seeking an experienced Senior Operations Research Analyst to support the Air National Guard Plans, Programs and Requirements Directorate (NGB/A8). The Air National Guard strategic planning processes enable a Guard force that is capable and sustainable as a cost-effective element of national security.

Plan and execute procedures for requesting, review, acceptance, storage, retrieval, updating and dissemination of cost, programmatic and technical data
Design, build, and manage scalable data pipelines, enterprise data warehouses, and architecture to support advanced analytics and operations research.
Perform a broad range of operations research analysis functions to examine unique problems through creativity, innovative techniques, advance approaches, and/or new technologies
Provide data science, data engineering, and architecture expertise, advisory support and assistance to Air National Guard programs, projects, and operations
Develop and maintain data models, data integration frameworks, and enterprise data architecture to ensure optimal data quality, reliability, and security.
Attend meetings, conferences, briefings, and seminars
Maintain proficiency through participation in technical and professional societies, associations, academia, symposia, research, and training
Research, write, and present complex data analysis results in an understandable format for senior leaders and decision makers. Technical data visualization products may be in the form of reports, informational graphics, and/or briefings
Inform Air National Guard studies, analysis, and lessons learned
Collaborate across directorates to synchronize datasets and reporting, ensuring seamless data interoperability and establishing robust data governance.

Requirements

Secret Security Clearance
Bachelor's Degree in Operations Research, Data Science, Computer Science, Data Engineering, Analytics, Statistics, Math, or Economics
Experience working in the Department of Defense environment, preferably the Air Force or National Guard
Highly proficient in computational programming languages such as Python, SQL, or related languages, with strong experience in developing and maintaining robust ETL/ELT pipelines
Proven experience designing, deploying, and maintaining scalable enterprise data architectures, data lakes, and cloud-based data environments (e.g., AWS, Azure, or GCP)
Proficiency with advanced database management, relational/non-relational databases, and big data processing technologies (e.g., Spark, Hadoop, PostgreSQL)
Highly proficient in data visualization tools such as Plotly, PowerBI, Tableau, or related tool
Ability to analyze complex issues and present them in a clear and concise manner
Understanding of Air National Guard functions and missions under Title 10 and Title 32
Highly desired: Master's Degree

Covers 100% of employee benefit premiums, including Medical (PPO or HDHP Option), Vision, Dental

jobs.title: Senior Excel Data Analyst (Remote)
jobs.description:
Role:
Senior Excel Data Analyst (Remote)
Location:
Remote (Work from Anywhere)
Payout:
Competitive, based on experience

Role Overview:

This role requires a Data Analyst (Excel) to provide data management and analysis services on a contractual basis. The work involves collecting, organizing, and transforming data to generate actionable insights for business decision-making. The role supports operational efficiency and data-driven strategies in a technology-focused environment.

Key Responsibilities:

• Collect and compile data from multiple sources into structured Excel spreadsheets.
• Clean, validate, and standardize data to ensure accuracy and consistency.
• Build and maintain Excel-based dashboards and reports for performance tracking.
• Perform ad-hoc data analysis to identify trends, patterns, and anomalies.
• Document processes, formulas, and methodologies for reproducibility and team reference.

Required Skills & Qualifications:

• Proficiency in Microsoft Excel, including advanced functions, pivot tables, and data visualization tools.
• Experience with data cleaning, validation, and transformation using Excel.
• Ability to interpret raw data and produce clear, concise summaries and reports.
• Familiarity with basic statistical analysis and trend identification in datasets.
• Strong attention to detail and commitment to maintaining high data integrity standards.

More About the Opportunity:

This opportunity allows you to contribute to a technology-driven organization by ensuring reliable, timely data is available for strategic and operational decisions. The role offers exposure to cross-functional data challenges with potential for growth in analytical methodologies and tooling.

Equal Opportunity Employer:

We hire based on skills and expertise. All qualified candidates are welcome regardless of background, experience, or prior employment history. Applications are reviewed solely on demonstrated technical ability and qualifications.

jobs.title: Data Analyst, Manager
jobs.description:
About The Company

Lockheed Martin is a global aerospace, defense, and security company renowned for its innovative solutions and cutting-edge technology. With a rich history of delivering advanced systems and services to government and commercial clients worldwide, Lockheed Martin is committed to pushing the boundaries of science and engineering. The company operates across various sectors including aeronautics, space, missile defense, and cybersecurity, fostering a culture of excellence, integrity, and continuous improvement. As a leader in technological innovation, Lockheed Martin strives to create a safer, more secure world through its diverse portfolio of products and services, supporting national security and advancing scientific discovery.

About The Role

The Data Analyst Manager will play a pivotal role within the Aero F&BO Digital Transformation Team, serving as a strategic leader responsible for integrating financial analysis, budgeting, forecasting, and cost management with advanced data modeling and visualization techniques. This position involves translating complex financial and operational data into actionable insights, leveraging AI-enhanced analytics to support organizational decision-making. The manager will lead projects, mentor analysts, and collaborate with cross-functional teams to drive digital transformation initiatives that enhance operational efficiency and strategic planning. The role requires a results-oriented professional capable of managing multiple priorities, communicating effectively with senior leadership, and fostering a culture of continuous improvement and innovation.

Qualifications

5+ years of related experience in data analysis, finance, or a similar field
Degree in Analytics, Accounting, Finance, or a related discipline
Experience with digital transformation initiatives
Experience with AI tool development and implementation
Proven leadership experience managing high-performing teams
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Excellent communication skills for presenting complex data insights
Proficiency in data modeling, visualization, and AI-driven analytics tools
Ability to manage multiple projects and prioritize effectively

Responsibilities

Serve as a strategic leader within the Aero F&BO Digital Transformation Team, guiding analytics initiatives
Apply expertise in financial analysis, budgeting, forecasting, and cost management to support business objectives
Leverage advanced data modeling and visualization techniques to convert complex data into actionable insights
Develop and deliver AI-enhanced analytics and reporting solutions to inform organizational decision-making
Lead project management activities including defining scope, resource allocation, scheduling, and deadline enforcement
Utilize critical thinking and problem-solving skills to identify trends, address challenges, and recommend data-driven solutions
Mentor, coach, and develop analysts, fostering a culture of accountability, collaboration, and continuous improvement
Ensure team adherence to project timelines, quality standards, and deliverables
Communicate complex analytical findings and strategic recommendations to senior finance leadership and stakeholders
Partner with cross-functional teams to support digital transformation initiatives and operational excellence

Benefits

Flexible work schedules including onsite and remote options
Comprehensive benefits package covering medical, dental, and vision insurance
Life insurance and disability coverage (short-term and long-term)
401(k) retirement plan with company match
Flexible Spending Accounts (FSA) and Employee Assistance Program (EAP)
Educational assistance and parental leave policies
Paid time off and holidays
Relocation assistance may be available

Equal Opportunity

Lockheed Martin is an equal opportunity employer. Qualified candidates will be considered without regard to legally protected characteristics, including race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, or any other protected class. The company values diversity and is committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.

jobs.title: Senior Data Analyst
jobs.description:
Swooped is a Talent Platform (not a staffing agency). We are not the employer for this role and do not make hiring decisions. Clicking "Apply" takes you to Swooped to review the role and hiring company details.

About the Opportunity

Owls in this role provide insights and analyses at all stages of project lifecycles, from earliest prototyping to live engagement with players. This position balances an analytical mindset and technical expertise while diving into data and turning it into meaningful insights. The role involves adapting data insights to the unique landscape of games and VR, and partnering with individuals at all levels across creative, accessibility, financial, and live services teams.

Key Responsibilities

Work closely with leadership to understand available data, suggest improvements, and develop approaches for collecting, analyzing, and automating data to inform decision-making across technical and business needs.
Develop and own data pipelines, models, tools, and processes to ensure accurate data collection and analysis.
Develop and own product dashboards that serve as the central point of truth for leadership, product development, and marketing.
Collaborate with stakeholders to provide recommendations for utilizing quantitative and qualitative metrics and key performance indicators to drive technical and business outcomes across business, marketing, financial, and development needs.
Drive discovery of trends in play patterns that help inspire the development of new or improved features and content for games.
Partner with marketing and sales to drive ongoing performance analyses of projects in development, at launch, and beyond.
Support the "Project-Role Framework" methodology with well-designed and comprehensive analyses at all stages of the product lifecycle.
Use industry best practices to document analysis through different formats including basic data visualization such as graphs, tables, and PowerPoint slides to help communicate findings.
Be involved in building automated dashboards and reports to facilitate understanding of business KPIs and player experience.
Stay current with Unity and VR industry trends, development practices, and help ensure distribution of that knowledge throughout the team.
Stay current with global data compliance regulations ensuring data integrity.

Required Qualifications

Experience with data modeling in an applied context.
Experience with large complex data sets using structured and unstructured data.
Developing methodological approach(s) for data collection and analysis to support business decisions.
Ability to perform data mining, pattern recognition & correlation analysis, and insight generation.
Familiarity with statistical and data programming languages such as SQL, PHP, Python, and C++.
Demonstrate critical thinking, provide confidentiality of data, and attention to detail.
Ability to provide clear, concise, accurate communication (both verbal and written) with audiences of varying technical abilities and business contexts.
Ability to research independently, challenge and verify data, and provide recommendations to improve data analysis methodology, with the ability to navigate dependencies and collaborations across projects and stakeholders.
Collaborative and creative-thinking, team-player mentality with the ability to give and receive constructive feedback.
Experience with Google or Microsoft spreadsheets and presentation tools.
University Diploma in Statistics, Math, Engineering, Economics, or another quantitative field or equivalent experience.
5+ years of experience working as a data analyst.
Experience with Looker Studio or similar analytical tools.
2+ years of games industry experience.
Live-operations games experience.

Preferred Qualifications

Knowledge of games, VR, or software development lifecycles.
Technical familiarity with Unity, its analytical capabilities, and backend services.
Ability to design and develop complex SQL queries and reports to retrieve required data, and troubleshoot data discrepancies.
Appreciation of puns (or at least the ability to groan at them).

Additional Information

Salary Range: $105,000 - $130,000
USD/year.
Physical Requirements: Individuals must be able to use virtual reality headsets but are not expected to own one. Must be located and eligible to work in the United States or Canada.
Position Type/Expected Hours of Work: This is a full-time, work from home, 40-hours per week position. Core hours of work are Monday through Friday, 10:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. CT.
Travel: For individuals working outside of the Austin, TX area, travel to Austin may be required 4 to 6 times per year.

Unlimited sick days.
School loan repayment assistance.
New parent benefits.
Health/Dental/Vision Plans.

About the Opportunity

The company provides a fast, free, and flexible way to send bulk payouts to people in over 230 countries and regions. Over 20,000 companies have utilized its services, sending over $1 billion and saving significant time on average. The organization is committed to making work more efficient, flexible, and fulfilling, operating with a fully remote, high-documentation, low-meeting culture. About the role

An experienced data analyst is sought to partner across go-to-market teams, including Sales, RevOps, Marketing, and Customer Success, to connect the entire revenue funnel from acquisition through retention. Apply a range of analytics methods to gain insights across go-to-market teams, such as conversion funnels and attribution.

jobs.title: Data Analyst (Spring, TX)
jobs.description:
It's fun to work in a company where people truly BELIEVE in what they're doing!

We're committed to bringing passion and customer focus to the business.

Position Overview

The Data Analyst serves as a critical liaison between Finance, IT, Operations, Sales, Customer Service, and other business units to ensure data integrity, accuracy, and alignment with organizational objectives. This role is responsible for overseeing data flows between vertical applications and the Core SAP system, managing customer and operational data, supporting reporting initiatives, and driving data governance and process improvement efforts across the organization.

The ideal candidate is analytical, detail-oriented, and highly collaborative, with the ability to manage large datasets, identify data discrepancies, and support strategic business initiatives through effective data management and system integration.

Key Responsibilities

Data Integrity & System Integration

Ensure billables, receivables, and adjustments accurately traverse between vertical applications and the Core SAP system
Support and execute ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) processes for data movement, migration, and system updates
Manage and maintain charge codes and service codes to ensure consistency, compliance, and operational alignment across systems
Perform ongoing data validation, auditing, cleansing, and quality checks to ensure data accuracy and completeness
Identify, troubleshoot, and resolve data inconsistencies, errors, and gaps across platforms

Operational & Customer Data Management

Validate routing information and collaborate with GIS Routing teams on data uploads, geolocation accuracy, and data hygiene initiatives
Review, clean, standardize, and maintain customer and operational data across internal systems
Provide guidance and validation for Sales Contract input to ensure alignment with departmental and organizational objectives
Ensure new customer data aligns with Customer Service standards and operational requirements

Reporting & Business Support

Ensure Finance has access to accurate and reliable data for reporting and analysis
Support ad-hoc reporting requests and collaborate with Data Analysts on reporting visualization and business insights
Maintain documentation for data processes, quality controls, and system workflows

Cross-Functional Collaboration & Data Governance

Coordinate strategic process and system changes impacting operational, financial, and customer data
Work closely with Operations, Accounting, IT, Sales, Customer Service, and other departments to support data-related initiatives
Assess and communicate the impact of process changes on data integrity and business operations
Develop and support long-term goals for data governance, integration, and business planning
Establish and promote best practices for enterprise data management and compliance standards

Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Business, Data Analytics, Information Systems, or a related field, or equivalent experience
Experience with data management, enterprise systems, SAP, and application integration preferred
Strong proficiency in Excel required; experience with SQL, Python, or other data management tools is a plus
Understanding of data structures, data cleansing, ETL processes, and reporting concepts
Familiarity with CRM systems and operational workflows preferred
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with exceptional attention to detail
Excellent communication and collaboration skills with the ability to work effectively across multiple departments
Ability to manage repetitive, detail-driven work while maintaining a high level of accuracy

jobs.description:
Advertisement Closes

7/4/2026 (8:00 PM EDT)

2603135

Environmental Scientist Consultant I

Pay Grade 17

Salary

$6,319.24 - $7,188.68 Monthly

Employment Type

EXECUTIVE BRANCH | FULL TIME | INELIGIBLE FOR OVERTIME PAY | 18A | 37.5 HR/WK

Click here for more details on state employment.

Hiring Agency

Energy & Environment Cabinet | Dept for Environmental Protection

Location

300 Sower Boulevard

Frankfort, KY 40601 USA

Description

The agency may authorize the selected candidate to telecommute. The agency may terminate or modify the telecommuting arrangement at any time.

The mission of the Kentucky Energy & Environment Cabinet (EEC) is to improve the quality of life for all Kentuckians by protecting our land, air, and water resources by utilizing our natural resources in an environmentally conscientious manner. We do this by helping families connect with nature and preserving the Commonwealth’s natural heritage; and by supporting innovative, resilient, and sustainable energy solutions that together bring economic benefit to the commonwealth.

If you are looking for a positive work environment and a career where you can make a difference in the environment we live in, then EEC is the cabinet for you. For more information on what our cabinet has to offer, check out our YouTube page: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCWSuC43XqlHchYeQ66tP7tg and our Careers page: https://eec.ky.gov/Pages/careers.aspx

By joining the Division of Water (DOW) Water Quality Branch as an Environmental Consultant I in Franklin County, you will have the opportunity to play an integral role in supporting DOW’s mission to manage, protect, and restore the quality and quantity of water resources in the Commonwealth.

Holders of the Environmental Scientist I position will complete tasks such as:

Review, develop, and maintain Kentucky's Administrative Regulations on water quality and water pollution control laws, policies and guidance documents. Support water quality standards review and permitting actions and compile technical information required to evaluate and recommend regulatory actions.
Evaluate water quality and biological monitoring data, external scientific research and technical supporting documents, and EPA guidance documents to support the development and maintenance of water quality standards and implementation of the Commonwealth’s antidegradation policy and methodology.
Respond to inquiries from regulated entities, local governments, federal agencies and the public regarding water quality standards and implementation. Lead and participate in stakeholder engagement meetings and inter- and intra-agency coordination efforts involving water quality standards and related water pollution control actions.
Prepare correspondence, briefing materials, presentations and program reports.

Desired candidates possess the following requisite skills or experience applicable to the position:

Understanding of state and federal environmental regulations, particularly those which support the objectives of the Clean Water Act and implementation of water pollution control programs.
Ability to analyze and interpret scientific, technical, and environmental monitoring data.
Strong written communication skills, including the ability to prepare technical reports, regulatory documents, and correspondence.
Ability to communicate technical information effectively to both technical and non-technical audiences.

EEC provides opportunities for development and upward movement. There is room to grow your skill set as you build your career. Come join our team and make an impact in the future of Kentucky. We look forward to reviewing your application.

EEC participates in E-Verify and will provide the federal government with your Form I-9 information to confirm that you are authorized to work in the U.S. EEC will only use E-Verify once you have accepted a job offer and completed the Form I-9. For more information on E-Verify, or if you believe that EEC has violated its E-Verify responsibilities, please contact Department of Homeland Security (DHS) at 888-897-7781 or https://www.e-verify.gov/ .

Minimum Requirements

EDUCATION:
Graduate of a college or university with a bachelor's degree in a natural, biological or physical science OR Graduate of a college or university with a bachelor's degree including or supplemented by 20 semester hours in a natural, biological or physical science.

EXPERIENCE, TRAINING, OR SKILLS:
Eight years of experience in natural resources, environmental control, or a related area.

Substitute EDUCATION for EXPERIENCE:
Graduate study in a natural, biological, or physical science will substitute for the required experience on a year-for-year basis.

Substitute EXPERIENCE for EDUCATION:
Experience in natural resources, environmental control, engineering, or a related area will substitute for the required education on a year-for-year basis.

SPECIAL REQUIREMENTS (AGE, LICENSURE, REGULATION, ETC.):
NONE

Working Conditions

Work is typically performed in an office setting. Incumbents in this class may perform work in the field with frequent travel on a statewide basis.

Probationary Period

This job has an initial and promotional probationary period of 6 months, except as provided in KRS 18A.111.

If you have questions about this advertisement, please contact Nicholas Reif at Nicholas.Reif@ky.gov or 502-782-3284.

jobs.title: Mid-Level Environmental Engineer/Geologist/Scientist
jobs.description:
At Jacobs, we're challenging today to reinvent tomorrow by solving the world's most critical problems for thriving cities, resilient environments, mission-critical outcomes, operational advancement, scientific discovery and cutting-edge manufacturing, turning abstract ideas into realities that transform the world for good.

Your impact

When it comes to the most challenging environmental issues facing our communities today, we’re focused on developing and delivering the most innovative solutions to improve our tomorrow. We’re looking for a Mid-Level Environmental Engineer/Geologist/Scientist to continue their career at Jacobs – where we’ll empower you to learn and grow in the delivery of environmental site characterization and remediation projects for government and global private-sector clients to make our world

better.

Based in the Midwest (Kansas City, MO, Saint Louis, MO, Minneapolis, MN, Chicago, IL, Milwaukee, WI, or Cincinnati, OH), you’ll participate in a variety of field and office tasks related to planning and implementing environmental investigation and remediation projects. You’ll learn from other experienced staff who are ready to mentor you, and you’ll pass your knowledge on to others. You’ll gain valuable experience in soil and groundwater sampling as well as drilling and subcontractor oversight, spending about 50% of your time in the field. You’ll balance your field work with some time spent in the office evaluating and reviewing data, writing reports and preparing project documents, and coordinating logistics for field activities. You’ll be juggling multiple tasks, so we’ll rely on you to prioritize effectively, keeping the big-picture in mind.

Here's What You'll Do

Lead and Manage Field Projects
Collaborate and Coordinate Work with subcontractors, internal support staff, and subject matter experts.
Technical Excellence Analyze data, prepare technical reports, and design and implement pilot studies and remedial
systems.
Mentorship and Growth Provide motivation and direction to project teams, and benefit from mentorship opportunities to
help you grow your career at Jacobs.

Here's what you'll need

Bachelor’s degree in engineering, geology, or other closely related degree
5-10 years of professional experience with delivering environmental site characterization and remediation projects, including fieldwork methodology/procedures and assessment/evaluation of data for various environmental media.
Experience working on project sites and overseeing subcontractors
Experience leading teams toward a common goal.
40-hr HAZWOPER certification or the ability to attain this certification

Ideally, You’ll Also Have

Master’s degree in engineering, geology, or other closely related degree
Professional registration (e.g., Professional Engineer or Professional Geologist) or the intent to obtain and maintain professional certification.
Effective verbal and written communication skills with coworkers, contractors, and clients
Strong attention to detail when conducting field work and evaluating data.
Familiarity of United State Environmental Protection Agency regulations including CERCLA and RCRA

Our health and welfare benefits are designed to invest in you, and in the things you care about. Your health. Your well-being. Your security. Your future. Employees have access to medical, dental, vision, and basic life insurance, a 401(k) plan, and the ability to purchase company stock at a discount. Eligible employees may also enroll in a deferred compensation plan or the Executive Deferral Plan. Jacobs has an unlimited U.S. Personalized Paid Time Off (PPTO) policy for full-time salaried/exempt employees, seven paid holidays, and caregiver leave. And certain roles may be eligible for additional rewards, including merit increases, performance discretionary bonus, and stock.

The base salary range for this position is $75,300.00 to $115,000.00. Within the range, individual pay is determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training.

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability or protected veteran status. Job posted on June 24, 2026. This position will be open for at least 3 days.

At Jacobs, we’re partnering across the globe to create the best project outcomes by maximizing the design, digital technology, and support capabilities of our Global Integrated Delivery (GID) teammates. By joining Jacobs, you’ll commit to supporting and engaging with these teams, as we work to build a company like no other.

We value collaboration and believe that in-person interactions are crucial for both our culture and client delivery. We empower employees with our hybrid working policy, allowing them to split their work week between Jacobs offices/projects and remote locations enabling them to deliver their best work.

Your application experience is important to us, and we’re keen to adapt to make every interaction even better. If you require further support or reasonable adjustments with regards to the recruitment process (for example, you require the application form in a different format), please contact the team via Careers Support.

__________________________________________________________________________________________

Job Description Summary

The search will remain open until the position has been filled.
First consideration will be given to applications received by May 18, 2026. Applications received after May 18, 2026 may be reviewed depending on search progress and needs but are not guaranteed full consideration.

_______________________________________________________________________________________________________

About URI:
The University of Rhode Island enrolls approximately 17,000 students across its graduate and undergraduate programs and is the State’s flagship public research university, as well as the land grant and sea grant university, for the state of Rhode Island. The main campus is located in the historic village of Kingston, and the Bay Campus is located in Narragansett. Both campuses are near major beaches in a beautiful coastal community. URI is just 30 minutes from Providence, RI and within easy reach of Newport, Boston, and New York City.

_______________________________________________________________________________________________________

Position Summary

Work with a multidisciplinary team of psychologists on a five-year NIH-funded project using intensive longitudinal data (i.e., wearable alcohol sensor and ecological momentary assessment surveys) to evaluate proximal associations between acute use of use and suicide risk, as well as to identify underlying psychological mechanisms, following residential alcohol use treatment. Will be responsible for managing, integrating, and analyzing intensive longitudinal data derived from ecological momentary assessment protocols and wearable sensor technologies, including the Skyn device. This role involves preprocessing and quality assurance of high-frequency, multimodal data streams; developing analysis-ready datasets; and applying statistical methods to examine within-person dynamics and between-person differences in behavioral and physiological processes.

Duties and Responsibilities

Key Duties And Responsibilities

Manage, preprocess, and ensure quality control of intensive longitudinal data collected through ecological momentary assessment and wearable sensors, including the Skyn device.

Integrate multimodal data streams (self-report from ecological momentary assessment and physiological data from Skyn) into analysis-ready datasets.

Apply statistical models to examine within-person dynamics and between-person variability (e.g., DSEM, GIMME).

Address challenges inherent to ecological momentary assessment and wearable data, including

missingness, compliance, temporal alignment, and noise.

Collaborate with interdisciplinary research teams to translate scientific questions into appropriate analytic strategies.

Create clear data visualizations, summaries, and reports to support interpretation.

Contribute to study design, analytic planning, and dissemination of findings through manuscripts,

presentations, and reports.

Maintain reproducible research workflows, including documentation, version control, and secure data management in compliance with IRB and data privacy requirements.

Provide direct supervision in data science to staff and students.

Other Duties And Responsibilities

Perform other duties as assigned.

Licenses, Tools, And Equipment

Personal computers, printers; Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Publisher); SPSS;

MPlus; Endnote; relevant laboratory equipment. Must have own transportation.

Required Qualifications

REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS:

Master’s degree in Data Science, Statistics, Biostatistics, Computer Science, Psychology, Biomedical Engineering, or a related quantitative field.
Strong experience working with intensive longitudinal or time-series data.
Proficiency in R and/or Mplus for data wrangling, statistical modeling, and visualization.
Experience processing and analyzing ecological momentary assessment and wearable sensor data, particularly from the Skyn device.
Knowledge of ecological momentary assessment and wearable sensor data methodologies and challenges (e.g., missingness, compliance, temporal alignment).
Demonstrated ability to work independently while collaborating in multidisciplinary research environments.
Demonstrated organizational and prioritization skills.
Demonstrated strong interpersonal and verbal communication skills.
Demonstrated proficiency in written communication skills.
Demonstrated ability to write effectively in scientific formats.
Demonstrated ability to work with diverse groups/populations.

Preferred Qualifications

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS:

PhD. in Data Science, Statistics, Biostatistics, Computer Science, Psychology, Biomedical Engineering, or a related quantitative field.

_______________________________________________________________________________________________________

In furtherance of its mission to serve and support all learners as the state’s public flagship university, URI continues to expand efforts to recruit and retain a broadly representative workforce as well as to regularly evaluate the effectiveness of those efforts. jobs.title: Environmental Scientist (Mid-Level) (3884)
jobs.description:
Navarro Research & Engineering is recruiting an Environmental Scientist (Mid-Level)
(3884) in Oak Ridge, TN.

Navarro is an award-winning provider of turnkey solutions, delivering comprehensive nuclear, environmental and technical services to federal agencies, commercial enterprises and private clients across the United States. Our expertise supports organizations such as the Department of Energy, the National Nuclear Security Administration, the Department of Defense and NASA, while also extending to commercial industries and private sector partners facing complex engineering, technical and environmental challenges. Navarro specializes in nuclear operations, environmental remediation, D&D, waste management, advanced energy, facilities management and technical and professional services. For more information, please visit Navarro-inc.com.
Location: Oak Ridge, TN (Local candidates preferred or willing to relocate)

This position is ideal for a mid-career professional with approximately 10 years of experience in groundwater remediation, soil remediation and environmental compliance who can contribute both technically and strategically to project execution and proposal development.

Position Summary
The Environmental Scientist will support environmental remediation and compliance activities while playing a key role in developing technical content for proposals. The successful candidate will bring strong regulatory knowledge, hands-on field or analytical experience, and the ability to clearly communicate complex environmental concepts in written deliverables.

Key Responsibilities
Support environmental remediation and/or environmental compliance projects across federal and commercial programs
Contribute to and develop technical proposal content in collaboration with Business Development teams
Interpret and apply environmental regulations, including federal and state requirements, to ongoing and proposed work
Prepare technical reports, evaluations, and supporting documentation for environmental projects
Support groundwater remediation activities, including site characterization, corrective actions, and long-term monitoring
Perform or support groundwater modeling and data analysis (preferred)
Collaborate with engineers, scientists, project managers, and regulatory stakeholders to ensure technical accuracy and compliance
Assist with project planning, data interpretation, and decision-making processes
Ensure work meets quality standards, regulatory requirements, and client expectations

Required Qualifications
Bachelor of Science in Environmental Science, Geology, or a related scientific discipline
Approximately 8-12 years of relevant experience (mid-level experience)
Deomonstrated experieince in groundwater remediation, soil remediation and environmental compliance
Experience supporting federal programs (DOE, DOD, or similar) preferred
Strong technical writing skills, including experience contributing to reports or proposals
Knowledge of applicable environmental regulations and compliance frameworks

Preferred Qualifications
Familiarity with site characterization, risk assessment, and corrective action strategies
Experience supporting proposal development or technical business development efforts
Advanced degree in environmental science or related field
Professional certifications (e.g., PG, CHMM, QEP)

Additional Requirements
Must be able to work onsite in Oak Ridge, TN (relocation assistance may be considered)
Due to the nature of government contract requirements, U.S. citizenship is required

Due to the nature of the government contract requirements and/or clearances requirements, US citizenship is required.

jobs.title: Field Technician-Water Solutions
jobs.description:
Primary Purpose

JOB DESCRIPTION

The Field Technician-Water Solutions is expected to perform a variety of routine and complex tasks including fluids unloading, equipment maintenance and all assigned work at and on company property, while providing outstanding customer service.

Responsibilities

Essential Duties & Responsibilities:

Off-load a high volume of water trucks daily.
Develop understanding of the operation of all equipment on assigned routes or facilities.
Measure and record truck, and pipeline volumes. Ensure accuracy of data received and entered.
Maintain daily contact with co-workers and clients to maintain close working relationships and to ensure the reliability of Tallgrass infrastructure is always intact.
Monitor contract compliance to ensure that facilities are performing satisfactorily, and that contract and regulatory requirements are adhered to.
Monitor day-to-day operations, safety, environmental compliance, and quality as directed by Tallgrass Management. Responsible for various daily reporting requirements.
Reporting volumes and key facility operating parameters at shift change and to all related personnel.
Identify production related issues and learn appropriate solutions under supervision while consulting with the appropriate Tallgrass personnel.
Follow preventative maintenance plan and all system integrity testing.
Execute routine repairs and maintenance on all equipment. Communicate corrective maintenance needs if unable to complete on own.
Deal with a wide variety of people with tact, courtesy, and professionalism.
Speak clearly, both in person and by telephone.
Maintain a regular attendance and consistently perform at a high level.
Will work non-traditional hours as needed.
Will require supporting multiple locations within multiple counties in North Dakota.
Maintain a high regard for personal safety, for the safety of company assets and employees, and the public.
Perform any other tasks assigned to support overall company operations.
Other duties as assigned.

Education

QUALIFICATIONS

High school diploma / GED or equivalent experience is required.
Work experience can be used in lieu of education

Experience/Specific Knowledge

Should have at least six (6) months of experience in Water Related Oilfield Services.
Should have understanding and experience with water related projects — sourcing, pipelines, treatment, and disposal.
Intermediate proficiency level in MS Office applications.

Certifications, Licenses & Registrations

Must possess and maintain a valid driver’s license and a driving record satisfactory to the company and its insurers.
If required to operate a company vehicle or equipment the Technician must drive safely to and from wells and well sites, maintain a clean driving record and adhere to Company policies

Competencies, Skills & Abilities

Promote continuous safety culture.
Knowledge and understanding of industry standards and best practices.
Apply Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) for routine tasks.
Willingness to learn company specific applications.
Written and verbal communication skills.
Must be able to take direction from management, adhere to required work schedules, focus attention on details, and follow work rules.
Actively participate in teams and collaborate for improved performance.
Must possess the ability to adapt to a changing and fast paced work environment.
Ability to organize and prioritize daily work.
Ability to work outside in all weather conditions.

Physical Demands

All the physical requirements listed below are those that may be necessary for an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

Sitting; standing; walking or moving throughout facility; driving; talking; seeing (specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision, distance vision, color vision, peripheral vision, depth perception and ability to adjust focus); hearing; feeling; bending or stooping; squatting or crouching; reaching; kneeling; pushing; pulling; lifting to 50 lbs.

Working Conditions

Varying working conditions from office settings to working outdoors in inclement weather conditions.
Working with and around industrial hazards.
Required to carry a cell phone and be available to respond during working and non-working hours.
This is an on-call position.
Hitch- 7 days on, 7 days off, 12-hour shifts and will receive overtime.
The selected candidate must successfully pass a drug screening, background check, and fit-for-duty examination, including credit reports for specific positions—after receiving a job offer and before commencing employment

Supervisory Responsibility

None

Preferred Education, Experience, Certifications, Competencies, Skills, & Abilities

Above the minimum requirements; not required but advantageous in this position:

Working understanding and knowledge of SWD (saltwater disposal) and associated equipment.
1+ years of SWD operating experience.

Other Responsibilities

Please note this job description is not designed to encompass or contain a comprehensive listing of activities, duties or responsibilities that are required of the employee for this job. Duties, responsibilities, and activities may change at any time with or without notice.

About Us

Tallgrass was named one of the Top Workplaces USA and highlighted in Colorado's Top Workplaces for the past seven consecutive years. Tallgrass is a leading energy infrastructure company focused on safely, reliably, and sustainably delivering the energy and services that power our nation and enable our quality of life.

At Tallgrass, we value our teams and strive to create an environment where employees feel respected, and their contributions are valued. We aim to support employees' physical, mental, and financial well-being through a comprehensive Total Rewards Program.

Industry competitive pay
Health insurance package options that include Flexible Spending & Health Savings Accounts
Infertility Coverage
Parental Leave
401(k) with up to a 6% match that vests immediately plus an employer discretionary contribution of up to 4%
Wellness Programs and Mental Health Resources
Employer-paid life insurance, short-term disability, and long-term disability coverage
Critical Illness & Accident Insurance
Vacation, sick days, paid caregiver leave, volunteer and bereavement paid time off
Identity theft protection
Annual discretionary bonus
Generous Tuition Reimbursement Program

Company vehicle (if applicable)
Employee discounts; vehicles, tires, cellular plans, and more
Networking and employee engagement events
Personal development to grow your career with us based on your strengths and interests

Application Deadline:
Recruiting timelines vary by position; however, all Tallgrass positions accept applications for at least five business days from the posting date. This position is open and still accepting applications.

Compensation
: Compensation ranges are provided in good faith based on what we anticipate when researching wages for this position at the state and national levels. We may ultimately pay more or less than the posted range. This salary range may also be modified in the future.

Notice to External Search Firms
: Tallgrass does not accept unsolicited resumes from search firms or employment agencies. Unsolicited referrals and resumes are considered Tallgrass property; therefore, Tallgrass will not pay a fee for any placement resulting from the receipt of an unsolicited referral. Approved vendors may be invited to refer talent for specific positions at Tallgrass's request only. A fully executed agreement with Tallgrass must be in place and current in these cases.
