jobs.title: College & Career Success Advising and Program Coordinator, Bachelor's Pathway
jobs.description:
About Breakthrough Central Texas

Breakthrough is the leading college access and success nonprofit in Central Texas. For more than 20 years, we have partnered with thousands of middle and high school students who aspire to be the first in their family to graduate with a degree or certificate. We make a radical, long-term commitment to students, to and through college, unlocking the power of education and a lifetime of opportunity. We support the whole student with personalized services, work closely with community partners, campuses, and employers, and advocate for our students with lawmakers. Breakthrough students attend 60+ public schools and persist at nearly 100 colleges and universities. The result is that 55% of Breakthrough students graduate from college, compared to 17% of low-income students in our region. Our work has resulted in more than 550 college graduates, with 4,000 on their way, positively impacting families for generations, our region’s educational access, and the Central Texas economy.

Why Work at Breakthrough Central Texas

By working for Breakthrough Central Texas, you will help hundreds of students achieve their dreams of success and receive the high-quality education they deserve. Breakthrough is an organization committed to its students and recognizes that our greatest resource to support them is our dedicated and talented staff. If you believe all young people deserve the opportunity to achieve postsecondary success and you desire to work at an organization committed to its employees and building the strongest team culture anywhere, then join us!

About The College & Career Success Program Coordinator Role

Breakthrough recognizes the value and need of high-quality advising services to students beyond high school graduation, with an emphasis on supporting students along bachelor’s degree completion pathways. In this model, we directly serve students through six years of their postsecondary journey, with a strong focus on college transition, early persistence, and continued progress toward degree completion and/or successful transfer. The overall goal of the program is to increase bachelor’s attainment rates and strengthen long-term postsecondary success among Breakthrough students.

Role

As a primary provider of direct services to students and their families, the CCSC is committed to the academic, career, and personal development of students as they navigate bachelor’s degree pathways, including community college-to-university transfer and direct enrollment at four-year institutions. This role carries a direct caseload of up to 75 students (primarily enrolled in two-year and four-year colleges and universities), as well as direct supervision of an AmeriCorps member with a similar caseload. This role reports to the Director of the College & Career Success Team. To achieve the above, Breakthrough’s College & Career Success Program Coordinator will:

Postsecondary Advising

Manage a caseload of up to 75 students, primarily enrolled in two-year and four-year colleges and universities, supporting their persistence, transfer (where applicable), and progress toward bachelor’s degree completion
Develop meaningful relationships with Breakthrough college students and recent high school graduates and their families via phone, text, social media, email, and individual/group meetings
Meet or exceed standards of service for student contact based on tiered support levels and accurately document all interactions in the Salesforce databaseTravel to campuses around Central Texas and across the state (caseload dependent) to meet face-to-face with students and campus partners

Stewarding Young Adults

Assist college students and recent graduates with enrollment, re-enrollment, transfer pathways, financial aid processes, and academic planning aligned to degree completion and career goals
Support students navigating institutional transitions, including transfer from community college to four-year universities and changes in major or career pathway

Resource Connection

Connect students to wraparound services that support persistence and degree completion, including emergency aid, mental health counseling referrals, and other student support resources
Experience working in a public or private university academic advising or financial aid office a plus

Data Tracking and Evaluation

Collect and analyze college persistence, transfer, and completion data to identify trends, barriers, and student support needs
Design and provide timely reports regarding program outputs, outcomes, and student progression toward bachelor’s attainment

Relationship Building

Foster a healthy, collaborative culture within the College & Career Success team and across the organization
Participate in committees and workgroups to support team planning, organizational change efforts, and cross-functional initiatives
Perform other job-related duties as assigned

Program Delivery and Design

Design, coordinate, and facilitate programs and events that support postsecondary success
Drive student success in alignment with Breakthrough’s mission of supporting students on their path to becoming first-generation college graduates and bachelor’s degree holders

About You

Breakthrough Central Texas seeks a talented, collaborative, and equity-minded professional to support the day-to-day leadership and implementation of college completion advising and programming. The ideal candidate demonstrates operational leadership, strong relationship-building skills, and a commitment to continuous improvement in support of postsecondary persistence and student success. Because of the size of the organization and the fast-paced nature of the work, the ideal candidate will also demonstrate strength in the following competencies:

CCST – Postsecondary Advising: Skillfully supports young adults in navigating the transition to and through postsecondary education by providing individualized guidance on institution and degree program fit, financial aid, registration and enrollment processes, degree progress, career exploration, and re-enrollment pathways. Coordinates support systems and campus resources to help students persist, address barriers, and maintain continuous enrollment, including applying the CASVE model when relevant.
CCST – Stewarding Young Adults: Effectively and compassionately guides, coaches, and advocates for young adults as they transition into adulthood and increase independence. Builds trusting relationships that foster self-efficacy, accountability, decision-making, and resilience while supporting students in navigating personal, academic, financial, and life transitions.
Data Tracking and Evaluation: Submits timely, accurate, and comprehensive case notes and program data while tracking and analyzing trends to inform student support strategies, improve program delivery, and ensure progress toward advising, persistence, and team goals. Uses data to prioritize action, strengthen operational effectiveness, and support continuous improvement efforts.
Resource Connection: Works collaboratively with Breakthrough’s Student and Family Support Specialist and external partners to connect students and families to non-academic resources, including mental health, food, housing, transportation, and other support services. Ensures referrals and follow-up processes are coordinated effectively to support student well-being and persistence.
Family Navigation and Caregiver Engagement: Builds trusting relationships with caregivers and families by providing accessible communication, guidance, and resources that support student engagement and success. Partners with families consistently to navigate academic, postsecondary, and institutional processes while strengthening family connection to programming and services.
Program Design and Delivery: Uses appropriate content knowledge and operational planning skills to design, coordinate, and facilitate programs and events that support postsecondary success. Delivers engaging experiences for groups of varying sizes while ensuring programming aligns with intended outcomes, organizational priorities, and student developmental needs.
Youth Development: Meets young people where they are and responds in developmentally appropriate ways that support social-emotional growth, self-efficacy, and academic identity development. Creates supportive environments that foster belonging, accountability, and student engagement across individual and group settings.
Campus, Institutional, and Systemic Navigation and Partner Cultivation: Works effectively within schools, postsecondary institutions, and community systems to support student success. Builds and maintains productive working relationships with campus staff, counselors, administrators, and community partners to improve coordination, remove barriers, and strengthen program implementation while stewarding Breakthrough partnerships.
Talent Development: Includes coaching, providing effective and timely feedback, and delivering structured training and support to a direct AmeriCorps member. The Program Coordinator actively supports the AmeriCorps member in setting priorities, building skills, meeting performance and program goals, and growing toward their professional and developmental aspirations.

Compensation & Benefits

Competitive salary between $56,500-$74,500 depending on experience and qualifications
Health, dental and vision insurance; Breakthrough pays 100% of employee only premiums
Long-term disability and life insurance
Matching 401K retirement plan
Thirty days of annualized PTO
Generous leave policy

At Breakthrough, we want to support growth within our team. For this role, internal candidates will be reviewed first.

Please note that the selected candidate for this role will begin employment on August 24, 2026.

Breakthrough Central Texas is committed to a policy of equal treatment and opportunity in every aspect of its relations with its applicants, staff members, and families, without regard to race, color, ethnicity, religion, gender identity and gender expression, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, age, marital status, military status, pregnancy, or parenthood. People of diverse backgrounds are strongly encouraged to apply.

jobs.title: Jr. HR Data Analyst, Human Resources
jobs.description:
Job Description

Who We Are

Creative Artists Agency (CAA) is the leading entertainment and sports agency, with global expertise in filmed and live entertainment, digital media, publishing, sponsorship sales and endorsements, media finance, consumer investing, fashion, trademark licensing, and philanthropy.  Distinguished by its culture of collaboration and exceptional client service, CAA’s diverse workforce identifies, innovates, and amplifies opportunities for the people and organizations that shape culture and inspire the world.  The trailblazer of the agency business, CAA was the first to build a sports business, create an investment bank, launch a venture fund, found technology start-up companies, establish a philanthropic arm, build a business in China, and form a brand marketing services division, among other innovations. Named Most Valuable Sports Agency by Forbes for eight consecutive years, CAA represents more than 2,000 of the world’s top athletes in football, baseball, basketball, hockey, soccer, in addition to coaches, on-air broadcasters, and sports personalities and works in the areas of broadcast rights, corporate marketing initiatives, social impact, and sports properties for sales and sponsorship opportunities. Founded in 1975, CAA is headquartered in Los Angeles, and has offices in New York, Nashville, Memphis, Chicago, Miami, London, Munich, Geneva, Stockholm, Shanghai, and Beijing, among other locations globally.

The Role

CAA is seeking a detail-oriented Junior Human Resources Data Analyst to join its Corporate Human Resources People Analytics team. You will be responsible for accurately entering and maintaining employee data in our HR systems and researching data integrity issues. This role is ideal for an early-career professional with 2+ years of experience who is passionate about HR metrics, workforce analytics, and turning data into actionable insights. The role focuses on maintaining HR data integrity, generating reports, and supporting strategic HR initiatives through analysis.

Responsibilities
Collaborate with HR stakeholders to collect required information for employee transactions (position changes, promotions, transfers, etc.) and ensure accurate and timely data entry into Workday.
Manage and optimize data within the Workday HCM system, including running queries, troubleshooting data issues, and maintaining data integrity.
Ensure data integrity and consistency across all HR systems.
Support ad-hoc data requests from HR leadership
Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and compliance with data privacy regulations

Qualifications
2+ years of experience in HR data analysis, people analytics, HRIS support, or a similar role.
Strong working knowledge data entry processes
General knowledge of Human Resources principles and best practices
Strong analytical mindset with excellent attention to detail, problem-solving skills, and organizational skills

Location

This role is hybrid, based out of the Los Angeles office.

Compensation

The base hourly rate for this position is in the range of $29.00 - $35.00. This position also is eligible for benefits and discretionary bonus. Ultimately, wages may vary based upon, but not limited to, relevant experience, time in role, business sector, and geographic location, among other criteria. jobs.title: Associate Geologist
jobs.description:
Job Description

Our Business

We are a global leader in environmental consulting with world-class expertise in environmental, social and governance (ESG), climate resiliency & sustainability (CRS), and earth sciences. Combined we have over 20,000 multidisciplinary professionals worldwide collaborating daily to protect and conserve natural landscapes, create firm foundations for communities to stand on, and champion sustainable development to combat climate change.

As part of the team, you will have access to an unparalleled network of resources and endless opportunities to collaborate on exciting projects with diverse teammates from across the globe.

This Opportunity

WSP is seeking an Associate Geologist
with 1 to 3 years consulting experience to join our Pacific Northwest Site Investigation & Remediation Team in our Redmond, WA
office. We offer flexible working arrangements to help with work/life balance.

WSP’s Pacific Northwest Site Investigation & Remediation Team offers environmental remediation and related services to a diverse set of clients, including industrial/commercial companies, local and state agencies, and the federal government. Project locations are local, regional, and nationwide. We have in-house experts in chemistry, risk assessment, data management, graphics/GIS, engineering, and industrial hygiene who support projects in the Pacific Northwest and many other locations, and have access to subject matter experts worldwide. The position requires a self-motivated candidate able to work as part of local, regional, or national project teams and eager to build consulting experience through a combination of field work, data evaluation, technical reporting, and project support.

Your Impact

Help solve problems and document solutions, express ideas through strong technical writing and verbal communication, and support technical reports, plans, and calculations in a consulting environment.

Work on a variety of multi-disciplinary projects, with substantial emphasis on soil, soil vapor/gas, groundwater, surface water, and stormwater investigations and remediation systems; chemical fate and transport assessment; hazardous waste management; permitting; and site planning.

Be a member of teams providing environmental consulting services to commercial and government clients.

Perform technical tasks under oversight of more experienced technical reviewers/directors as appropriate.

Possess knowledge and technical skills in Federal, Oregon, and Washington regulations.

This position provides the opportunity for career growth over time, and the ideal candidate will be excited about the opportunity to help grow our environmental consulting business.

Exercise responsible and ethical decision-making regarding company funds, resources and conduct, and adhere to WSP’s Code of Conduct and related policies and procedures.

Perform Additional Responsibilities As Required By Business Needs.

Who You Are

Required Qualifications

B.S. or higher in Geology, Hydrogeology, or related discipline

Minimum 1 Or More Years Post-education Environmental Consulting Experience

Experience supporting environmental sampling, drilling oversight, site assessment, remediation, and/or compliance projects

Experience preparing tables, figures, boring logs, and technical summaries for reports.

Excellent verbal and written communication skills

Consistent and highly accurate project work with a strong attention to detail

Applies sound judgment and ability to manages multiple tasks within project deadlines

Ability to work efficiently and demonstrate effective organizational skills

Comfortable working independently in the field and within both small and large project teams to achieve objectives

Ability to work in industrial and field environments as well as in the office

Flexible to work outside of business hours, when necessary

Proficiency with MS Office: Excel, Word, and Outlook, in particular

Proven track record of upholding workplace safety and ability to abide by WSP’s health, safety and drug/alcohol and harassment policies.

Ability to work schedules conducive to project-specific requirements that may extend beyond the typical workweek.

Ability to travel based on project-specific requirements

Preferred Qualifications

Exposure to Oregon and/or Washington environmental regulatory programs.

Geologist-in-Training Professional registration in Washington if a Geologist/Hydrogeologist is preferred

Master’s Degree is preferred

40-Hour OSHA Health & Safety Training (HAZWOPER) (29 CFR 1910.120) preferred

Basic First Aid and Adult CPR training desired

WSP Benefits

WSP provides a comprehensive suite of benefits focused on providing health and financial stability throughout the employee’s career. These benefits include coverage related to medical, dental, vision, disability, and life; retirement savings; paid sick leave; paid vacation (or other personal time); paid parental leave; and paid time off for purposes of bereavement, voting, and/or attendance at naturalization proceedings.

Compensation

Expected Salary (all locations): $80,169 - $94,000

WSP USA is providing the compensation range that the company in good faith believes it might pay and offer for this position, based on the successful applicant’s education, experience, knowledge, skills, abilities in addition to internal equity and specific geographic location. WSP USA reserves the right to ultimately pay more or less than the posted range and offer additional benefits and other compensation, depending on circumstances not related to an applicant’s sex or other status protected by local, state, and/or federal law.

About WSP

ABOUT US

WSP USA is the U.S. operating company of WSP, one of the world's leading engineering and professional services firms. Dedicated to serving local communities, we are engineers, planners, technical experts, strategic advisors and construction management professionals. WSP USA designs lasting solutions in the buildings, transportation, energy, water and environment markets. With more than 15,000 employees in over 300 offices across the U.S., we partner with our clients to help communities prosper.

www.wsp.com

WSP provides a flexible and agile workplace model while meeting client needs. Employees are also afforded a comprehensive suite of benefits including medical, dental, vision, disability, life, and retirement savings focused on providing health and financial stability throughout the employee’s career.

At WSP, we want to give our employees the challenges they seek to grow their careers and knowledge base. Your daily contributions to your team will be essential in meeting client objectives, goals and challenges. Are you ready to get started?

WSP USA (and all of its U.S. companies) is an Equal Opportunity Employer Race/Age/Color/Religion/Sex/Sexual Orientation/Gender Identity/National Origin/Disability or Protected Veteran Status.

The selected candidate must be authorized to work in the United States.

Notice To Third Party Agencies

WSP does not accept unsolicited resumes from recruiters, employment agencies, or other staffing services. Unsolicited resumes include any resume or hiring document sent to WSP in the absence of a signed Service Agreement where WSP has expressly requested recruitment/staffing services specific to the position at hand. Any unsolicited resumes, including those submitted to hiring managers or other business leaders, will become the property of WSP and WSP will have the right to hire that candidate without reservation – no fee or other compensation will be owed or paid to the recruiter, employment agency, or other staffing service.

jobs.title: Operations Technology and Data Analyst
jobs.description:
Operations Technology and Data Analyst – Insights, ESG & Operations

Position Summary

A high-caliber Operations Technology & Data Manager will oversee the technical infrastructure and data strategy for our premier food service program at a Key Account Client in the US. They will be responsible for leveraging data to optimize the food program while vetting and implementing cutting-edge food-tech platforms. This role is critical in transforming operational, ESG, and business data into clear, actionable insights. This position is highly cross‐functional, partnering with operations, ESG, finance, culinary, and marketing teams to ensure that data is presented in digestible, and compelling formats while upholding rigorous compliance and reporting standards.

Key Responsibilities

Reporting, Analysis & Data Visualization

Develop and maintain dashboards to track key performance indicators, recurring reports, and ad‐hoc analyses that simplify complex data for diverse audiences.
Develop visualizations that clearly communicate trends in operations, customer experience, ESG metrics, and business performance.
Ensure data accuracy by validating, cleaning, and reconciling datasets as part of monthly and quarterly reporting cycles.
Translate complex data sets into actionable insights for the executive leadership team and the client.

AI‐Enabled Insights & Automation

Use AI‐assisted reporting and summarization tools to streamline workflows and enhance data clarity.
Apply basic machine‐learning–assisted techniques (e.g., trend detection, sentiment analysis) under the guidance of technical partners.
Identify opportunities to automate manual reporting tasks to improve efficiency and consistency.

IT Platform Management & Vetting

Lead the end-to-end lifecycle (vetting, testing, and deployment) of food service technologies for system implementation.
Act as the primary point of contact for third-party software vendors, ensuring SLAs are met and platforms are optimized for the client environment.
Serve as the first line of support for high-level technical issues within the tech stack.

Liaison & IT Compliance

Cross-Functional Collaboration by acting as the technical translator between our Corporate IT department and Client’s Internal IT/Security teams.
Ensure all software and hardware deployments comply with our internal and client’s strict data privacy, and networking security protocols.
Manage the integration of food service hardware within the corporate networks.

Preferred Qualifications

Education:
Bachelor’s degree in Information Systems, Business Analytics, Data Science, IT project management, or a related field.

Experience: 5+ years of experience in technical project management or operations analytics. Experience within the food service, facilities management, or hospitality industry is highly preferred.
Tech Stack: Proficiency in SQL, Tableau/PowerBI, Looker, Google Data Studio, and advanced Excel. Experience with food-tech platforms is a plus.

Core Competencies:
This role is ideal for a detail‐oriented, creative problem-solver who enjoys building reports, visualizations, and analysis that help leaders make data‐driven decisions.

Analytical Mindset: Ability to identify trends in large data sets and turn them into operational improvements. Experience with data cleaning, feature identification, and building intuitive visual dashboards.
Technical Literacy: Comfortable discussing API integrations, network security, and cloud architecture with technical engineers. Ability to work with AI‐based tools to enhance reporting outputs.
Communication: Exceptional ability to communicate technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders. Strong visual storytelling skills.
Adaptability: Thrives in a fast-paced, "fail-fast" environment where innovation is encouraged. Ability to draw meaningful conclusions from complex datasets.
Mindset & Interpersonal Qualities: High attention to detail with a commitment to accuracy and compliance. Curiosity, creativity, and a desire to continuously learn new tools and methods.

Physical Demands & Work Environment

Sitting at a computer for long periods of time, at least 90% of the time
Must be able to lift a minimum of 10 pounds
Must be able to work under pressure and time deadlines during peak periods

HSEQ Compliance:
All employees must adhere to Health, Safety, Environment, and Quality (HSEQ) policies and procedures to ensure a safe and compliant work environment. This includes following safety protocols, maintaining environmental responsibility, and upholding quality standards in all tasks. Employees are expected to actively participate in HSEQ training and report any potential hazards or compliance concerns.

ISS offers full-time employees a variety of benefits, including medical, dental, life, and disability insurance, as well as a comprehensive leave program based on employment status. The annual salary range for this position is $105000.00 per year - $127200.00 per year. Final compensation will be determined based on experience and skills and may vary from the above range.

To Executive Search Firms and Staffing Agencies: ISS Facility Services / Guckenheimer does not accept unsolicited resumes from agencies. All unsolicited resumes will be considered ISS Facility Services / Guckenheimer property, and ISS Facility Services / Guckenheimer will not be obligated to pay a referral fee. This includes resumes submitted directly to Hiring Managers without contacting the ISS Facility Services / Guckenheimer Human Resources Talent Acquisition Department.

As a global organization, ISS Group is committed to making the international community more resilient and just for all people. We encourage diversity and inclusion in their broadest terms, including ethnicity, race, age, gender, gender identity, disability, sexual orientation, religious beliefs, language, culture, and educational background. We look to lead our industry by example and to influence the market positively wherever we operate.

jobs.title: Staff Geologist / Scientist II
jobs.description:
Position Overview

This position will assist the project team and Project Manager in providing field support, and technical assistance on environmental investigation and remediation projects.

Position Requirements

Bachelor’s degree in relevant technical field.
Minimum of 1 year of environmental consulting experience.
Proficient in performing various routine field activities including but not limited to applying proper sampling techniques for all environmental media, overseeing and directing subcontractors, and managing and directing junior field staff.
Experience overseeing various drilling techniques (including direct push, hollow stem, sonic, etc.) and collecting soil, groundwater, and vapor samples.
Demonstrated ability to work independently in the field.
Preliminary data evaluation and analytical skills.
Ability to assist in the preparation of the major components of routine deliverables (i.e., tables, figures, appendices, etc.).
Ability and willingness to support the technical growth of Staff I level professionals based on direct hands-on interactions.
Ability to manage tasks or projects to develop project management skills.

Essential Duties

Field Project Activities:
Coordinate and implement field project work; oversee drilling, monitoring, sampling of typical environmental media, treatment system operations, and subcontractors.

Data Analysis and Report Preparation:
Prepare technical reports by coordinating figures, data tables, site descriptions, and other necessary details to ensure report completeness.
Analyze field data and project work to ensure effective project completion.

Physical Demands/Work Environment Conditions

This position works predominantly in the field working independently or as part of a larger team at exterior project sites (i.e., industrial/brownfield facilities, monitoring wells, treatment systems, construction projects) to perform sample collection, recording of operational/meter parameters, and oversight of subcontractors (e.g., drillers, heavy equipment operators, truckers). These field activities require the ability to drive to different project sites and physically move around outdoors in various terrains, in adverse weather conditions and occasionally for extended or irregular hours. Conducting work involves the use of hand-held monitoring instruments and tools, as well as water sampling equipment, which requires the ability to routinely lift and carry objects, stand, walk, reach, bend, squat, kneel and twist. Office work is primarily stationary/sedentary using a computer and keyboard/mouse requiring repetitive hand movements.

SSES - Field Technician
jobs.description:
OVERVIEW: McKim & Creed is seeking an enthusiastic Sewer System Evaluation Survey (SSES) Field Technician in support of Civil Engineering Water/Wastewater design projects as part of a multi-discipline project team to produce design/construction drawings and specifications. In this role, you will be responsible for conducting data collection activities associated with the completion of a typical Sewer System Evaluation Survey (SSES) related project. Individual will be responsible for gathering and recording data in an efficient and timely manner. Individual must ensure that all data is recorded accurately.

During Your Work-day, You Will

Performing field inspections and investigations by collecting high quality and accurate data.
Manhole Inspections - record various structure attributes and identify defects.
Smoke Testing - conduct smoke tests and record defects encountered.
Night Flow Isolations - measure wastewater flows during non-peak usage.
Dye Testing - conduct dye water tracing and dye water flooding.
Flow Monitoring - install & maintain open channel flow metering equipment.
CCTV Inspections – conduct robotic video inspections of wastewater sewer pipelines in accordance to NASSCO PACP, MACP, and LACP standards.
Frequent overnight travel is required.
This position requires a lot of walking outside and climbing ladders year-round through various terrain and in extreme heat and cold.
Ability to work in Confined Spaces, including sanitary sewer manholes.

What You’ll Need

High School diploma or GED is required.
Must be able to travel.
Willing to work in and around open sanitary sewer lines.
Have the ability to obtain and maintain a Confined Space Entry certification.
Should possess basic computer skills.
Good technical skills.
Valid drivers license.

What Willl Make You Stand Out

Experience with ESRI ArcGIS and/or Trimble Products is preferred.
Previous SSES experience preferred but not required

jobs.title: Data Analyst (Global Trade)
jobs.description:
Role Summary

As a Manager within Global Trade Advisory, you will lead analytics-driven engagements that support import/export compliance and improve global supply chain performance. You will translate complex trade and operational questions into actionable outputs such as dashboards, automated workflows, exception reporting, and audit-ready deliverables.

This role is ideal for professionals who are analytics-first-strong in data wrangling, visualization, and solution delivery-with working knowledge of international trade concepts and regulatory environments.

Key Responsibilities

Client Delivery & Analytics Leadership

Lead trade analytics initiatives that help organizations improve compliance, reduce risk, and optimize operational performance.
Translate trade and supply chain challenges into actionable analytics, process improvements, and technology-enabled solutions.
Design and deliver dashboards, automated reporting, exception monitoring tools, and audit-ready outputs.
Guide teams through data discovery, cleansing, transformation, and analysis across trade, ERP, and logistics systems.

Global Trade Strategy & Advisory

Advise multinational organizations on import/export compliance and global trade strategies using data-driven insights.
Support the assessment and implementation of trade analytics solutions and technology platforms.
Contribute to trade technology strategy efforts, including: Current-state assessments
Requirements gathering
Solution evaluation
Roadmap development
Implementation planning

Technology & Data Integration

Lead integration of trade data across ERP, Global Trade Management (GTM), and logistics platforms.
Partner with IT and engineering teams on cloud and data architecture initiatives, including: Data ingestion and transformation
Data modeling and architecture
Governance, security, and controls
Deployment readiness

Program & Delivery Oversight

Oversee quality of deliverables, including data mapping, testing, validation, and reporting standards.
Ensure alignment across stakeholders and drive adoption of analytics solutions.
Lead workstreams focused on analytics, reporting, and technology enablement.

Team Leadership & Development

Supervise and mentor consultants and senior consultants.
Provide coaching, performance feedback, and career development support.
Contribute to team development, knowledge sharing, and practice growth initiatives.

Practice Support

Assist leadership with practice operations, business development support, and internal initiatives.
Maintain knowledge of emerging technologies and trends in global trade, analytics, and compliance.

Qualifications

6+ years (Manager) or 10+ years (Senior Manager) of relevant experience
Background in global trade, supply chain, or international business operations
Strong analytics and data expertise (data wrangling, visualization, reporting tools)
Experience working with ERP, GTM, or logistics systems
Knowledge of import/export regulations and trade compliance concepts
Experience leading cross-functional teams and client-facing engagements

jobs.title: Data Analyst
jobs.description:
About The Company

FSA Federal (FSA) is dedicated to delivering exceptional services in support of law enforcement and homeland security initiatives. With a focus on integrity, professionalism, and efficiency, FSA Federal provides vital support to various government agencies, ensuring the effective management of financial and legal processes. Our organization prides itself on fostering a collaborative and innovative work environment where dedicated professionals can thrive and contribute to national security efforts.

About The Role

We are currently seeking a detail-oriented and analytical Data Analyst to join our team at FSA Federal. In this role, you will play a crucial part in supporting the United States Attorney's Office (USAO) Financial Litigation Unit (FLU). Your primary responsibilities will include managing debt data, generating reports, and assisting in the collection of debts related to criminal and civil cases. This position offers a unique opportunity to contribute to meaningful work that directly supports law enforcement and justice initiatives. The ideal candidate will possess strong analytical skills, attention to detail, and the ability to work collaboratively within a dynamic team environment.

Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Data Analysis, or a related field.
Proven experience working with data management and reporting tools.
Strong proficiency in database management systems such as CDCS and Pega.
Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills.
Ability to generate detailed reports and interpret complex data sets.
Experience supporting legal or governmental financial operations is preferred.
Exceptional communication skills, both written and verbal.
Ability to work independently and as part of a team in a fast-paced environment.

Responsibilities

Establish new debts in CDCS/Pega, the FLU collection database, ensuring accurate and timely entry of data.
Close expired or fully paid debts to maintain an up-to-date and accurate database.
Generate routine reports through Analytics, the FLU reporting database, including Weekly and Monthly Collection Reports, Statistical Reports, Tickle/Task Reports, and Indirect Payment Reports.
Create routine documents in CDCS/Pega such as Demand Letters, Letters requesting Financial Statements, Proposed Re-Payment Agreements, Default Letters, Employment Verification Letters, and correspondence with the Department of Labor regarding liens and judgments.
Assist and support FLU Agents, Paralegal Specialists, and Supervisory Paralegal Specialists in debt collection activities, including reconciling payment histories, posting missing payments, and processing indirect payments.
Support the preparation of legal documents, motions, and pleadings related to debt collection cases.
Collaborate with team members to ensure compliance with legal and procedural standards.
Maintain accurate records and documentation to support ongoing and future debt collection efforts.

Benefits

FSA Federal offers a comprehensive benefits package designed to support the well-being and professional growth of our employees. Benefits include competitive health, dental, and vision insurance plans; retirement savings options; paid time off and holidays; professional development opportunities; and a supportive work environment committed to diversity and inclusion. We believe in fostering a workplace where employees feel valued, empowered, and motivated to achieve their best.

Equal Opportunity

FSA Federal is an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees and applicants. We do not discriminate based on race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, or any other protected characteristic. We believe that diversity enhances our organization and strengthens our ability to serve our mission effectively.

At ITA International, we ’ re a tech-enabled professional services company. Headquartered in Newport News, Virginia, we leverage subject matter expertise, data analytics and technology to challenge boundaries and transform possibilities.

With a global presence and a passionate team of over 200 ITAers, we ’ re driven by mission success for our customers, “ In The Arena. ” Our expertise spans Operations, Training, Engineering, Nanotechnology, Statistics, Machine Learning and Software Engineering – enabling data and tech-enabled solutions that deliver real value.

Join our impactful journey at ITA International. As Theodore Roosevelt said, “ The credit belongs to the man who is actually in the arena. ” We ’ re here, standing beside our customers , ready to serve and succeed.

ITA is seeking a Junior Data Analyst to join our team.

Responsibilities

We are looking for a motivated Junior Data Analyst to join our analytics team. This entry-level position provides an exciting opportunity for individuals seeking to launch their careers in data analysis. The successful candidate will assist in collecting, analyzing, and interpreting data to support decision-making processes and enhance operational effectiveness.

Responsibilities:

Assist in gathering and preparing data from various sources for analysis.
Conduct preliminary data analysis to identify trends, patterns, and anomalies.
Create visualizations and reports to communicate findings to team members and stakeholders.

jobs.title: Data Analyst
jobs.description:
Description

Find YOUR fit at Ohio Machinery Co.! Join a winning culture that strives to be the top-performing, dynamic industry leader and partner of choice for the products and solutions that help our customers build, improve, feed, power, and protect our world. Come grow your career - See what opportunities await today!

Summary

The Data Analyst will play a vital role in transforming complex data into actionable insights, supporting informed decision-making across the organization. This role is responsible for evaluating dashboards and metrics, conducting training sessions to ensure effective utilization of data tools, and advocating for a data-driven culture. The Data Analyst will support and maintain data analysis systems, such as Power BI, and follow a structured business intelligence process to address specific business questions.

The ideal candidate will have strong analytical skills, technical expertise in data visualization platforms, and excellent communication abilities to effectively share insights with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.

Essential Functions And Responsibilities

Evaluate Dashboards and Metrics: Assess the accuracy, clarity, and relevance of dashboards and key performance indicators to ensure alignment with business objectives and decision-making needs.
Advocate for Dashboards: Promote the adoption and consistent use of dashboards across the organization, fostering a culture of data-driven decision-making.
Collaboration: Partner with team members, managers, and business stakeholders to gather requirements, clarify objectives, and deliver tailored data-driven solutions.
Training and Documentation: Develop, document, and deliver training plans and materials to ensure effective dashboard usage, administration, and data interpretation.
Continuous Development: Proactively expand knowledge of data warehouses, BI tools, enterprise applications, query languages, and organizational data structures to improve analytical outcomes.
System Support and Maintenance: Support and maintain data analysis platforms, including Power BI, ensuring system reliability, performance, and user accessibility.
Business Intelligence Analysis: Follow a structured BI process to address specific business questions, delivering statistical models, analytic reports, or clear data summaries.
Research and Insights Development: Conduct research on emerging data trends, best practices, and advanced analytics techniques to inform improvements in reporting and dashboard design.
Data Security and Compliance: Safeguard the integrity and security of enterprise data, adhering to best practices for privacy, security, and compliance with relevant regulations.

Minimum Skills And Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Data Analytics, Statistics, Computer Science, Business Intelligence, or equivalent work experience in a related field.
4+ years of experience in business intelligence, data analysis, or a related role.
Experience supporting end-users, addressing inquiries, and resolving issues in a performance-driven environment.
Experience working with large datasets and data warehousing concepts.
Previous experience in a dealership, retail, or operational analytics environment is a plus.
Proficiency with Power BI, Excel, and other reporting tools.
Strong skills in data querying techniques and SQL.
Solid understanding of statistical analysis and data modeling.
Familiarity with programming languages such as Python or R is a plus.
Experience documenting and maintaining configuration settings and BI process documentation.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
Ability to translate complex data into clear, actionable insights for a non-technical audience.
Strong problem-solving abilities and attention to detail.
Exceptional time management skills and the ability to handle multiple tasks simultaneously.
Highly self-motivated and proactive, with a strong attention to detail.
Adaptability and eagerness to learn new technologies and reporting tools.
Familiarity with Agile project management methodologies.

Employee Benefits

401(k):Match and employer discretionary contribution.
Health Insurance:Two options are available, including an HSA with a dollar-for-dollar match of up to $1,200 per year.
Dental & Vision Insurance:Comprehensive coverage options.
Financial Access:Credit Union membership is available.
Insurance:Life Insurance, Short-Term Disability, and Long-Term Disability coverage.
Educational Opportunities:Scholarships for employees’ spouses and children through the Ohio Machinery Education and Opportunity Foundation.
Compensation: Base pay, annual bonus potential, and company success share bonuses.

Physical Requirements

Sitting for extended periods of time.
Dexterity of hands and fingers to operate a computer keyboard, mouse, power tools, and to handle other computer components.
Lifting and transporting of moderately heavy objects, such as computers and peripherals.

EEO, Veterans & Disabled Employer and VEVRAA/503 Federal Contractor

jobs.title: Data Analyst III
jobs.description:
About IXIS

IXIS is a data and AI company transforming how organizations understand customers, markets, and business performance.

Founded in 2012, IXIS specializes in connecting fragmented data ecosystems and transforming complex information into actionable intelligence. Through our AI-native analytics platform, ATLAS

, we help brands, agencies, retailers, and enterprises uncover opportunities, predict outcomes, and make smarter decisions.

Our work sits at the intersection of data engineering, advanced analytics, machine learning, business strategy, and customer experience.

We are growing rapidly and are looking for exceptional analytical talent to help shape the next generation of data products and insights.

The Opportunity

IXIS is seeking a highly analytical, intellectually curious, and technically skilled Data Analyst III

to join our Analytics team.

This is not a traditional reporting role.

You will work directly with large-scale datasets, predictive models, AI-powered analytics solutions, and executive stakeholders to solve complex business problems. The ideal candidate is equally comfortable writing advanced SQL, building statistical models, designing dashboards, and presenting strategic recommendations to clients and leadership teams.

You'll partner closely with data engineers, software developers, strategists, and product leaders to help drive innovation across our ATLAS platform and client engagements.

This role is ideal for someone who enjoys turning ambiguity into clarity and using data to influence real business decisions.

What You'll Do

Advanced Analytics & Insights

Conduct exploratory analyses across large, complex, and disparate datasets
Identify meaningful trends, patterns, anomalies, and opportunities hidden within data
Develop clear, compelling narratives that connect analytical findings to business outcomes
Translate complex analytical results into actionable recommendations for technical and non-technical audiences

Predictive Modeling & Statistical Analysis

Develop and evaluate predictive models, forecasting methodologies, segmentation frameworks, and advanced analytical solutions
Design and execute statistical analyses, hypothesis tests, experiments, and measurement frameworks
Support machine learning initiatives by preparing data, validating outputs, and measuring performance

Data Products & Visualization

Build and maintain executive dashboards and self-service analytics solutions within the ATLAS platform
Create intuitive visualizations that simplify complex datasets and support strategic decision-making
Improve reporting frameworks, visualization standards, and analytical methodologies

Data Engineering & Automation

Extract, transform, and analyze data from APIs, databases, cloud platforms, and third-party systems
Build reproducible analytical workflows using SQL, Python, Git, and modern analytics tools
Contribute to scalable data models and analytical infrastructure

Client & Business Strategy

Collaborate directly with clients and internal stakeholders to solve business challenges
Support strategic planning initiatives through quantitative and qualitative research
Serve as a trusted analytical advisor for both internal teams and external partners

Innovation

Identify opportunities to improve existing processes, methodologies, products, and analytical frameworks
Evaluate emerging technologies, AI tools, and analytical techniques
Help shape the future roadmap of the ATLAS platform

Required Qualifications

What We're Looking For

Bachelor's or Master's degree in Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Economics, Data Science, Engineering, or a related quantitative discipline
5–8+ years of experience in analytics, business intelligence, data science, consulting, or related fields
Advanced SQL skills with experience querying and optimizing large datasets
Strong proficiency in Python, R, or both
Deep understanding of statistical methods, experimental design, and hypothesis testing
Experience building dashboards and visual analytics solutions
Exceptional problem-solving abilities and analytical thinking
Strong communication and storytelling skills with the ability to present complex findings clearly
Demonstrated ability to work independently in ambiguous environments

Preferred Qualifications

Experience in one or more of the following areas:

Predictive modeling and machine learning
Customer analytics and segmentation
Marketing analytics and attribution
Digital analytics (Google Analytics, Adobe Analytics, GA4)
Retail, automotive, or e-commerce analytics
Cloud analytics environments (AWS, Azure, or GCP)
API integrations and automated data pipelines
Data engineering and ETL workflows
Git-based development workflows
Agile/Scrum environments
Generative AI and AI-assisted analytics

Characteristics of Successful Candidates

The Best Candidates For This Role

Ask questions others don't think to ask
Are relentlessly curious about how businesses operate
Can move seamlessly between technical details and strategic conversations
Enjoy solving difficult, ambiguous problems
Have strong opinions backed by data
Constantly seek better methods, tools, and approaches
Take ownership and accountability for outcomes
View learning as a continuous process

Why Join IXIS?

At IXIS, you'll have the opportunity to work on challenging problems with modern technologies, AI-driven platforms, and diverse datasets that directly influence business outcomes.

You'll collaborate with a highly skilled team, contribute to innovative products, and play a meaningful role in shaping the future of data intelligence.

Benefits Include

Competitive compensation
Health, dental, and vision insurance
Short-term and long-term disability coverage
401(k) with company match
Wellness program
Professional development opportunities
Flexible hybrid work environment
Significant career growth potential

Standout Candidate

You'll stand out if you've ever built a model that changed a business decision, automated an analysis that saved hundreds of hours, uncovered an insight no one else saw, or taught yourself a technical skill simply because you wanted to solve a problem better.

jobs.description:
At Clarus, we are harnessing intelligence to propel pet health towards a brighter, smarter, more connected future. A future where information flows freely across caregivers & care environments, insights are more instant and actionable, and financial decisions are empowered.

For us, that future starts by fundamentally changing how pet insurance operates. We’re engineering systems that decomplexify coverage and facilitate reimbursement, so more pets can get the care they deserve.

Our team of pet industry veterans and passionate pet people come from across the tech and health continuums. Together, we’re openly challenging industry convention in service of creating totally new possibilities. Which is why our culture is built on the values of curiosity, entrepreneurship, humility and fierce collaboration.

About Us:
Clarus is a pioneering pet health technology company on a mission to transform the future of pet healthcare. We are dynamic entrepreneurs improving pet health by providing trusted and transparent information across the entire pet health landscape. Join us in shaping a smarter, healthier future for pets everywhere.

As we expand, we are looking for a seasoned Data Analyst under the data team to work closely with our cross functional teams to ensure data integrity and accuracy across systems.

Role Overview:
We are seeking a skilled Data Analyst to join our team. The successful candidate will be responsible for analyzing the inbound data sources such as insurance policies, claims and, product performance of claims processing systems. This role is critical in supporting the product development and data governance processes.

Key Responsibilities

Query and analyze large datasets to generate production and ad hoc reports for internal and external stakeholders; support data validation and data preparation efforts.
Manage internal data requests by clarifying stakeholder needs, conducting analyses, and effectively communicating results to end users.
Triage and document stakeholder requests, and support the development of accurate, timely responses.
Support ad hoc analysis requests from leadership, product, marketing and sales.
Serving as a knowledge expert for insurance and claims data processes and identifying areas of improvement to ensure appropriate turnaround times and data quality standards are being met.
Collaborates with data engineering team members to enhance data models, pipelines and reporting infrastructure based on evolving analytical requirements
Translates complex analytical finding into actionable insights and concise presentations for both technical and non-technical audiences
Proactively identify trends, anomalies and opportunities for growth, efficiency and risk mitigation.
Collaborate with cross functional teams to resolve data quality issues and enhance the reporting infrastructure
Ensure data integrity and accuracy across systems and reporting tools.
Support data governance lead with cataloging and documentation of data sources.
Support data definition, documentation, and alignment across product and engineering partners.
Contribute to a growing data culture and support implementation of best practices in analytics methodology and governance.

Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Information Technology, or related field.

At least 5 years of experience as a data analyst in insurance space.
Experience working with Databricks, Spark, and Azure data services.
Proficiency with NoSQL and SQL databases.
Strong attention to detail, critical thinking and problem-solving skills.
Excellent oral and written communication skills.
Proficient with data visualization tools (i.e. PowerBI).
Knowledge and experience with Python a plus.
Experience with CI/CD pipelines a plus.

Additional Information

You must be authorized to work in the U.S.
Hours are flexible, but availability during core business hours (Eastern) is expected.
Requires travel of up to 10%.

Position Summary...

What you'll do...

Job Description Summary

About Transportation Operational Analytics The Transportation Operational Analytics team serves as the central analytics hub for Walmart’s Transportation organization, driving data-informed decisions across the network. Our mission is to deliver scalable analytical tools, actionable insights, and strategic solutions that empower both the Home Office and Field Support teams.

We focus on:

Building dashboards and tools to monitor transportation KPIs and operational performance.
Supporting AOP savings initiatives through advanced analytics and optimization strategies.
Providing ad-hoc analytics for leadership to enable quick, informed decision-making.
Driving analytics for pilots and strategic initiatives, ensuring data-backed recommendations for future growth.

As part of this team, you’ll collaborate with cross-functional partners, leverage cutting-edge technologies, and apply advanced analytics to solve complex transportation challenges—ultimately improving efficiency, reducing costs, and enhancing operational excellence.

What You'll Do

Data Management & Quality

Extract and validate data from multiple sources while maintaining scalable pipelines and architectures.
Ensure data accuracy, integrity, and compliance with governance standards.

Analytics & Insights

Perform advanced analysis using SQL, Python, and statistical methods to uncover trends and opportunities.
Build dashboards and translate complex data into actionable insights that drive efficiency and cost savings.

Business Partnership

Collaborate with cross-functional teams to define business problems and align metrics.
Communicate insights through clear storytelling and data visualizations tailored to diverse audiences.

Optimization & Continuous Improvement

Lead root cause analyses and recommend process enhancements.
Identify and implement data-driven opportunities for operational efficiency.

Leadership & Technical Expertise

Ensure data quality and governance for analytics and model deployment (e.g., GCP BigQuery).

What You’ll Bring

Expertise in SQL, Python, and data visualization tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI).
Strong knowledge of data quality management, data governance, and database technologies.
Experience applying statistical techniques and exploratory data analysis.
Ability to translate business requirements into impactful data projects.
Proven track record of delivering insights in a fast-paced, cross-functional environment.

Act with Integrity Follows the law our code of conduct and company policies and sets expectations for others to do the same promotes an environment where associates feel comfortable sharing concerns and reinforces our culture of nonretaliation listens to concerns raised by associates takes action and encourages others to do the same holds self and others accountable for achieving results in a way that is consistent with our values

Act with Integrity Acts as an altruistic servant leader and is consistently humble self-aware honest and transparent

Serve our Customers and Members Delivers expected business results while putting the customer member first and consistently applying an omni merchant mindset and acts with an Every Day Low-cost mindset to drive value and Every Day Low Prices for customers members

Serve our Customers and Members Adopts a holistic perspective that considers data analytics customer member insights and different parts of the business when making plans and shaping the teams strategy

Strive for Excellence Consistently raises the bar and seeks to improve demonstrates curiosity and a growth mindset seeks feedback asks thoughtful questions fosters an environment that supports learning innovation and learning from mistakes and intelligent risk-taking and exhibits resilience in the face of setbacks

Strive for Excellence Seeks and implements continuous improvements and encourages the team to leverage new digital tools and ways of working

At Walmart, we offer competitive pay as well as performance-based bonus awards and other great benefits for a happier mind, body, and wallet. Health benefits include medical, vision and dental coverage. Financial benefits include 401(k), stock purchase and company-paid life insurance. jobs.title: Field Technician-Water Solutions
jobs.description:
Primary Purpose

JOB DESCRIPTION

The Field Technician-Water Solutions is expected to perform a variety of routine and complex tasks including fluids unloading, equipment maintenance and all assigned work at and on company property, while providing outstanding customer service.

Responsibilities

Essential Duties & Responsibilities:

Off-load a high volume of water trucks daily.
Develop understanding of the operation of all equipment on assigned routes or facilities.
Measure and record truck, and pipeline volumes. Ensure accuracy of data received and entered.
Maintain daily contact with co-workers and clients to maintain close working relationships and to ensure the reliability of Tallgrass infrastructure is always intact.
Monitor contract compliance to ensure that facilities are performing satisfactorily, and that contract and regulatory requirements are adhered to.
Monitor day-to-day operations, safety, environmental compliance, and quality as directed by Tallgrass Management. Responsible for various daily reporting requirements.
Reporting volumes and key facility operating parameters at shift change and to all related personnel.
Identify production related issues and learn appropriate solutions under supervision while consulting with the appropriate Tallgrass personnel.
Follow preventative maintenance plan and all system integrity testing.
Execute routine repairs and maintenance on all equipment. Communicate corrective maintenance needs if unable to complete on own.
Deal with a wide variety of people with tact, courtesy, and professionalism.
Speak clearly, both in person and by telephone.
Maintain a regular attendance and consistently perform at a high level.
Will work non-traditional hours as needed.
Maintain a high regard for personal safety, for the safety of company assets and employees, and the public.
Perform any other tasks assigned to support overall company operations.
Other duties as assigned.

Education

QUALIFICATIONS

High school diploma / GED or equivalent experience is required.
Work experience can be used in lieu of education.

Experience/Specific Knowledge

Should have at least six (6) months of experience in Water Related Oilfield Services.
Should have understanding and experience with water related projects — sourcing, pipelines, treatment, and disposal.
Intermediate proficiency level in MS Office applications.

Certifications, Licenses & Registrations

Must possess and maintain a valid driver’s license and a driving record satisfactory to the company and its insurers.
If required to operate a company vehicle or equipment the Technician must drive safely to and from wells and well sites, maintain a clean driving record and adhere to Company policies

Competencies, Skills & Abilities

Promote continuous safety culture.
Knowledge and understanding of industry standards and best practices.
Apply Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) for routine tasks.
Willingness to learn company specific applications.
Written and verbal communication skills.
Must be able to take direction from management, adhere to required work schedules, focus attention on details, and follow work rules.
Actively participate in teams and collaborate for improved performance.
Must possess the ability to adapt to a changing and fast paced work environment.
Ability to organize and prioritize daily work.
Ability to work outside in all weather conditions.

Physical Demands

All the physical requirements listed below are those that may be necessary for an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

Ability to lift and carry up to 32 pounds for tasks such as handling filters, tools, and cases of water up to 300 feet.
Capable of pushing forces up to 59.7 pounds of force for equipment like garage doors and skimming hatches.
Able to pull with a force of approximately 60 pounds when operating equipment such as rock baskets.
Frequent manual material handling involving bending, stooping, squatting, crouching, and kneeling to access equipment and perform tasks.
Must be able to walk, stand, climb stairs/ladders, and ascend/descend for extended periods during field operations.
Reach overhead, at shoulder, waist, knee, and floor levels, with fine motor movements and grasping/pinching capabilities.
Ability to perform whole body movements such as twisting, pivoting, lateral flexion, and exerting rotational force.
Capable of working in environmental conditions involving exposure to hot, cold, wet, and vibrating environments, along with loud noise.

Working Conditions

Varying working conditions from office settings to working outdoors in inclement weather conditions.
Working with and around industrial hazards.
Required to carry a cell phone and be available to respond during working and non-working hours.
This is an on-call position.
Hitch-7/7, 12-hour shifts and will receive overtime.
The selected candidate must successfully pass a drug screening, background check, and fit-for-duty examination, including credit reports for specific positions—after receiving a job offer and before commencing employment

Supervisory Responsibility

None

Preferred Education, Experience, Certifications, Competencies, Skills, & Abilities

Above the minimum requirements; not required but advantageous in this position:

Working understanding and knowledge of SWD (saltwater disposal) and associated equipment.
1+ years of SWD operating experience.

Compensation

The annual hourly range for this position will be $28.00-$30.00/hr.

Other Responsibilities

Please note this job description is not designed to encompass or contain a comprehensive listing of activities, duties or responsibilities that are required of the employee for this job. Duties, responsibilities, and activities may change at any time with or without notice.

About Us

Tallgrass was named one of the Top Workplaces USA and highlighted in Colorado's Top Workplaces for the past seven consecutive years. Tallgrass is a leading energy infrastructure company focused on safely, reliably, and sustainably delivering the energy and services that power our nation and enable our quality of life.

At Tallgrass, we value our teams and strive to create an environment where employees feel respected, and their contributions are valued. We aim to support employees' physical, mental, and financial well-being through a comprehensive Total Rewards Program.

Industry competitive pay
Health insurance package options that include Flexible Spending & Health Savings Accounts
Infertility Coverage
Parental Leave
401(k) with up to a 6% match that vests immediately plus an employer discretionary contribution of up to 4%
Wellness Programs and Mental Health Resources
Employer-paid life insurance, short-term disability, and long-term disability coverage
Critical Illness & Accident Insurance
Vacation, sick days, paid caregiver leave, volunteer and bereavement paid time off
Identity theft protection
Annual discretionary bonus
Generous Tuition Reimbursement Program
Company-paid holidays and floating holidays
Company vehicle (if applicable)
Employee discounts; vehicles, tires, cellular plans, and more
Networking and employee engagement events
Personal development to grow your career with us based on your strengths and interests

Recruiting timelines vary by position; however, all Tallgrass positions accept applications for at least five business days from the posting date. This position is open and still accepting applications.

Compensation

jobs.title: Project-Level Environmental Scientist
jobs.description:
About The Opportunity

SWCA Environmental Consultants is an employee-owned company specializing in natural and cultural resource management, environmental planning, and regulatory compliance. SWCA’s Chicago, Illinois team is growing and seeks a Project-Level
Environmental Scientist.
This position offers a collaborative and supportive work environment with a diverse team of scientists working on a wide range of projects, and is based in Cincinnati, OH. The successful candidate will possess a broad range of skills including a well-developed understanding of environmental permitting in the Midwest U.S. across multiple sectors including energy, utilities, and land development, strong technical field skills, and effective communication skills.

This is a full-time, regular status position located in Cincinnati, OH, with hybrid work environments encouraged. This position will report into our Chicago, ILL office

Under this job requisition, we may hire the following job position:

Assistant Project Environmental Scientist- Typically with at least 3 years of relevant industry experience and expertise
Associate Project Environmental Scientist - Typically with at least 5 years of relevant industry experience and expertise
Project Environmental Scientist - Typically with at least 7 years of relevant industry experience and expertise

Application deadline:
Application is due at 5:00 PM EDT on 6.27.2026

What You Will Accomplish

Responsibilities for this position include serving as a task manager, overseeing, and directing field activities, and managing small to mid-sized project projects. As part of this position, the Project Environmental Scientist will:

Develop project management and technical lead skills on a variety of environmental and water resource projects.
Manage a variety of tasks, including projects related to wetland science and threatened/endangered species.
Oversee and direct all aspects of environmental permitting related to Clean Water Act (CWA) Sections 401 (Water Quality Certification), 402 (National Pollutant Discharge Elimination System), and 404 (Dredge and Fill Material), as well as the U.S. Endangered Species Act, and other state and local laws and regulations.
Collaborate with a team of natural and cultural resources specialists to fulfill a variety of environmental planning and permitting needs for various projects.
Be responsible for multi-disciplinary scope of work and budget development with the natural resources team, as well as tracking scopes and budgets during project execution.
Direct agency and client coordination regarding wetland, rare species, stormwater, and compliance monitoring programs and building trusted relationships with the responsible agencies and stakeholders.
Prepare resource inventory reports, environmental permitting support, and other documents.
Organize, review, and transfer field data, and write technical reports and permit applications related to the CWA, floodplain management, and other state and local water resources regulations.
Coordinate and collaborate with other SWCA scientists, including wetland specialists, ecologists, stormwater specialists, restoration specialists, hydrologists, floodplain specialists, wildlife specialists, etc. for quality assurance for scope of work and project approach development.
Take responsibility for team safety, developing safety plans (when applicable), and anticipating potential safety hazards.

Required

Experience and qualifications for success

A minimum of a Bachelor’s degree in Wetland Science, Wildlife Biology, Environmental Science, Ecology or other related field.
A minimum of three (3) years of progressively responsible experience in natural resources.
Demonstrated prior experience leading wetland delineations, rare species investigations, construction monitoring, and other similar tasks.
Previous experience with federal, state, and local environmental laws and regulations in the midwest United States.
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills

Preferred

At least one (1) year of natural resources (or related) project management experience, including developing and implementing budgets, scopes of work, and project schedules.
Five (5) years’ experience of progressively responsible experience in natural resources.
Experience working on linear projects, including natural gas clients.
Experience working on renewable energy projects
Professional certifications including PWS, CWB, CERP, NHCWS, CESSWI, Registered (Professional) Soil Scientist, CE, or similar.

Additional Information

Valid driver's license.
Ability and willingness to travel, sometimes at moment’s notice, for extended periods, at night and occasionally on the weekend.
Ability to pass a Motor Vehicle background screening.
Available to access projects within the geographic area.
Ability to work long hours in harsh conditions.

Why Work At SWCA

SWCA Environmental Consultants
is a growing employee-owned firm, providing a full-spectrum of environmental services. Our Vision (the North Star) is
to make SWCA the best workplace and industry leader in sustainability, bringing sound science and creative solutions to global environmental challenges while maintaining a dedication to employee-ownership. We live our core values: we collaborate as #OneSWCA;
we are #AlwaysLearning
, we #NeverSettle,
and we #GiveBack.

We are 100% employee-owned
, and we build our success together. With offices across the United States, SWCA is one of the largest environmental compliance firms and ranks among Engineering News-Record’s Top 200 Environmental Firms
. We offer a supportive, team-oriented work environment and competitive wages and benefits, including an 100% employee stock ownership plan (retirement).

At SWCA, we support our team members in developing their careers to make them leaders in their industry. Our Career Landscape initiative is a process and guide designed to help develop chart rewarding career paths for employees at SWCA. We encourage professional conference attendance, internal and external professional development and training programs, education reimbursement, and bonuses for publications meeting certain criteria. All regular status employees are eligible to participate in SWCA medical, dental, vision, employee assistance, wellness, life and disability plans, and are eligible to participate in the SWCA 401(k) Profit Sharing Plan and Trust.

If you need assistance accessing SWCA’s website, completing the online application, or require accommodations at any point during the hiring process due to a disability, please contact us at accommodations@swca.com or call 1-480-581-5378. We’re committed to providing an inclusive and supportive experience for all candidates. This contact information is specifically for accommodation requests; other inquiries will not receive a response. SWCA is committed to salary equity and salary transparency for all its employees. In alignment with this commitment, SWCA posts good faith pay ranges in all its advertised job postings to promote pay equity and transparency.

SWCA is committed to salary equity and salary transparency for all its employees. In alignment with this commitment, SWCA posts good faith pay ranges in all its advertised job postings to promote pay equity and transparency.

A Project Level Scientist in this Chicago, ILL- based
position can expect a compensation rate of:

Assistant Project Environmental Scientist- $28.67/hour to $37.40/hour.
Associate Project Environmental Scientist - $35.10/hour to $45.03/hour.
Project Environmental Scientist - $74,955/year to $96,533 annually.

Actual pay within this range may depend on experience, qualifications, geographic location, client requirements where applicable, and other factors permitted by law. Regular-status employees are also eligible for performance bonuses. Candidates are also encouraged to consider SWCA’s Total Rewards package, which includes a competitive benefits package ( https://www.swca.com/careers/benefits-wellness/ ), forward-thinking workplace flexibility, outstanding corporate culture, award-winning career development, and more.

EOE - women, minorities, individuals with disabilities and veterans are encouraged to apply. At SWCA Environmental Consultants, we celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive work environment. We strongly encourage candidates from all backgrounds, including those with diverse experiences and veterans, to apply. We believe that a diverse and inclusive workforce enhances creativity, innovation, and overall organizational success.

SWCA continues to invest deeply in career development programs, delivering our award-winning Career Landscape support resources to accelerate the growth of our staff. We recognize the valuable skills and experiences our internal team members bring to SWCA’s continued success. Qualified internal candidates are encouraged to apply and will be seriously considered for this position. We believe in promoting from within, where possible, providing our existing employees with compelling opportunities to advance their careers.
