jobs.title: Data Analyst - Water Infrastructure
jobs.description:
Ardurra is seeking a Data Analyst to join our Public Works team in Houston, TX!

Ardurra is a multidisciplinary civil engineering services firm providing innovative infrastructure solutions to public and private sector clients throughout the United States. We leverage our technical expertise, integrated project delivery, and local market knowledge to deliver practical, sustainable solutions for public infrastructure, utilities, transportation, drainage, and community development projects.

Our Houston Public Works Infrastructure team partners with municipalities, local governments, utility providers, and public agencies to plan, evaluate, and improve critical infrastructure systems. We support projects involving utility master planning, capital improvement programs, infrastructure resiliency, system capacity evaluations, drainage and utility assessments, and long-range infrastructure planning.

Primary Function

The Data Analyst (Level I or II) will support water and wastewater infrastructure projects through the application of engineering principles, data analytics, and visualization techniques. This role will support hydraulic modeling efforts, develop dashboards and analytical tools, automate workflows, and transform complex infrastructure data into actionable insights for engineers, project managers, and utility clients.

The ideal candidate will possess an engineering background combined with strong analytical and programming skills, a passion for learning, and an interest in applying data science techniques to municipal infrastructure challenges.

Key Responsibilities

Develop and maintain dashboards, reports, and data visualization tools for infrastructure and utility projects.
Analyze engineering datasets and identify trends, anomalies, and opportunities for operational improvements.
Support hydraulic modeling and system analysis efforts for water and wastewater infrastructure projects.
Assist with model calibration and interpretation of hydraulic modeling results.
Utilize Python, SQL, and other analytical tools to automate workflows and process large datasets.
Work with GIS, SCADA, and other infrastructure datasets.
Support capital improvement planning, asset management, and infrastructure resiliency initiatives.
Collaborate with engineers, project managers, and technical staff to communicate findings to both technical and non-technical audiences.
Continuously learn and apply emerging technologies and data science methodologies to engineering challenges.

Required

Education and Experience Requirements

Bachelor's degree in Civil Engineering, Environmental Engineering, Chemical Engineering, or related engineering discipline from an ABET-accredited university, or an equivalent quantitative, data science, or computer science background with relevant infrastructure experience.
Minimum of 1 year (Level I) or 3 years (Level II) of experience in engineering, analytics, or infrastructure-related data analysis.
Demonstrated understanding of data science concepts, statistical methods, or analytical programming through education or professional experience.
Experience utilizing Python and/or SQL for data analysis and automation.
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills.
Ability to work independently while collaborating effectively within multidisciplinary teams.
An experienced AI worker with strong judgment about when and which AI tools are appropriate, and a track record of integrating them effectively and responsibly into technical work.
Working understanding of machine learning concepts and methods, and how they apply to data analysis problems.

Preferred

Engineer-in-Training (EIT) certification.
Master's degree in Data Science, Engineering, Water Resources, or related field.
Experience with water/wastewater hydraulic modeling.
Experience with Power BI, Tableau, or similar visualization platforms.
Experience with GIS platforms and geospatial data.
Experience with SCADA data, operational data, or utility system datasets preferred.
Familiarity with AI tools and Large Language Models.
Understanding of how machine learning applies in a water or wastewater context (e.g., demand forecasting, anomaly detection, asset condition prediction).
Experience within municipal infrastructure, public works, or water/wastewater environments.
Strong desire to learn, grow, and develop new technical skills.
Data engineering, automation, cloud infrastructure, database engineering, analytics, or machine learning certificates from online learning platforms such as Coursera, Codecademy, or Udemy.

Why Ardurra?

While Ardurra offers competitive compensation and rich benefits programs, it is our culture that truly sets us apart from our peers. We nurture a family-like culture, striving to create a work environment that is enjoyable, challenging and rewarding but also fun. We are acutely focused on developing our staff, whether through our internal Ardurra Academy or through our industry-leading Leadership program. We have made a deliberate and focused commitment to nurture a people-centric culture where people are: valued as individuals; supported in their professional and career development with multiple, varied career paths; provided the tools and resources to be successful, engaged, and satisfied in their work; and positive benefits, time-off programs, and flexibility to help maintain a healthy balance between work and home.

Ardurra is an Equal Opportunity/ Affirmative Action Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability, protected veteran status, gender identity or sexual orientation.

NOTICE TO THIRD PARTY AGENCIES:

Ardurra does not accept unsolicited resumes from recruiters or employment agencies. In the absence of a signed Agency Agreement, Ardurra will not consider or agree to payment of any referral compensation or recruiter fee. If a resume or candidate is submitted to any hiring manager without a previously signed agreement, Ardurra reserves the right to pursue and hire those candidate(s) without any financial obligation to the recruiter or agency. These candidates will be considered property of Ardurra. We’re not currently looking to add any more agencies to our list of approved vendors, so please do not contact any of our managers or recruitment team with sales calls or details of your candidates.

jobs.title: Data Analyst
jobs.description:
Koniag Professional Services, LLC, a Koniag Government Services company, is seeking a Data Analyst to support KPS and our government customer in Dallas, TX. This position requires the candidate to be able to obtain a Public Trust. This position is for a Future New Business Opportunity.

We offer competitive compensation and an extraordinary benefits package including health, dental and vision insurance, 401K with company matching, flexible spending accounts, paid holidays, three weeks paid time off, and more.

The Data Analyst will collect, analyze, and report on program data to identify trends, support decision-making, and drive process improvements. Core responsibilities include data quality oversight, system maintenance, and conducting quantitative and qualitative analyses to develop reports, visualizations, and data-driven insights for federal staff and program leadership. This is an ONSITE position required to be performed at the Dallas office locations, with telework available upon approval.

Essential Functions, Responsibilities & Duties may include, but are not limited to:

Compile, prepare, and analyze quantitative data using Excel, Python, R, and other analytical tools to support grant administration and program oversight.
Conduct both basic and complex statistical analyses to identify trends, patterns, and actionable insights across federal grant programs.
Perform longitudinal data analysis, including Program Information Report (PIR) analysis, and develop high-quality written reports explaining the results of data compilation and analysis activities.
Prepare management information reports on a periodic, cyclical, or ad-hoc basis to support program leadership and decision-making.
Identify and resolve reporting anomalies and data quality issues to ensure the integrity of program data.
Create data-driven presentations for management meetings, conferences, and leadership briefings.
Support congressional reporting and respond to other high-priority data requests in a timely and accurate manner.
Conduct trend analysis and comparative assessments across programs and regions to identify opportunities for program improvement.
Conduct qualitative analysis of grant applications, budgets, intake forms, and other grant-related documents.
Review documentation to identify errors, missing information, or required follow-up actions and communicate findings appropriately.
Analyze correspondence and communications between the federal government and grant recipients to identify themes and patterns in textual data.
Leverage AI tools appropriately while adhering to federal policies, including approved AI platforms.
Synthesize qualitative findings into actionable recommendations for program staff and leadership.
Develop compelling data stories that communicate complex insights to diverse audiences, including both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Create executive summaries and briefing materials for leadership, including materials for state conferences and internal decision-making support.
Design and deliver oral presentations with support materials to a variety of audiences.
Generate crosstabs, detailed analytical tables, and data visualizations to support reporting needs.
Translate complex data findings into clear, actionable recommendations that drive program improvements.

Education

Position Requirements:

Bachelor's degree in Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, Data Science, or a related field from an accredited college or university.

Required Experience

A minimum of 3 years of relevant post-baccalaureate professional experience directly related to data analysis.

Required Skills And Competencies

Strong analytical and critical-thinking skills with the ability to work independently and collaboratively in a team environment.
Demonstrated ability to aggregate, analyze, and present data from multiple sources in a clear and meaningful way.
Experience with methodology or implementation of AI application tools (GitHub, R, Qualtrics, Tableau, or Power BI), ArcGIS
Experience reporting analytical findings to diverse audiences, including both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Experience with Excel, Python, R, and preferably Smartsheet
Experience working with data layers and data infrastructure to support analytical and reporting activities.
Experience with qualitative data analysis methodologies, including identifying themes and patterns in textual data.
Proven ability to manage multiple analytical projects simultaneously and meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment.
Proficiency with Microsoft Office Suite (Excel, Word, PowerPoint) and web-based collaboration tools.
Professional communication skills, both written and oral, include the ability to produce high-quality written reports and deliver oral presentations.

Travel Requirements

Contractor personnel are required to travel within and outside the Region during the performance of this contract to conduct site visits, attend meetings, conferences, and training

Additional Requirements

U.S. citizenship or legal authorization to work in the United States
Ability to obtain and maintain a Public Trust security clearance
Ability to pass required background investigations for access to federal facilities and systems
Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a team environment
Ability to maintain confidentiality and handle sensitive information appropriately
Willingness to complete mandatory training requirements

Security Requirement

Ability to obtain and maintain a Public Trust security clearance

Desired Skills And Competencies

Experience working with federal grant programs, particularly in data analysis or program oversight capacity.
Knowledge of Head Start or similar early childhood education programs.
SQL, GIS, Data Visualizations, Survey Methodology or Implementation, application of AI
Knowledge of survey methodology or survey implementation practices.
Experience with AI tools and platforms, including GitHub and Qualtrics, in compliance with applicable federal policies.
Familiarity with web-based data quality and collaboration tools.

Our Equal Employment Opportunity Policy

The company is an equal opportunity employer. The company shall not discriminate against any employee or applicant because of race, color, religion, creed, ethnicity, sex, sexual orientation, gender or gender identity (except where gender is a bona fide occupational qualification), national origin or ancestry, age, disability, citizenship, military/veteran status, marital status, genetic information or any other characteristic protected by applicable federal, state, or local law. We are committed to equal employment opportunity in all decisions related to employment, promotion, wages, benefits, and all other privileges, terms, and conditions of employment.

The company is dedicated to seeking all qualified applicants. If you require an accommodation to navigate or apply for a position on our website, please get in touch with Heaven Wood via e-mail at accommodations@koniag-gs.com or by calling 703-488-9377 to request accommodations.

Koniag Government Services (KGS) is an Alaska Native Owned corporation supporting the values and traditions of our native communities through an agile employee and corporate culture that delivers Enterprise Solutions, Professional Services and Operational Management to Federal Government Agencies. As a wholly owned subsidiary of Koniag, we apply our proven commercial solutions to a deep knowledge of Defense and Civilian missions to provide forward leaning technical, professional, and operational solutions. KGS enables successful mission outcomes for our customers through solution-oriented business partnerships and a commitment to exceptional service delivery. We ensure long-term success with a continuous improvement approach while balancing the collective interests of our customers, employees, and native communities. For more information, please visit www.koniag-gs.com.

jobs.title: Data Analyst
jobs.description:
This role is part of the Jobright Direct Hiring Network, where top companies like Cresta AI, Plaud, Mercor, OpenArt, and 1,500+ others hire top talent directly through our platform.

Hiring Company: Matricstek Inc.

One-liner: Matricstek Inc. is a US-based IT consulting and employment solutions firm focused on bridging the gap between skilled professionals and industry opportunities.

Salary: $35/hr - $40/hr

Why Join Us:

Role Responsibilities

• Collect, clean, and analyze large datasets to identify trends, patterns, and insights
• Perform exploratory data analysis (EDA) and statistical analysis to support decision-making
• Develop and deploy machine learning models where applicable
• Create dashboards and reports using BI tools to communicate insights effectively
• Write complex SQL queries to extract and manipulate data from multiple sources
• Collaborate with stakeholders to understand business needs and translate them into analytical solutions
• Monitor data quality and ensure accuracy and consistency across datasets
• Present findings to non-technical stakeholders in a clear and actionable manner

Qualifications

Required

• Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, or related field
• Experience in data analysis, data science, or a similar role
• Strong proficiency in Python (Pandas, NumPy) or R
• Advanced SQL skills for data querying and transformation
• Experience with data visualization tools such as Tableau, Power BI, or Looker
• Solid understanding of statistics, probability, and data modeling
• Experience working with large datasets and relational databases

Preferred

• Experience with machine learning frameworks (Scikit-learn, TensorFlow, or PyTorch)
• Familiarity with cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure, or GCP
• Knowledge of ETL processes and data pipeline development
• Experience with big data tools (Spark, Hadoop)
• Exposure to A/B testing and experimentation

jobs.title: Data Analyst
jobs.description:
Koniag Professional Services, LLC, a Koniag Government Services company, is seeking a Data Analyst to support KPS and our government customer in Atlanta, GA. This position requires the candidate to be able to obtain a Public Trust. This position is for a Future New Business Opportunity.

We offer competitive compensation and an extraordinary benefits package including health, dental and vision insurance, 401K with company matching, flexible spending accounts, paid holidays, three weeks paid time off, and more

The Data Analyst will collect, analyze, and report on program data to identify trends, support decision-making, and drive process improvements. Core responsibilities include data quality oversight, system maintenance, and conducting quantitative and qualitative analyses to develop reports, visualizations, and data-driven insights for federal staff and program leadership. This is an ONSITE position required to be performed at Atlanta office locations, with telework available upon approval.

Essential Functions, Responsibilities & Duties may include, but are not limited to:

Compile, prepare, and analyze quantitative data using Excel, Python, R, and other analytical tools to support grant administration and program oversight.
Conduct both basic and complex statistical analyses to identify trends, patterns, and actionable insights across federal grant programs.
Perform longitudinal data analysis, including Program Information Report (PIR) analysis, and develop high-quality written reports explaining the results of data compilation and analysis activities.
Prepare management information reports on a periodic, cyclical, or ad-hoc basis to support program leadership and decision-making.
Identify and resolve reporting anomalies and data quality issues to ensure the integrity of program data.
Create data-driven presentations for management meetings, conferences, and leadership briefings.
Support congressional reporting and respond to other high-priority data requests in a timely and accurate manner.
Conduct trend analysis and comparative assessments across programs and regions to identify opportunities for program improvement.
Conduct qualitative analysis of grant applications, budgets, intake forms, and other grant-related documents.
Review documentation to identify errors, missing information, or required follow-up actions and communicate findings appropriately.
Analyze correspondence and communications between the federal government and grant recipients to identify themes and patterns in textual data.
Leverage AI tools appropriately while adhering to federal policies, including approved AI platforms.
Synthesize qualitative findings into actionable recommendations for program staff and leadership.
Develop compelling data stories that communicate complex insights to diverse audiences, including both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Create executive summaries and briefing materials for leadership, including materials for state conferences and internal decision-making support.
Design and deliver oral presentations with support materials to a variety of audiences.
Generate crosstabs, detailed analytical tables, and data visualizations to support reporting needs.
Translate complex data findings into clear, actionable recommendations that drive program improvements.

Education

Position Requirements:

Bachelor's degree in Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, Data Science, or a related field from an accredited college or university.

Required Experience

A minimum of 3 years of relevant post-baccalaureate professional experience directly related to data analysis.

Required Skills And Competencies

Strong analytical and critical-thinking skills with the ability to work independently and collaboratively in a team environment.
Demonstrated ability to aggregate, analyze, and present data from multiple sources in a clear and meaningful way.
Experience with methodology or implementation of AI application tools (GitHub, R, Qualtrics, Tableau, or Power BI), ArcGIS
Experience reporting analytical findings to diverse audiences, including both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Experience with Excel, Python, R, and preferably Smartsheet
Experience working with data layers and data infrastructure to support analytical and reporting activities.
Experience with qualitative data analysis methodologies, including identifying themes and patterns in textual data.
Proven ability to manage multiple analytical projects simultaneously and meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment.
Proficiency with Microsoft Office Suite (Excel, Word, PowerPoint) and web-based collaboration tools.
Professional communication skills, both written and oral, include the ability to produce high-quality written reports and deliver oral presentations.

Travel Requirements

Contractor personnel are required to travel within and outside the Region during the performance of this contract to conduct site visits, attend meetings, conferences, and training

Additional Requirements

U.S. citizenship or legal authorization to work in the United States
Ability to obtain and maintain a Public Trust security clearance
Ability to pass required background investigations for access to federal facilities and systems
Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a team environment
Ability to maintain confidentiality and handle sensitive information appropriately
Willingness to complete mandatory training requirements

Security Requirement

Ability to obtain and maintain a Public Trust security clearance

Desired Skills And Competencies

Experience working with federal grant programs, particularly in data analysis or program oversight capacity.
Knowledge of Head Start or similar early childhood education programs.
SQL, GIS, Data Visualizations, Survey Methodology or Implementation, application of AI
Knowledge of survey methodology or survey implementation practices.
Experience with AI tools and platforms, including GitHub and Qualtrics, in compliance with applicable federal policies.
Familiarity with web-based data quality and collaboration tools.

Our Equal Employment Opportunity Policy

The company is an equal opportunity employer. The company shall not discriminate against any employee or applicant because of race, color, religion, creed, ethnicity, sex, sexual orientation, gender or gender identity (except where gender is a bona fide occupational qualification), national origin or ancestry, age, disability, citizenship, military/veteran status, marital status, genetic information or any other characteristic protected by applicable federal, state, or local law. We are committed to equal employment opportunity in all decisions related to employment, promotion, wages, benefits, and all other privileges, terms, and conditions of employment.

The company is dedicated to seeking all qualified applicants. If you require an accommodation to navigate or apply for a position on our website, please get in touch with Heaven Wood via e-mail at accommodations@koniag-gs.com or by calling 703-488-9377 to request accommodations.

Koniag Government Services (KGS) is an Alaska Native Owned corporation supporting the values and traditions of our native communities through an agile employee and corporate culture that delivers Enterprise Solutions, Professional Services and Operational Management to Federal Government Agencies. As a wholly owned subsidiary of Koniag, we apply our proven commercial solutions to a deep knowledge of Defense and Civilian missions to provide forward leaning technical, professional, and operational solutions. KGS enables successful mission outcomes for our customers through solution-oriented business partnerships and a commitment to exceptional service delivery. We ensure long-term success with a continuous improvement approach while balancing the collective interests of our customers, employees, and native communities. For more information, please visit www.koniag-gs.com.

BASIC PURPOSE AND SCOPE OF POSITION

The primary focus of the Data Analyst II is to manage the warranty accrual planning, reserve planning and warranty expense recovery efforts.

Manage the calculation of the warranty budget.
Develop forecasting models for warranty exposure.
Administer and maintain Vendor/Factory agreements
Drive and design new methods to increase warranty expense recovery

Structure facts and conceive arguments and present to suppliers gaining concurrence of responsible costs

Establish partnerships both internally and externally and to influence without direct authority
Support warranty process development initiatives, including supplier recovery processes
Additional duties as assigned by management.

High School Diploma or GED required.
BS in Accounting, Finance, Business Analytics, or related experience preferred:
In lieu of a degree at least 7 years of experience in a similar field required.

Detail oriented with high attention to detail

Ability to resolve conflicts, and to remain calm during stressful situations.
Data analysis experience, using SAP, and Salesforce
Expert level Microsoft Office ability, especially Excel, PowerPoint, and Power Bi
Knowledge of Agricultural/Construction/Equipment Retail/Service business is a plus
Strategically-minded, well organized and disciplined with ability to multi-task

jobs.title: Exploration Geologist
jobs.description:
Exploration Geologist

Winnemucca, NV

$25 - $35/hr

Our client is an established gold producer operating a growing mining operation in northern Nevada, one of the world's premier mining jurisdictions. With active exploration programs supporting both near-mine resource growth and long-term project development, they are seeking an Exploration Geologist for a 3 month contract to support their exploration success.
This is an excellent opportunity for a geologist or experienced geological technician who enjoys hands-on core logging, geological interpretation, and working closely with exploration teams.

PERKS & BENEFITS

Compensation $25 - $35/hr
Join an active exploration program with significant geological potential
Work alongside experienced geologists and technical professionals
Gain valuable exposure to drill programs, geological interpretation, and exploration workflows
Opportunity to contribute directly to project advancement and discovery
Accommodations, and travel support provided where applicable
Collaborative team environment with a strong focus on safety
Potential for contract extensions and future exploration opportunities

WHAT YOU WILL BE DOING

Log drill core to company standards, including lithology, mineralization, alteration, structure, recovery, and geotechnical characteristics
Select, mark, and manage sample intervals while ensuring adherence to QA/QC and sampling protocols
Collect, label, package, and track samples for laboratory submission while maintaining chain-of-custody procedures
Maintain accurate digital and physical geological records, including logging databases, sample sheets, and photo documentation
Capture and organize high-quality core photography and assist with geological data management
Support geological interpretation through accurate data collection and communication with senior geologists and exploration teams

WHAT YOU BRING TO THE TABLE

Experience logging drill core in a mineral exploration, mining, or geotechnical environment.
Comfort working with geological databases, digital logging systems, and field technology.
A proactive, safety-focused approach and the ability to work effectively within a collaborative exploration team.
Degree or diploma in Geology, Earth Sciences, Geological Technology, or a related discipline, or equivalent practical experience.

ABOUT US:

TPD is a leading workforce solutions provider with a mission to help individuals succeed by providing access to opportunities in jobs, careers, and professional development. A niche provider serving North America, we specialize in placing skilled candidates within the Manufacturing, Semiconductor, and Mining industries. Whether you seek flexible or permanent positions, we assist in starting, changing, or advancing careers in a way that aligns with your goals.
We are an Equal Employment Opportunity employer that values the strength diversity brings to the workplace. All qualified applicants, regardless of race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, marital status, gender identity or expression, national origin, genetics, age, disability status, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law, are strongly encouraged to apply. Accommodations are available on request for candidates taking part in all aspects of the selection process.

jobs.title: Senior Environmental Scientist (Industrial Hygienist)
jobs.description:
NOVA Engineering & Environmental is seeking a Senior Environmental Scientist (Industrial Hygienist)
 to join our Kennesaw, GA
office. This position will support a diverse set of projects focused on indoor air quality (IAQ) assessments, environmental investigations, and hazardous materials consulting, including asbestos, mold, and lead-based paint.
This is an excellent opportunity for a driven professional who enjoys a mix of field investigations, technical reporting, and client interaction, with the ability to contribute across NOVA’s environmental service lines.
Key Responsibilities:

Perform indoor air quality (IAQ) assessments and investigations for commercial, industrial, and institutional clients
Conduct asbestos, mold, and lead-based paint inspections, sampling, and assessments
Complete air monitoring, exposure assessments, and industrial hygiene evaluations
Perform Phase I Environmental Site Assessments (ESAs) and support Phase II subsurface investigations
Conduct soil and groundwater sampling and assist with environmental data interpretation
Prepare technical reports, risk assessments, and regulatory documentation
Interface directly with clients to communicate findings and recommendations
Maintain compliance with OSHA, EPA, and state/local regulatory requirements
Support proposal development and contribute to business growth efforts
Mentor junior staff as needed and provide technical guidance

Required Qualifications:

Bachelor’s degree in Industrial Hygiene, Environmental Science, Occupational Health, or related field

Hands-on experience with: Indoor Air Quality (IAQ) investigations (Mold, asbestos, and lead-based paint assessments)

Strong understanding of field sampling techniques and environmental data collection

Excellent technical writing and communication skills

Valid driver’s license and ability to travel to project sites as needed

Preferred Qualifications:

Certified Industrial Hygienist (CIH) certification highly preferred

The following certifications are also preferred: Asbestos Inspector/Management Planner, Lead Inspector/Risk Assessor, Mold Inspector, OSHA HAZWOPER, and NIOSH 582
Experience with Phase I & II ESAs, soil and groundwater sampling, air sampling and exposure monitoring
Familiarity with ASTM standards, OSHA regulations, and EPA guidelines
Professional certifications such as CSP, CHMM, or related credentials

Work Environment & Physical Requirements:

Combination of field and office work, with frequent site visits
Ability to work in varied environmental conditions, including heat, humidity, and confined spaces 
Occasional lifting and handling of field equipment
Potential exposure to airborne particulates or hazardous materials in controlled environments

jobs.title: Field Technician
jobs.description:
Overview

At UES, we’re a team of more than 4,000 engineers, scientists, geologists, inspectors, technicians, and drillers united by a shared purpose—to make a meaningful impact in the communities we serve. As a national leader in geotechnical engineering, environmental consulting, and materials testing and inspection, we collaborate on transformative projects across transportation, energy, water, healthcare, and more. Learn more about the benefits of joining Team UES and our core values at careers.teamues.com.

Position Overview

The Field Technician is responsible for performing field inspections, testing, and data collection related to foundation construction and geotechnical engineering. Working under the supervision of senior technicians and engineers, the technician ensures compliance with project specifications, industry standards, and safety requirements.This role involves site work, detailed documentation, and collaboration with construction and engineering teams to help ensure the safe and proper installation of foundation systems.

Responsibilities

Perform field observations, testing, and documentation during the installation of foundation systems
Collect and log field data including foundation depths, installation torque or blow counts, concrete volumes, rebar placement, and alignment.
Monitor construction activities to ensure they conform to project specifications and geotechnical recommendations.
Conduct on-site testing such as concrete sampling, slump tests, air content, and temperature measurements during foundation pours.
Document findings and prepare accurate and timely field reports, logs, and daily summaries.
Communicate effectively with contractors, engineers, and field supervisors to report progress and identify any non-conformance or issues.
Ensure all equipment used is calibrated, maintained, and operated according to company standards.
Follow project schedules and meet daily site responsibilities with flexibility to adapt to changing conditions.
Adhere to all site-specific and company-wide safety protocols, including the use of required PPE.
Participate in training, certification, and development opportunities to enhance technical knowledge and skills.
Support other field-testing duties as needed for geotechnical and construction materials testing projects.
Other duties as assigned

Qualifications

High school diploma or equivalent required; vocational training or coursework in construction, engineering, or geotechnical disciplines preferred.
0-3 years of experience in a field-based or foundation-related construction preferred
Valid driver’s license with an acceptable driving record; ability to operate company-assigned vehicles safely.
Industry certifications such as ACI Level 1 Concrete Testing, or ability to obtain within a specified time period.
Commitment to workplace safety, following OSHA standards and company safety policies, including the proper use of personal protective equipment (PPE).
Familiarity with common foundation construction methods and terminology is a plus.
Basic computer proficiency, including experience with Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, Outlook) and Field Data Collection (FDC) software or similar industry tools.
Mathematical skills sufficient to add, subtract, multiply, and divide into all units of measure using whole numbers, common fractions, and decimals.
Ability to read, write, and interpret blueprints, procedures, manuals, technical documents, and reports related to drilling, safety, and project requirements.
Ability to perform physical tasks, including lifting and carrying equipment weighing up to 50 lbs., standing for extended periods, and working in various weather and environmental conditions.
Efficient time management, organizational, and problem-solving skills, with the ability to complete tasks efficiently and accurately.
Attention to detail to ensure accurate sample collection, testing procedures, and documentation.
Ability to work independently and collaboratively, demonstrating a strong work ethic and commitment to producing high-quality results.
Flexibility to work varying schedules, including early mornings, weekends, overtime, and potential overnight travel as project demands require.

Travel Requirements

This position requires extensive travel, approximately up to 70% of the time, in support of business

objectives and client engagements. Travel may be both scheduled and on short notice, depending on

project needs. Destinations typically include job sites, field locations, or regional offices, where work may

be conducted in outdoor or operational environments. Adherence to all company safety protocols and

use of PPE is required during travel and on-site work .

Physical Demands & Work Environment

This position primarily works in an outdoor work environment, regularly exposed to varying field conditions, including:
Sites that may not be accessible by vehicle, requiring travel on foot through wetlands, swamps, grasslands, fields, and other uneven or rugged terrain with natural and man-made obstacles such as sand, brush, and debris.
Moderate to loud noise levels, common in active construction and industrial environments.
Exposure to environmental elements including dirt, dust, sun, heat, humidity, and adverse weather conditions.

The physical requirements for this position may include and are not limited to:
Ability to lift and/or carry 35 to 75 lbs. up to 75 feet, involving regular repetitive motion.
Ability to stand, walk, drive, and operate equipment for extended periods.
Frequent use of hands and arms for typing, handling tools, and operating equipment.
Ability to bend, twist, reach, stoop, pull, kneel, climb, balance, lift, carry, and push as required for fieldwork and inspections.
Ability to speak and hear effectively to communicate in noisy environments and with project teams.
Ability to use your senses to see (including close, distant, and peripheral vison, depth vision, and ability to focus) smell

EEO Statement

UES is an Equal Opportunity Employer and is proud to recruit the most qualified candidates. Please see our full EEO Statement at the bottom of the page
here

jobs.title: Senior Specialist, Data Analyst/AI Specialist
jobs.description:
Job Description

In this role, you’ll make an impact in the following ways:

Assisting with the design, testing, deployment, and ongoing management of digital employees and AI‑enabled tools that support legal workflows, including contracting and negotiations.
Supporting enterprise AI governance efforts, including intake triage, issue tracking, workflow coordination, and the maintenance of governance documentation.
Performing data analytics and reporting to support transactional work, including extracting, analysing, and presenting insights on contracts and trends, and partnering with attorneys and other stakeholders to maintain and enhance contracting‑related datasets, dashboards, and reporting used for management, risk, and strategic decision‑making.
Supporting the administration, optimization, and adoption of legal technology platforms, including CLM systems, contract repositories, analytics tools, and AI‑driven legal solutions.
Helping to develop, document, and maintain processes, controls, and playbooks relating to AI use, data governance, and legal operations.
Communicating and coordinating with a broad range of internal and external stakeholders, including business teams, sourcing, and external counsel, to support contracting processes, vendor management, data quality, and related governance initiatives.

To be successful in this role, we’re seeking the following:

Experience in a paralegal, analyst, or legal operations role, preferably within a corporate or financial services environment.
Strong data analytics skills, including experience working with structured and unstructured data, spreadsheets, reporting tools, dashboards, or legal analytics platforms.
Demonstrated experience or strong interest in AI, automation, and advanced legal technology.
Experience supporting contracting, negotiations, or third-party governance is strongly preferred.
High degree of comfort working with technology platforms and digital workflows.
Strong analytical, organizational, and problem-solving skills with attention to detail.
Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.
Effective communicator and collaborator.
Curious, proactive, and motivated to contribute to legal innovation.

At BNY, our culture speaks for itself. We’re proud to be recognized for our commitment to innovation, inclusion, and excellence - both as a firm and within our Legal department.

About Us

At BNY, our culture allows us to run our company better and enables employees’ growth and success. As a leading global financial services company at the heart of the global financial system, we influence nearly 20% of the world’s investible assets. Every day, our teams harness cutting-edge AI and breakthrough technologies to collaborate with clients, driving transformative solutions that redefine industries and uplift communities worldwide.

Recognized as a top destination for innovators, BNY is where bold ideas meet advanced technology and exceptional talent. Together, we power the future of finance – and this is what is all about. Join us and be part of something extraordinary.

Our Benefits And Rewards

BNY offers highly competitive compensation, benefits, and wellbeing programs rooted in a strong culture of excellence and our pay-for-performance philosophy. We provide access to flexible global resources and tools for your life’s journey. Focus on your health, foster your personal resilience, and reach your financial goals as a valued member of our team, along with generous paid leaves, including paid volunteer time, that can support you and your family through moments that matter.

BNY is an Equal Employment Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer - Underrepresented racial and ethnic groups/Females/Individuals with Disabilities/Protected Veterans.

BNY assesses market data to ensure a competitive compensation package for our employees. The expected base salary for this position when employment commences can be found in the Job Info section at the bottom of the posting.

jobs.title: Data Analyst
jobs.description:
Role: Data Analyst

Location: Phoenix, Arizona, United States

Experience Required - 8+ Years

Must Have Technical/Functional Skills

SQL , Data Analysis & Analytics, Data Governance, Data Quality Management, Metadata & Data Lineage Management, Reporting & Visualization, Cloud Data Platforms(GCP), Data Integration & ETL, AI/ML Fundamentals

The Role

As a Data Analyst / Data Management & Governance Professional with Emerging AI Experience, you will play a key role in analyzing, managing, and governing financial data while supporting the development and implementation of AI-enabled solutions. You will collaborate with engineers, product managers, data stewards, and business stakeholders to ensure high-quality, reliable, and compliant data assets that drive business decisions and customer-facing solutions.

This role provides opportunities to deepen your expertise in data analytics, data management, data governance, reporting, regulatory compliance, and cloud-based data platforms, while gaining hands-on exposure to Artificial Intelligence (AI), Large Language Models (LLMs), Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), and Agentic AI technologies. Working within a highly regulated financial environment, you will contribute to building scalable data solutions and support AI initiatives under the guidance of experienced engineers and data leaders.

Responsibilities

• Analyze and interpret large financial datasets to generate actionable insights, support decision-making, and improve business outcomes.
• Support data management initiatives, including data quality, data lineage, metadata management, master data management, and data lifecycle processes.
• Contribute to the implementation and maintenance of data governance frameworks, policies, standards, controls, and regulatory compliance requirements.
• Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure data accuracy, consistency, accessibility, and governance across enterprise data platforms.
• Assist in the development and maintenance of reporting solutions, dashboards, KPIs, and analytical models to support business and operational needs.
• Participate in cloud-based data platform initiatives, including data migration, integration, automation, and process optimization activities.
• Support AI and machine learning initiatives by preparing, validating, and governing datasets used for model development and deployment.
• Gain hands-on experience with AI technologies, including Large Language Models (LLMs), Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), and Agentic AI solutions, while assisting in the development and testing of AI-powered features and workflows.
• Contribute to monitoring, evaluating, and improving data and AI solution performance, reliability, and compliance in production environments.
• Work closely with product managers, engineers, data stewards, and business stakeholders to translate business requirements into scalable data and analytics solutions.
• Support the documentation of data governance processes, data dictionaries, business rules, and operational procedures to ensure transparency and compliance.
• Participate in continuous improvement efforts focused on data quality, governance maturity, automation, and AI adoption across the organization.
Preferred Skills & Experience
• Experience in Data Analysis, Data Management, Data Governance, Data Quality, Reporting, and Regulatory Compliance.
• Knowledge of SQL, data visualization tools (Power BI, Tableau, Looker, etc.), and cloud-based data platforms.
• Understanding of data governance concepts, including metadata management, data lineage, data stewardship, and data controls.
• Exposure to cloud technologies such as Google Cloud Platform (GCP), AWS, or Azure.
• Beginner-level knowledge or hands-on experience with Artificial Intelligence (AI), Machine Learning, Large Language Models (LLMs), Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), or Agentic AI concepts.
• Strong analytical, problem-solving, communication, and stakeholder management skills.

Base Salary Range : $90,000 to $130,000 Per Annum

TCS Employee Benefits Summary:
Discretionary Annual Incentive.
Comprehensive Medical Coverage: Medical & Health, Dental & Vision, Disability Planning & Insurance, Pet Insurance Plans.
Family Support: Maternal & Parental Leaves.
Insurance Options: Auto & Home Insurance, Identity Theft Protection.
Convenience & Professional Growth: Commuter Benefits & Certification & Training Reimbursement.
Time Off: Vacation, Time Off, Sick Leave & Holidays.
Legal & Financial Assistance: Legal Assistance, 401K Plan, Performance Bonus, College Fund, Student Loan Refinancing.

jobs.title: CRM & Data Analyst
jobs.description:
There has never been a more exciting time to join Chicago Fire FC.

As one of Major League Soccer's original clubs, Chicago Fire FC is in the midst of an exciting era fueled by tremendous investment, ambition, and growth. With a new $100M state-of-the-art Endeavor Health Performance Center now open, new offices in Chicago's iconic Wrigley Building, and plans underway for a privately-funded $750M+ soccer-specific stadium set to open in 2028 in the heart of Chicago, the Club is building the future of soccer in one of the world's great sports cities.

Chicago Fire FC is becoming a cultural force in Chicago by connecting communities, shaping experiences, and creating new ways for fans to engage with the game and the city. The Club's future home, McDonald’s Park, will anchor Chicago's newest neighborhood and serve as a catalyst of growth for the next chapter of professional sports and entertainment in the city.

Joining Chicago Fire FC today means joining an organization on the rise. Our growth creates once-in-a-lifetime opportunities for ambitious professionals to generate meaningful impact and help shape the future of our Club. Whether on the pitch working match day or in the front office, every team member plays a role in building something incredibly special.

At Chicago Fire FC, our mission is to entertain, serve, and unite Chicago through world-class experiences and a relentless pursuit of excellence. We are guided by our core values: Integrity, Respect, Innovation, Accountability, and a commitment to Never Settle.

If you're looking for a place where your work will matter, your ideas will be heard, and your career can grow alongside one of the most ambitious projects in professional sports, we'd love to hear from you.

Position Overview

Chicago Fire Football Club is seeking a CRM & Data Analyst to support and optimize the Club’s fan data and CRM ecosystem. This role will report to the Sr. Director, Marketing and serve as the primary administrator for Salesforce on the Business Strategy & Analytics team, partnering closely with Marketing, Ticketing and Corporate Partnerships to improve the quality, automation, reporting and fan engagement initiatives.

Job Responsibilities

Serve as the primary administrator and subject matter expert for Salesforce, ensuring the platform supports the operational and strategic needs of the Club.
Manage day-to-day Salesforce administration, including user management, security permissions, data hygiene, field configuration and workflow/automation (Flows, Process Builder, triggers).
Lead CRM onboarding and training for new employees, while developing and maintaining training materials (guides, FAQs, Trailhead paths, etc.).
Partner with Ticketing, Corporate Partnerships, Marketing and other business stakeholders to identify, document and implement CRM enhancements that improve efficiency, data quality and user adoption.
Build, maintain and optimize Salesforce reports, dashboards and audience segments to support revenue generation, fan engagement, marketing performance and business decision-making.
Troubleshoot, monitor and enhance integrations between Salesforce and related tools and data providers (JotForm, deal management platforms, lead nurturing tools like Conversica, integrated mobile apps, deduplication tools, data providers like ZoomInfo, etc.).
Collaborate with the Business Strategy & Analytics team to reconcile CRM with external data sources, including integrations with the Club’s data warehouse (e.g., Snowflake, Databricks) and data visualization tools (e.g., Tableau, Sigma).
Utilize SQL and other analytical tools to investigate data issues, validate business processes, support reporting requirements and improve data integrity across systems.
Support fan lifecycle marketing initiatives through Salesforce campaigns, journeys, audience segmentation, lead routing and automation workflows.
Develop and maintain documentation for CRM processes, integrations, data governance standards and system configurations.
Perform routine system audits, data quality reviews, duplicate management and platform health assessments to ensure long-term system reliability.
Stay current on Salesforce platform releases, emerging CRM technologies, AI-enabled capabilities and industry best practices, recommending enhancements where appropriate.
Support cross-functional initiatives focused on fan engagement, membership growth, sponsorship activation, premium sales and revenue optimization.
Other duties as assigned.

Required Qualifications

3+ years of professional Salesforce administration experience, with a strong understanding of CRM architecture, workflows, automation and CRM best practices.
Hands-on experience configuring and supporting Salesforce, including Flows, validation rules, security models, custom objects, reports, dashboards and user management.
Strong SQL skills and experience working with relational databases to query, validate and troubleshoot fan and business data.
Experience leading CRM user onboarding, training, change management and adoption initiatives across cross-functional business teams.
Experience supporting CRM integrations and connected business systems, including marketing automation, ticketing, lead management or fan engagement platforms.
Experience building reports, dashboards and audience segments to support business operations, sales, marketing and fan engagement initiatives.
Strong understanding of data governance, data quality management and CRM data hygiene best practices.
Ability to translate business requirements into scalable CRM solutions while communicating effectively with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Strong project management, organizational and problem-solving skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities and meet deadlines.
Exceptional attention to detail and commitment to maintaining data integrity and system reliability.
Ability to work independently while collaborating effectively within a fast-paced, entrepreneurial and high-performing environment.

Preferred Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in information systems, Computer Science, Business Analytics, Data Analytics or a related field, or equivalent work experience.
Salesforce Administrator Certification and/or additional Salesforce certifications such as Advanced Administrator, Platform App Builder, Sales Cloud Consultant or Marketing Cloud certifications.
Experience working with cloud data warehouse platforms such as Snowflake, Databricks, BigQuery or Redshift.
Experience using business intelligence and visualization tools such as Sigma, Tableau, Power BI or similar platforms.
Familiarity with Python or other scripting languages for data analysis, process automation, or system integrations.
Experience supporting fan lifecycle marketing, audience segmentation, marketing automation, or campaign management initiatives.
Experience within professional sports, entertainment, live events, or a consumer-facing subscription business.
Familiarity with cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure or Google Cloud Platform.
Experience within a sports organization or entertainment business.

Compensation

This role offers a salary range of $65,000.00 - $75,000.00
Final salary is determined by carefully considering the applicant's background, experience, and qualifications. Base pay may vary significantly based on geographic location, job-specific knowledge, skills, and overall experience. This role is classified as Full-time, exempt
This role is bonus eligible based on club-wide goals
Additional benefits & perks: Official Chicago Fire FC Website

Please Note

Chicago Fire FC is committed to fostering, cultivating, and preserving a culture of diversity, equity, accessibility, and inclusion. Our diversity initiatives start with the recruitment and selection process; therefore, we’d like to welcome all diverse candidates to apply to opportunities within our Club.
CFFC is committed to providing equal employment opportunity and maintaining a workplace for employees and applicants for employment that is free from discrimination. The Company does not discriminate in employment opportunities or practices on the basis of race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy), national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, citizenship, age, veteran or military status, disability, sexual orientation, sexual orientation or expression, marital status or any other characteristic protected by law. This policy governs all aspects of employment, including hiring, promotion, compensation, discipline, termination, and access to benefits and training.
If, because of a medical condition or disability, you need a reasonable accommodation for any part of the application process, please contact the People and Culture Department at [email protected] to let us know the nature of your request and your contact information.

MLS Privacy Policy

jobs.title: Solutions Engineer II - DoD
jobs.description:
Radar Reinvented.

Echodyne offers the world's first compact solid-state true beam-steering radar for a wide range of industries and applications. Our high-performance radars work in all weather and are designed for autonomous vehicles, uncrewed aircraft & drones, and security of borders, critical infrastructure, and smart cities. The company combines the patented technology of metamaterials with powerful software to create a radar sensor with unprecedented performance at commercial price points. Echodyne offers its radars to companies working in Automotive, Transportation, Critical Infrastructure Protection, Border Security, Smart Cities, Uncrewed Aircraft Systems (UAS), and Airspace Management including Urban Air Mobility (UTM).

Echodyne is seeking a Solutions Engineer to join our fast-growing team.

RESPONSIBILITIES

Be a key member of the Echodyne sales team responsible for both pre and post-sales technical engagement with Echodyne's customer base.
Showcase Echodyne's products by performing on-site radar demonstrations at external events, conferences, partner, and customer sites
Support the formation of customer and partner relationships by presenting the value that our radar products bring to their solutions and applications
Lead installation planning and integrations, execute deployment configuration and system tuning, and provide ongoing technical support and troubleshooting.
Manage relationships with existing and potential customers and partners while at demonstrations to ensure a positive impression.
Participate in post-demo data analysis and report creation
Support the scaling of Echodyne's customer and partner bases by developing and maintaining training materials, including application notes and user manuals.
Help improve Echodyne's products by regularly collecting customer feedback and interfacing with Echodyne product managers and engineers.
Up to 50% travel may be required.
Other related tasks required to fulfill the needs of the business.

REQUIRED SKILLS / EXPERIENCE

Proven track record of learning a technical product and conveying the value of that product to potential customers.
Personal integrity, trustworthiness, and respect while representing Echodyne, demonstrating the product, answering questions, and effectively communicating the product and company value propositions.
Ability to multi-task, work independently, prioritize tasks, honor rules of engagement; and follow policies, processes, and procedures.
Resiliency with a positive attitude, as well as analytical, reasoning, and problem-solving skills.
Organized and detail-oriented with excellent follow-up and decision-making skills.
Solid technical foundation and good technical writing skills.
Enthusiastic with outstanding customer-communication skills, including the ability to convey high-level overviews and deeply detailed explanations.
Must be able to regularly lift, carry and move equipment, occasionally up to 50 pounds.

Desired Skills / Experience

(Looking for one or more as a complement to the core skills)

Experienced in coordinating efforts across teams and organizations.
Experience working on a sales team
Experience deploying or operating radar systems, especially Electronically Scanning Array radars.
Familiarity with a computer programming language, preferably Python or Matlab.
Experience with Linux operating systems and using a Command Line Interface.
Basic understanding of digital signal processing functions and application to various sensor modalities. Direct experience with radar signal processing a plus.
Comfortable troubleshooting both hardware and software issues and working with customers to determine root causes of issues.
Experience conducting basic data plotting and analysis.

QUALIFICATIONS

5+ years of experience in a quota-carrying technical role

Echodyne's technology is export controlled by the U.S. Government and we must evaluate an applicant's eligibility to handle export-controlled information or obtain required Government authorizations.  Therefore, we will ask you as part of the application process to identify whether you are a U.S. Citizen or green card holder, or have asylum/refugee status in the U.S.

WHAT WE OFFER

The pay range for this position is a base salary of $100,000 - $120,000 per year plus commission
.

This is an exempt position.

LOCATION: Remote
TRAVEL: Travel as required, both air and ground; 40-50%

If your skills generally align but not perfectly, please apply anyway. There may be another role that's a great fit for you!

We offer competitive compensation and benefits to our full-time, US-based employees, including:

RSU (Restricted Stock Units)
Generous Benefits:
Fully-paid medical, dental, and vision insurance premiums for you
Choice of PPO or HDHP/HSA

Mental Health coverage through Springhealth & Talkspace (Premera)

jobs.title: Project Coordinator
jobs.description:
Location:
Onsite in East Windsor, NJ

Employment Status:
Full-Time

Compensation:
$55,000 – $75,000 Annually + Bonus

Travel:
Up to 25% (as needed)

Who We Are

Thermo Systems is a global, full-service control systems integration partner delivering premier class automation solutions across the Energy, Life Sciences, and Mission Critical
markets.

Guided By The Thermo Way, We Are Unified, Dynamic, And Committed To Delivering Excellence. Our Culture Is Grounded In Three Core Principles

Employee Success – investing in our people and their growth
Customer Success – delivering reliable, high-quality solutions
Financial Strength – operating with discipline and long-term focus

We believe strong teams, trusted partnerships, and technical excellence drive sustainable success.

The Opportunity

The Project Coordinator supports the successful delivery of automation and control system integration projects by providing project coordination, controls, and administrative support to Project Managers and the PMO.

This role plays a critical part in maintaining project organization, supporting the Stage-Gate delivery model, and ensuring project documentation, reporting, and communication remain accurate and on track.

The Project Coordinator acts as a central point of coordination across Engineering, Procurement, Field/Operations, and customers to support day-to-day execution and follow-through.

What You’ll Do

Core Responsibilities:

Project Coordination & Communication

Coordinate internal and customer meetings including agendas, notes, action items, and follow-ups.
Maintain stakeholder lists and support project communication plans.
Serve as a coordination point across Engineering, Procurement, Field/Operations, subcontractors, and customers.
Ensure timely follow-through on project updates, deliverables, and communications

Stage-Gate & Documentation Management

Support Project Managers in executing Thermo Systems’ Stage-Gate model.
Maintain and organize project documentation, Gate artifacts, and approval records.
Ensure proper version control, configuration management, and audit readiness.
Track Gate readiness and escalate risks to schedule adherence.

Project Controls & Reporting

Maintain project schedules, milestone trackers, and look-ahead plans.
Track action items, deliverables, and dependencies with clear ownership and deadlines.
Prepare recurring status updates, dashboards, and PMO reporting.
Support cost tracking, PO logs, invoice routing, and basic budget visibility as directed.

Change Control & Risk Support

Track and manage change requests, including documentation and approval workflows.
Maintain risk registers and support mitigation tracking.
Track project issues, decisions, and lessons learned

Procurement & Vendor Coordination

Support procurement activities including RFQs, quote tracking, and submittal logs.
Assist with vendor and subcontractor coordination, including documentation and onboarding requirements.
Track delivery schedules and follow up on vendor commitments

Quality & Closeout Support

Assist with QA/QC documentation including inspections, test records, and punch lists.
Support project closeout including as-builts, O&M manuals, training records, and final deliverables.
Contribute to lessons learned and continuous improvement efforts.
Provide feedback to PMO on process and template improvements

What We’re Looking For

Education & Experience:

0–5 years of experience in project coordination, project administration, or operations support (industrial, construction, or technical environments preferred).
Bachelor’s degree in business, engineering, construction management, operations, or related field preferred.
Equivalent practical experience will be considered in lieu of a degree.
Familiarity with basic project management concepts (scope, schedule, cost, risk, and change control) is a plus.

Skills & Qualifications

Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities
Effective communication skills across cross-functional teams and customers
Detail-oriented with strong follow-through and accountability
Ability to work independently while supporting a team environment
Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Outlook)
Experience with project tools (MS Project, Smartsheet, SharePoint, Teams, Jira, or similar) is a plus
Ability to quickly learn internal systems, tools, and PMO processes

What You’ll Get

Competitive compensation with annual bonus opportunity
401(k) with company match
Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision coverage with low-cost options
Paid time off and sick leave
Tuition reimbursement and professional credential support
In-house and external technical training opportunities
Exposure to complex, high-impact automation projects
A collaborative culture built on integrity, accountability, and teamwork
Learn more at www.thermosystems.com

Work Environment & Physical Requirements

The physical and environmental demands described here are representative of those required to perform the essential functions of this role. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform essential functions.

jobs.title: Project Coordinator
jobs.description:
KOMPAN US is looking for a Project Coordinator to work with our sales professionals and field project managers from the early planning stages of playground and outdoor fitness projects all the way through installation and project completion.
You will have dedicated area sales managers and field project managers to support, and will be part of a team of Project Coordinators located at the US headquarters of KOMPAN in Austin, TX.
As a global leader with an organizational focus on people, passion, progress, and performance, our mission is to create healthier and happier communities by delivering the best in play and fitness solutions. We’re KOMPAN - let’s play!

Who We Are

For more than 50 years, KOMPAN has researched, designed, and built innovative and imaginative commercial playground equipment and outdoor fitness sites that captivate all ages and abilities.
In collaboration with our global headquarters in Denmark, KOMPAN designs, manufactures, and installs more than 1,000 sites a month across 90 different countries. Our North American headquarters, located in Austin, Texas, supports field sales around the U.S. and Canada.
At KOMPAN, we take pride in our commitment to the global environment. We exceed sustainability efforts and make it possible to lower carbon emissions by utilizing recycled ocean waste, consumer-recycled plastics, and textile waste to create playgrounds that are born green or made green.

What You’ll Do

Plan and prepare overviews of the installation schedules for playground and outdoor fitness construction projects
Collaborate with a team of designated sales professionals, field project managers, installers, third-party suppliers, and internal colleagues such as playground designers and pre-sales coordinators
Conduct pre-start meetings for project hand-off to verify that documentation is complete, contacting the sales professional or customer as needed to obtain clarification
Schedule and coordinate the start and end dates of installers
Coordinate with customers, field project managers, and third-party vendors to ensure installations meet customer expectations
Submit purchase orders for equipment and installation
Coordinate deliveries of KOMPAN and third-party products to US sites
Process change orders and adjust documentation to execute a successful installation
Ensure timely, accurate, and complete data in the project management software
Work with a team to troubleshoot unforeseen onsite conditions and minimize additional costs
Generate submittal packages and closeout documents to obtain project completion certificates from customers and installers

What You’ll Need

3 or more years of professional experience
High School Diploma/GED required; advanced education preferred
Excellent time management, project management, and organizational skills
Microsoft Excel experience

jobs.description:
Do you love helping people through difficult situations?

Then don’t miss your chance to join our Franchise as a new Project Coordinator. In this position, you will be making a difference each and every day. We have a sincere drive toward the goal of helping make fire and water damage “Like it never even happened”!

Our Franchise is seeking someone who is comfortable working hard in challenging situations, enjoys meeting new people, has excellent communication skills, enjoys supervising others, and is a serious multi-tasker. If you are self-motivated and have superb interpersonal skills, then you’ll thrive in this work environment. Are you highly dependable and super-excited about routinely exceeding expectations? Then you may be our perfect hero
!

As a valued SERVPRO® Franchise employee, you will receive a competitive pay rate, with opportunity to learn and grow.

Job Description

Perform all job file coordinator tasks related to customer calls, job monitoring, tracking, coordination, and audits of jobs. Create preliminary estimates and job file backup. Perform general office duties, such as drafting correspondence, filing, and creating reports.

Responsibilities

Monitor job file status and job file audit status
Maintain job file WIPs
Monitor and ensure client requirements are followed
Review and validate initial field documentation
Create preliminary estimate
Daily job file coordination, including preparing job file reports, performing job file backup, and completing job file audit process
Maintain internal and external communications
Complete and review job file documentation for final upload and the audit process
Perform job close-out

Qualifications

2+ year(s) of administrative or office-related experience and business experience
Experience in the commercial cleaning and restoration or insurance/service industry is desired
Experience with writing estimates, job file processes, and quality assurance, a plus
Outstanding written and verbal communication skills, including proper pronunciation and grammar, and a consistently courteous and professional tone of voice at all times
Ability to remain calm and professional during tense or stressful situations
Excellent organizational skills and strong attention to detail
Very self-motivated and goal-oriented
Capability to work in a fast-paced, team-oriented office environment
Proficiency in Microsoft Office (i.e. Outlook, Word, Excel)
Ability to learn new software, including Xactimate and proprietary software
Minimum of HSD/GED, Associates/bachelor’s degree preferred
Ability to successfully complete a background check subject to applicable law

jobs.title: Remote - Project Coordinator
jobs.description:
1. About Our Client:
The organization operates within case management and project operations, addressing the challenges of managing large volumes of claims and associated communications. It supports efficient case processing and settlement management through coordinated project oversight, data handling, and claimant communication.

2. About the Opportunity:
The Project Coordinator role is designed to support the project management team in overseeing case management activities across the organization. This position ensures the smooth execution of project plans, timely processing of claims, and effective coordination of resources, contributing to the successful delivery of settlement and case management objectives.

3. Responsibilities:
• Support company-wide case management through skip tracing, handling escalated claimant communications, assisting with IVRs and social media, and organizing document retention
• Assist project management staff in managing cases and implementing detailed project plans
• Create and maintain comprehensive project documentation, including status updates, deadlines, and task ownership
• Monitor and communicate project milestones and schedule commitments
• Coordinate internal resources and third parties for project execution
• Assist with case invoice creation and execution
• Review settlement agreement documentation for thorough understanding
• Process, deduplicate, normalize, optimize, and analyze large datasets and prepare reports
• Ensure timely and accurate processing of claims and correspondence per settlement agreements
• Review claims and documentation to determine eligibility
• Follow adjudication policies for proper claim payment or appropriate program placement
• Respond to notice packet requests accurately and promptly
• Conduct skip tracing for undeliverable notices and checks
• Resolve issues arising from claims adjudication
• Support distribution of settlement benefits and post-distribution services
• Maintain confidentiality of sensitive information
• Deliver high-level customer service representing the organization positively
• Enhance industry knowledge and application proficiency
• Communicate effectively with colleagues
• Exhibit organizational skills and attention to detail
• Provide feedback and facilitate communication with management
• Adhere to business processes in task performance
• Take on new tasks and responsibilities as needed
• Perform other duties as assigned

4. Requirements:
• 1-2 years of project support or associate experience in business, accounting, finance, paralegal, or related fields
• Demonstrated track record of accomplishments
• Preferred experience in financial, insurance, or legal industries
• Ability to manage multiple tasks productively in a fast-paced environment
• Strong written, verbal, communication, and research skills
• Flexibility and responsiveness to urgent matters
• Ability to work patiently with diverse individuals
• Professional and positive demeanor toward clients, vendors, and staff
• Problem diagnosis and creative problem-solving skills
• Commitment to confidentiality
• Excellent attention to detail and time management
• Independent work capability
• Strategic and functional problem analysis and resolution
• Proficiency in MS Office applications (Word, Excel, Outlook, PowerPoint)
• Authorization to work in the United States without sponsorship

5. Pay Range and Compensation Package:
• The pay range and compensation package for this role will be determined based on the candidate’s experience, skills, and other relevant factors.

jobs.title: Assistant Program Coordinator
jobs.description:
Description

JOB SUMMARY:

To provide administrative and secretarial support to the Program Director, the Program

Coordinators, the House Managers and the Staff of the Community Supports and Residential Programs .

General Clerical Duties

Provide administrative support for the Community Supports/Residential Programs to include telephone coverage, copier projects, mail handling, supplies control and necessary communications internally and externally via letters, email, and distribution of policies and procedures.
Assist the Program Coordinators in the management of their schedules and handle calls for these managers from both internal and external sources.
Set-up and maintain suspense “tickler” files to track activities with specific deadlines. Manage central files. Update as needed contact lists.
Purchase office supplies and equipment with the approval of the Program Director. Maintain documentation.
Provide administrative support for Monthly Meetings. Provide logistical support by assuring meeting room is prepared and set up appropriately for events and meetings. Attend, take notes, transcribe and distribute minutes of meetings.
Perform special projects as assigned by Program Coordinator or designee.

Core

Assist staff with missed punches (in or out, leave-time, or other questions)
Take care of staff inquiries and needs, as well as phone calls on payroll Monday
Screen and process incoming calls and questions from staff to PC on payroll Monday

Mileage

Do monthly log on mileage, oil change
DOA mileage & trip summary
Figure the billable mileage and turn in to finance
Contact staff for current insurance and DL
Notify PC if there is questionable mileage in a timely manner
Contact HM if mileage is not turned in, utilizing good customer service skills
Track mileage, making sure HM is turning it in on time and it is correct
Alert PC if there are issues with timeliness, neatness, etc….

Personal Money, Petty Cash, PEX Cards, Fuel Receipts

Check the personal money & petty cash to ensure all is correct
Alert PC if there are issues
Call HM’s if not turned in on time and alert PC if this is an ongoing issue

Responsibilities With Claremore Area Program Business

Courier for Claremore
Pick up client personal money and petty cash
Deliver client personal money and petty cash
Deliver pay stubs

General Job Duties

Enter on-line incident reports
Type up outcomes
Fax/scan DDS-5’s and get to PC for med changes
Assist OCA during staff training audit – can locate copies of in-services if needed
Assist staff when PC is out of office
Assist PC with checking all in-services, making sure all are current – especially new hires and change of staffing between houses
Order office supplies
Filing
Fax PT, nutrition, ETL and other professionals monthly paperwork
Handout random drug screenings
Help PC’s with quarterly summaries
All other duties as assigned.

Core Values

C
lient Centered Care:
We are committed to making all decisions and performing all actions with the well-being of our clients as our primary focus, and to always putting our clients’ interests ahead of our own.

A
ccountability:
We take personal responsibility, as an organization and as individuals, for our actions, hold each other accountable, and expect to be held accountable at any point throughout the year for our decisions and actions.

R
espect:
While keeping the focus on the needs of our clients, we also commit to demonstrating mutual respect in communication and actions so that a positive environment for everyone is maintained.

E
xcellence:
We are committed to using established best practices, to evaluating effectiveness of our efforts, and to demonstrating a willingness to change current practices at any point based on evidence that change is needed.

Requirements

EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE:

Associates degree or equivalent relevant experience. Demonstrated proficiency in word processing, spreadsheets, and effective oral and written communication.
Will need to be a organized, possess excellent computer skills and people skills. Previous HOH experience preferred.
