jobs.title: Senior Mine Resource Geologist
jobs.description:
Faraday Copper is a growth oriented Canadian junior mining company focused on responsibly advancing our flagship development stage project in Arizona, through our subsidiary company, Redhawk Copper. With a highly energetic and collaborative team, we are committed to building a modern mining operation grounded in operational excellence, environmental stewardship, and strong community partnerships.

Faraday recently announced it has entered a letter of intent to acquire BHP’s San Manuel property in Arizona, creating the potential for a multi-asset copper district in the USA. As we transition from exploration to project development, we are seeking a strategic and hands-on Senior Mine Resource Geologist to build and lead resource geology in Arizona.

The Senior Mine Resource Geologist is responsible for all aspects of mineral resource estimation, geological modeling, database integrity, QA/QC program management, and resource reporting in support of exploration, project evaluation, feasibility study advancement, and mine planning activities at Redhawk Copper's San Manuel-Copper Creek combined project. Working closely with exploration, engineering, and technical services teams, this role provides primary technical leadership in resource geology, directs the work of independent QP consultants, ensures compliance with NI 43-101 and applicable reporting standards, and applies rigorous geological and geostatistical methods to develop reliable, auditable resource models that support strategic and investment-grade decision making. The position plays a central role in advancing the project through prefeasibility and feasibility stages and will mentor junior and site geological staff as the team grows.

No recruiting agencies.

Primary Responsibilities

Develop, maintain, and update 3D geological domain models and resource block models using Leapfrog Geo and geostatistical estimation platforms, ensuring models are current, auditable, and fit for feasibility-level decision making. Direct the technical scope and review deliverables of independent Qualified Person consultants for mineral resource estimations prepared in accordance with NI 43-101 and, where applicable, SEC S-K 1300 reporting standards. Interpret geological, geochemical, and geophysical data to support resource classification and mine planning
Integrate geometallurgical domains into resource models to support process design, recovery forecasting, and mine planning for both sulfide flotation and leach amenable material.
Contribute resource model inputs to prefeasibility and feasibility study work, including resource classification confidence upgrades through targeted infill and condemnation drilling. Conduct data validation, direct QA/QC procedures, ensure database integrity, and perform timely model reconciliation
Collaborate with mine planning engineers to optimize mine designs and production schedules
Develop and implement resource reconciliation programs to track model performance against actual production and grade control data as the project advances toward operations.
Provide technical guidance and mentorship to mine geologists and technicians
Prepare technical reports, documentation, and presentations for internal and external stakeholders
Manage resource classification methodology, including variogram modeling, estimation validation, and confidence category assignments consistent with NI 43-101 category definitions.
Develop resource models in support of corporate M&A work, due diligence studies, and other corporate business development efforts.

Physical Demands

Ability to understand, follow, and communicate work assignments, procedures, and safety requirements in English. Must be able to frequently sit, stand, walk, and lift or carry up to 10 pounds.
Redhawk Copper Inc. promotes a drug/alcohol free work environment with mandatory employment drug testing and ongoing random drug testing as per applicable State Laws

Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Geology, Geological Engineering, or a related field,
Minimum 8 years of experience in mining geology, with a strong focus on resource estimation
Professional designation (e.g., P.Geo, SME-RM, CPG)) required or eligibility
Proficiency in Leapfrog Geo for 3D geological and resource modeling, combined with demonstrated competency in at least one geostatistical estimation platform for variography, estimation, and model validation. Strong understanding of geostatistics and estimation techniques
Direct experience in resource estimation on porphyry copper deposits, with demonstrated understanding of deposit-scale geological controls, alteration zoning, and their influence on domain definition and grade continuity. Experience with both open-pit and underground mining techniques
Demonstrated experience designing, managing, and interpreting resource definition and infill drilling programs, including drill targeting, collar surveying, logging standards, and integration of results into updated resource models.
Proficiency in geological database management using AcQuire or equivalent platform, including QAQC protocol design, data validation workflows, and compliance with corporate database standards. Experience contributing resource model inputs to PEA, PFS, or feasibility study technical reports at a development stage project.
Leadership and a desire to mentor and develop site staff
Ability to work independently and in a dynamic, collaborative, progressive, and high-performing team
Must be legally authorized to work in the United States.

What We Offer

Competitive salary commensurate with experience.
Bonus eligibility.
Comprehensive benefits package including medical, dental, vision, life insurance, disability coverage, and paid time off.
Redhawk Copper, Inc. is an equal opportunity employer

jobs.title: Entry-Level Field Technician - Construction Materials Testing
jobs.description:
Job Description

Entry-Level Field Technician - Construction Materials Testing – San Antonio, Texas

Intertek, a leading provider of quality and safety solutions to many of the world’s top-recognized brands and companies, is actively seeking an Entry-Level Field Technician I
to join our Professional Services Industries, Inc. (Intertek-PSI) team in San Antonio, Texas
. This is a fantastic opportunity to grow a versatile career in the architectural, engineering, and construction (A/E/C) industry.

Professional Service Industries, Inc. (Intertek-PSI) is a leading US based provider of construction assurance, testing and inspection in civil and commercial construction. Our broad service offering includes construction materials testing, geotechnical services, environmental engineering, industrial hygiene, and specialty testing.

What are we looking for?

The Field Technician
is responsible for performing a variety of testing, project specific observations and site assessment duties under general supervision and/or from detailed controlled procedures.

In addition to competitive compensation packages, when working with Intertek you can expect benefits including medical, dental, vision, life, disability, 401(k) with company match, generous vacation / sick time (PTO), tuition reimbursement and more.

What you’ll do:

Operate testing equipment and conduct testing (example: Soil, concrete or other) and provide assessment of data through reporting
Utilize drawings, specifications, and diagrams
Use specific methods and perform tasks to make detailed observations of site activities and give limited interpretation of results
Maintain detailed documentation and data from test results

Salary & Benefits Information:

This position outline is a general guideline and does not represent all encompassing details. The position assumes that the incumbent has both the mental and physical requirements to carry out the above defined duties.

Minimum Requirements & Qualifications:

Valid driver’s license and reliable driving record
Must be able to work off shift and overtime as needed
Ability to lift, move, push and pull 30 to 50 pounds frequently; Occasionally, over 50 pounds with assistance
Ability to receive detailed information through oral communication, (hearing) and to make the discriminations in sound
Ability to kneel and squat occasionally
Ability to walk and stand for long periods of time
Ability to work outdoors in adverse weather conditions, including hot and cold temperatures
Ability to climb occasionally, and work at various heights
Ability to work over night hours and off shift

Preferred Requirements & Qualifications:

High School Diploma or equivalent
6 months of experience in construction materials / density testing
ACI Certification

Intertek: Total Quality. Assured.

Intertek is a world leader in the Quality Assurance market, with a proven, high-quality business model and a global network of customer-focused operations and highly engaged subject matter experts. With passion, pace, and precision we work to exceed our customers’ expectations, while engaging with our employees to be 10X in their performance and professional growth.

Intertek is a drug-free workplace. For certain safety-sensitive roles, a pre-employment drug screen will be required. Based on local law and legislation, marijuana may be included in that screening.

We Value Diversity

Intertek’s network of phenomenal people are our greatest assets, and the diversity they bring fuels our success. Intertek is an Equal Employment Opportunity Employer that values inclusion and diversity. We take affirmative action to ensure all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, veteran status, or other legally protected characteristics.

For individuals with disabilities who would like to request accommodation, or who need assistance applying, please email intertekhrusa@intertek.com or call 1-877-694-8543 (option #5) to speak with a member of the HR Department.

CA-ST
Intertek does not accept unsolicited approaches from agencies and will not pay a fee for any placement resulting from the receipt of an unsolicited resume.

About Us

Intertek is a leading Total Quality Assurance provider to industries worldwide. Our network of more than 1,000 laboratories and offices in more than 100 countries, delivers innovative and bespoke Assurance, Testing, Inspection and Certification (ATIC) solutions for our customers' operations and supply chains. Working at Intertek means joining a global network of state-of-the-art facilities and passionate people who deliver superior customer service with a purpose of bringing quality, safety, and sustainability to life.

About The Team

The Building & Construction team provides assurance, testing, inspection, and certification services and building science solutions to deliver Total Quality Assurance to the built environment. The team offers the architectural, engineering and construction (A/E/C) industry with a full suite of capabilities both in the lab and on site.

jobs.title: Cleared Solutions Engineer
jobs.description:
At ICEYE, we design, build and operate the largest fleet of Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) satellites in the world. Using advanced technology, our constellation collects topographical data about any location on Earth, day or night, through any weather conditions.

We are on a mission to improve the quality of data, the quality of decisions, and the quality of life on earth. Our growing satellite constellation provides access to timely and reliable radar imagery, day and night, in any weather, to help the environment, advance global infrastructure, manage natural resources, and resolve security and intelligence challenges. Our team is a tight-knit group of driven innovators that strive for excellence; we don't listen to those who say it can't be done, we do it!

Our journey is just beginning. Join us.

Role Description

We are looking for a SAR Solutions Engineer, with an active TS/SCI clearance, to join our Technical Sales Team. In this role you will enable ICEYE to win strategic opportunities by defining and leading tailored technical solutions, with a focus on complex government tenders.

As a SAR Solutions Engineer you will provide critical technical insight, analysis and guidance in collaboration with sales account managers, proposal managers, marketing and the broader ICEYE engineering, operations and product teams.

Responsibilities:

Provide tailored technical and product support to Sales, Marketing and Government Affairs teams
Support on-site customer visits to locations requiring SCIF access in support of our strategic government customers
Collaborate with ICEYE technical teams to understand, synergize, and explain our current and future capabilities and processes
Develop technical solutions using a broad range of tools and processes to include ArcPro, Socet GXP, Python, and other SAR related tools
Understand the science behind ICEYE's proprietary SAR satellites (basic instrument performance) + data products (data specifications + calibration techniques)
Clarify the complexity of SAR technology, products and services in meaningful terms to customers
Partner with ICEYE sales to define products and solutions that meet our customers’ needs, ensure bilateral understanding of key requirements
Develop new business proposals for data, capability, and system deliveries and the coordination of this activity with the respective stakeholders
Structure proposal responses from multiple inputs - including sales strategy and RFP requirements

Requirements

Must be US citizen with an active TS/SCI clearance
3+ years experience in a sale-engineering role or operated in a similar capacity
3+ years technical writing experience to include industry-standard technical proposal writing processes (i.e. Shipley)
Solid SAR data product and RADAR technology knowledge
Ability to synthesize technical requirements from sales & Customers into a coherent technical solution
High attention to technical details with the ability to write directly and succinctly
SAR data products and RADAR technology knowledge
Strong client-facing interpersonal skills, including a good temperament
Advanced verbal and written communications skills (technical fluency in English required).
Project management expertise across multiple, ongoing tasks requiring cross-functional, multi-time zone coordination
Intrinsic self-motivation in highly disparate environments juggling multiple assignments.
Background in advanced scripting for system administration, data ingestion, or automated reporting
Experience with APIs
Experience with cloud-native architectures
Proficiency with data analysis libraries and frameworks (e.g., Pandas, NumPy, Jupyter Notebooks) and visualization tools (e.g., Matplotlib, Tableau, Power BI)

Preferred Skills And Experience

General experience with digital signal processing and engineering physics
Engineering or science degree ideally paired with a research background
Experience or familiarity with GEOINT concepts

Pay Range And Compensation Package

The estimated base salary range for this role is $115,000 - $125,000 depending on experience
Other benefits include health coverage, flexible time off - plus extra fun perks!

Physical Requirements

This position primarily works in an office environment. It requires the ability to sit or stand for long periods of time and frequent walking. Daily use of a computer, phone, office equipment and other computing and digital devices is required. May be required to stand for extended periods when facilitating meetings or walking in the facilities. Some local travel may be necessary, so the ability to travel by plane, operate a motor vehicle and maintain a valid Driver’s license and/or effectively navigate public transportation is required. While performing the responsibilities of the job, the employee must be able to read and respond to interoffice communications as well as effectively participate in meetings. The employee is often required to sit and use their hands and fingers, to lift up to 50 lbs., pull, push, carry, handle or feel. The employee is required to carry, handle items, reach with arms and hands, to stoop, kneel, or crouch; talk or hear. Mental demands may require prolonged concentration, reading comprehension, understanding and interpretation of concepts, ideas and philosophies. The physical demands of the position described herein are essential functions of the job and employees must be able to successfully perform these tasks for extended periods. Reasonable accommodations may be made for those individuals with real or perceived disabilities to perform the essential functions of the job described unless such accommodations would cause ICEYE US an undue burden.

EEO Statement

ICEYE US is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, veteran status, age, or any other protected characteristic under federal, state, or local law.

jobs.title: PMO Solutions Engineer
jobs.description:
About The Role

The GSW PMO Solutions Engineer will act as an internal consultant, partnering with regional operations and operational support teams to diagnose operational inefficiencies and co-create solutions that fit their unique needs. The Engineer will support and extend the technical capabilities of the Process Improvement function by building, automating, and maintaining digital solutions that streamline operations. A core focus of this role is Joulz, the team’s proprietary Azure-native, multi-tenant field-workforce management platform, contributing across the Flutter/Dart client, the C#/.NET 10 API, and the supporting Azure Kubernetes (AKS) infrastructureThe Engineer will play a crucial role in turning complex processes into intuitive and streamlined solutions and will explore and implement emerging technologies, including AI and Large Language Models (LLMs).

Company Overview

Established in 1977, Great Southwestern Construction, Inc. (Great Southwestern) – a subsidiary of MYR Group Inc. – has successfully completed hundreds of transmission, distribution, substation, live line, and clean energy projects nationwide. We are well positioned as a contractor of choice for providing transmission, distribution, and substation construction, telecommunication systems, maintenance, and emergency restoration services. We utilize our well-established, long-standing reputation to create enduring relationships. We have built this reputation by meeting the needs of our clients and team members in a safe and innovative work atmosphere.

Our leadership team is second to none in providing support and opportunities for growth, with over half of our senior management team having been promoted within the company. We believe in creating a balanced work-life environment built on our culture and values of safety, integrity, respect, and initiative. With industry leading benefits and compensation, we showcase the true value of our craft workers and truly put our People First. Are you ready to grow?

Essential Functions

Collaborate with the PMO Solutions Lead to execute the PMO team's technical vision and automation strategy
Develop and maintain workflows and automations using Joulz, Power Automate, and other scripting or workflow tools to reduce manual, repetitive tasks
Assist in the design and development of integrations between key business systems, including our ERP (JD Edwards E1), SharePoint, Salesforce and Project Management Tools, to ensure seamless data flow

jobs.title: Data Analyst I
jobs.description:
Evaluates business requirements and performs basic data integration and analysis. Develops routine analytical and reporting tools utilizing integrated data. Provides technical support.

Med-Neurology

The University of Pittsburgh, Department of Neurology
, would like to hire a data analyst for projected related to real-world evidence for the effectiveness, safety, and tolerability of diagnostic and treatments related to seizures, as well as the analysis of clinical trials for seizures. Data Analyst is expected to be familiar with coding in R and, ideally, have familiarity with multivariable generalized linear models (e.g., logistic regression, survival models, log-normal regression, rank regression), propensity matching/inverse probability weighting, and other data wrangling techniques. Other required skills include the handling of private and protected health information, discussion of results and methods with other statistical and non-statistical people, creation of figures and tables. We will expect to train the analyst on the design and interpretation of these techniques within the electronic health record and other clinical data, as well as teach techniques of machine learning/artificial intelligence, large language models, and clinical trial methodology. Writing peer-reviewed manuscripts and presentations can be available to interested analysts.

Education is expected to be an undergraduate degree in a biostatistical-related field (e.g., statistics, biostatistics, bioinformatics, biomathematics, data science, computational neuroscience). Additional interest or background in neurology, including functional and/or epileptic seizures, would be a bonus.

The position focuses on the analysis of data; therefore, it’s expected to be almost entirely remote. There may be a single conference per year to attend in person (e.g., American Epilepsy Society in December). The rest of the group is 100% remote.

Essential Functions

Essential job functions

Coding multivariable regression in R
Basic knowledge of statistics, including null hypothesis testing, regression, multivariable regression
Microsoft office
Appropriate handling of protected health information

Virtual meetings (e.g., teams)

Physical Effort

typing, interactions with others in the group and external to the group, using computer processing.

jobs.title: Administrator Data Analyst
jobs.description:
Job Description:

The Role

We are seeking a highly skilled Data Analyst with hands-on Administrator experience and a strong foundation in data analytics. This role partners closely with business stakeholders to understand strategic and operational needs, translate those needs into clear system and data requirements, and deliver scalable, user-focused CRM solutions. Passionate about the strategic use of data to drive business results. The ideal candidate thrives in a fast-paced, agile environment and brings a customer-centric mindset to every decision.

The Expertise and Skills You Bring

Bachelor's or equivalent.
Salesforce.com Administrator certification desired.
2+ years of application or production support experience.
3+ years of relevant experience applying systems analysis methodologies, including requirements definition, data analysis, and data mapping.
Proven ability to partner with business and technology teams to analyze problems and design scalable, sustainable solutions.
Strong passion for leveraging data strategically to drive business outcomes.
Salesforce Expertise
Hands-on experience administering Salesforce using declarative tools.
End-to-end ownership of Salesforce data hygiene, including quality, and governance.
Experience significantly contributing to large-scale Salesforce data hygiene initiatives.
Advocate and champion for Salesforce data management best practices.
Data Analytics & Data Management
Strong data analytics background with experience analyzing large datasets.
Deep curiosity and capability to follow data flows and understand complex data relationships.
Ability to communicate analytical findings clearly and provide actionable, data-driven recommendations.
Data quality expertise, including identifying issues and designing solutions using Salesforce features such as duplicate rules, matching rules, validation rules, and automation.
Data governance experience, partnering with business and technology leadership to define data ownership, stewardship, and governance models.
Development and implementation of Salesforce data hygiene metrics and standards.
Root cause analysis skills to identify behavioral, process, system, or integration drivers of poor data quality and recommend improvements to business processes.
Systems-thinking approach to assess integrations and upstream/downstream impacts.
Familiarity with enterprise data ecosystems and tools such as ETL processes, data lakes/warehouses, and analytics platforms (e.g., Power BI).
Experience exporting, cleansing, and re-importing Salesforce data using SOQL, Salesforce Data Loader (or similar tools), and MS Excel (e.g., PivotTables, Conditional Formatting, VLOOKUP).
Technical Skills
Strong understanding of relational database concepts and data modeling.
Experience with Snowflake or similar cloud data platforms.
Experience working with large datasets, including structured and semi-structured data (e.g., JSON files).
Proficiency in MS Excel for data analysis and validation.
Communication & Collaboration
Excellent written and verbal communication skills with a strong emphasis on active listening.
Ability to create clear, thorough documentation, including solution designs, knowledge base articles, and step-by-step user guides.
Comfortable collaborating with business users, technology partners, and leadership.
Organization & Prioritization
Strong ability to multitask in a fast-paced, agile environment.
Proven ability to prioritize work based on business impact, urgency, and SLAs.
Track record of delivering results while maintaining positive working relationships.
Demonstrated initiative, intellectual curiosity, and a continuous learning mindset.

Dexian stands at the forefront of Talent + Technology solutions with a presence spanning more than 70 locations worldwide and a team exceeding 10,000 professionals. As one of the largest technology and professional staffing companies and one of the largest minority-owned staffing companies in the United States, Dexian combines over 30 years of industry expertise with cutting-edge technologies to deliver comprehensive global services and support.  

Dexian connects the right talent and the right technology with the right organizations to deliver trajectory-changing results that help everyone achieve their ambitions and goals. To learn more, please visit https://dexian.com/.

Role
– Business Data Analyst
Location
– Jersey City, NJ (3-4 days reporting in office)
Type
– Both Contract and FTE ( Only W2 or Fulltime candidate )

Job Description -
Job Summary:

We are seeking a Business Data Analyst
with a strong background in large-scale data applications, data analysis, and transformation to support enterprise-level initiatives in the Property & Casualty (P&C) Insurance
domain. The ideal candidate will bring hands-on expertise in SQL, data mapping, and business analysis, along with deep domain knowledge of policy and claims data. This role requires close collaboration with business stakeholders, SMEs, and technical teams to drive data accuracy, consistency, and value.

Key Responsibilities:

Perform end-to-end data analysis, including data profiling, validation, and transformation
for large-scale data integration and reporting projects.
Develop source-to-target data mappings (STTM)
and documentation based on business requirements.
Collaborate with business users and stakeholders to gather, analyze, and translate functional requirements
into technical specifications.
Work with data engineers and developers to implement and validate data consolidation, standardization, and transformation logic.
Optimize complex SQL queries for performance and accuracy in handling large datasets.
Participate in data quality assessments and recommend data cleansing or enrichment strategies.
Leverage domain knowledge of Property & Casualty Insurance
—including policy administration and claims lifecycle—to ensure data alignment with business processes.
Facilitate communication between business SMEs, data modelers, and technical teams for successful data delivery and adoption.

Required Skills & Experience:

5+ years
of hands-on experience in data analysis, data mapping, and data transformation within enterprise data environments.
Strong proficiency in SQL
, including analytical functions, query optimization, and performance tuning.
Proven experience developing source-to-target mapping (STTM)
documents and understanding ETL/data flow processes.
Exposure to data consolidation, standardization
, and transformation across heterogeneous data sources.
In-depth knowledge of the Property & Casualty Insurance domain
,
Exceptional communication and interpersonal skills
—able to work effectively with technical teams, business stakeholders, and subject matter experts.

Strong documentation skills with the ability to create clear and concise technical and business artifacts.

jobs.description:
QXO, Inc. (NYSE: QXO) is the largest publicly traded distributor of roofing, waterproofing, and related products, and the second largest publicly traded distributor of lumber and building materials in North America. QXO is the fastest growing company in the $800 billion building products distribution industry and plans to become the tech-enabled leader by delivering best-in-class customer satisfaction and outsized returns for its shareholders. The company is targeting $50 billion in annual revenues within the next decade through accretive acquisitions and organic growth.

The Project Coordinator supports the planning, coordination, and execution of projects from start to finish. This role works closely with internal teams, customers, and stakeholders to ensure projects are delivered on time, within scope, and in alignment with company standards. The ideal candidate is organized, detail-oriented, and able to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.

What You'll Do

Support project lifecycle activities from initiation through completion
Review project details, specifications, and requirements to ensure accuracy
Coordinate schedules, materials, and resources to support project timelines

Ensure accuracy of data and maintain organized project records
Assist with creating, updating, and managing project documentation

Coordinate meetings and facilitate communication to ensure alignment across stakeholders
Address project issues and escalate as needed
Identify and resolve project-related challenges or delays
Support continuous improvement of processes and workflows
Assist with training or onboarding of team members as needed
Ensure adherence to company policies, procedures, and quality standards
Support safe work practices and report any concerns
Perform additional duties and special projects as assigned

What You'll Bring

Strong organizational and time management skills
Excellent communication and interpersonal abilities
Attention to detail and ability to manage multiple tasks simultaneously
Problem-solving skills and ability to adapt in a dynamic environment
Proficiency with business software tools
Experience in project coordination, operations, or administrative roles

Bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience
Primarily office-based with collaboration across departments
Ability to sit, stand, and work at a computer for extended periods

Health Care Benefits
Paid Time Off

jobs.title: Program Coordinator
jobs.description:
Company Description
ASSURE (Alliance for System Safety of UAS through Research Excellence) is a global coalition of more than thirty leading research institutions and over one hundred industry and government partners focused on unmanned aircraft systems (UAS) safety. As the FAA Center of Excellence for Unmanned Aircraft Systems, ASSURE provides specialized expertise, advanced infrastructure, and proven research capabilities to support safe UAS integration into the national airspace. Member organizations include major universities across the United States and internationally, reflecting a broad and collaborative research network. ASSURE’s work is grounded in applied research, real-world testing, and close partnerships with regulatory bodies and industry leaders. The organization offers a mission-driven environment for professionals interested in advancing aviation safety and UAS innovation. 
Role Description
The Program Coordinator is a full-time, on-site role based in Starkville, MS, supporting the planning, execution, and monitoring of ASSURE programs and projects. This position coordinates schedules, meetings, and deliverables across partner institutions, industry collaborators, and internal teams, ensuring milestones are met and documentation is accurate and up to date. The Program Coordinator assists the Program Managers with reporting, budget tracking, and compliance requirements, while maintaining organized records of contracts, agreements, and project outcomes. Day-to-day responsibilities include responding to stakeholder inquiries, preparing presentations and status updates, and supporting events, workshops, and site visits. The role also involves maintaining effective communication channels, identifying potential issues or risks, and collaborating with leadership to improve processes and program outcomes. 
Qualifications

Bachelor of Science in Business Administration, Management, Engineering, or other technical field.  
Three (3) years of related experience in project coordination/management.  
Demonstrated skills in program management and program development, including coordinating multiple initiatives, tracking progress, and supporting long-term program goals. 
Strong project management capabilities, such as organizing tasks, managing timelines, and aligning resources to meet project milestones. 
Excellent communication and customer service skills, with the ability to interact professionally with diverse stakeholders in higher education, industry, and government. 
Proficiency with productivity and collaboration tools (e.g., MS Office, project management software) and the ability to prepare clear reports and presentations. 
Strong organizational skills, attention to detail, and the ability to manage competing priorities in a fast-paced environment. 
Experience working in research, higher education, aerospace, or UAS-related programs is preferred but not required.

jobs.title: Project Coordinator
jobs.description:
Job Title: Project Coordinator

Job Category: Project and Program Management

Time Type: Full time

Minimum Clearance Required to Start: TS/SCI

Employee Type: Regular

Percentage of Travel Required: Up to 25%

Type of Travel: Local

* * *

The Opportunity

In support of the Joint Navigation Warfare Center (JNWC) contract, you will be a part of our team’s efforts to empower the Department of Defense’s navigation warfare (NAVWAR) mission ensuring positioning, navigation and timing superiority. Our mission is to provide combatant commanders and joint force commanders with subject matter expertise on planning and conducting NAVWAR by providing knowledge of friendly and enemy NAVWAR Order of Battle and PNT-related successes and threats. 

This position is located in Albuquerque, New Mexico and requires an active TS/SCI Clearance.

Responsibilities

Primary POC to the JNWC Director and Division Leadership for all support programs to include but not limited to task management, DDR, LeaveWeb, updates via MilPDS, PROA, tracking/execution of military evals in myEval, CMS case management, military personnel awards via myDec, myFSS, various roster management in BLSDM, TMT Tasker Management for various S4S awards; assists in oversight of organization manpower resource requirements.
Provide oversight and troubleshooting for the organization's travel and logistics operations, acting as a primary coordinator for the Defense Travel System (DTS) and Government Travel Charge Card (GTCC) programs.
Supports the analysis and development of strategy, policy and doctrine to support JNWC efforts;
Routinely coordinates with the PNT S&T community of interest (CoI) to include OUSD/R&E, Joint StaffJ7/J8, OUSD/DOT&E, Army PEO PNT, laboratories, academia, civil and commercial entities;
Provides input to JNWC strategic plans for future growth, organization and processes;
Provides support to the creation of mid-long term military PNT capability, analysis, and identification of S&T technology in support of JNWC modernization efforts and enhanced mission capability;
Provides support to PNT initial document construction and data collection.
Prepares, routes, and reviews all documents as required for JNWC assessments
Support Operational Capability Demonstrations (ODCs), to include analysis of emerging and relevant NAVWAR technologies and preparation of assessments

Qualifications

BA/BS or equivalent experience.
Experience serving as primary coordinator for the Defense Travel System (DTS) and Government Travel Charge Card (GTCC) programs.
At least 5 years of relevant experience with program operations with a government contractor.
Ability to work under tight time constraints with little to no oversight.
Must have an active TS/SCI Clearance.

What You Can Expect

A culture of integrity.

At CACI, we place character and innovation at the center of everything we do. As a valued team member, you’ll be part of a high-performing group dedicated to our customer’s missions and driven by a higher purpose – to ensure the safety of our nation.

An environment of trust.

CACI values the unique contributions that every employee brings to our company and our customers - every day. You’ll have the autonomy to take the time you need through a unique flexible time off benefit and have access to robust learning resources to make your ambitions a reality.

A focus on continuous growth.

Together, we will advance our nation's most critical missions, build on our lengthy track record of business success, and find opportunities to break new ground — in your career and in our legacy.

Pay Range

There are a host of factors that can influence final salary including, but not limited to, geographic location, Federal Government contract labor categories and contract wage rates, relevant prior work experience, specific skills and competencies, education, and certifications. Our employees value the flexibility at CACI that allows them to balance quality work and their personal lives. We offer competitive compensation, benefits and learning and development opportunities. Our broad and competitive mix of benefits options is designed to support and protect employees and their families. At CACI, you will receive comprehensive benefits such as; healthcare, wellness, financial, retirement, family support, continuing education, and time off benefits.

The Proposed Salary Range For This Position Is

$65,000 - $136,500

CACI is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, pregnancy, sexual orientation, age, national origin, disability, status as a protected veteran, or any other protected characteristic.

jobs.title: Project Coordinator I
jobs.description:
Hybrid opportunity in Portland, OR; Medford, OR; Boise, ID; Salt Lake City, UT and Fargo, ND

Build a career with purpose. Join our Cause to create a person-focused and economically sustainable health care system.

Who We Are Looking For

Every day, Cambia’s dedicated team of Project Coordinators are living our mission to make health care easier and lives better. As a member of the Project Management team, our Project Coordinators support project management activities across all project phases, including scheduling, budgeting, status reporting, and meeting coordination for one or multiple projects or programs. This role may also independently plan and manage small projects or sub-projects with minimal supervision – all in service of making our members’ health journeys easier.

Do you have a passion for keeping projects organized and on track? Do you thrive in a collaborative environment where attention to detail and clear communication are essential? Then this role may be the perfect fit.

Qualifications

What You Bring to Cambia:

The Project Coordinator I would have a Bachelor’s degree in business, project management, healthcare, or related field and at least 1 year of experience in a project coordinator role, or equivalent combination of education and experience.

Skills And Attributes

Highly detail-oriented with excellent analysis and problem-solving skills
Experience with Microsoft Office suite and automated scheduling software
Ability to work effectively with minimal supervision
Proficiency with AI tools and technologies a plus
Demonstrated experience in project management methods, techniques, and development life cycle disciplines
Ability to manage small to moderately complex projects with minimal supervision
Highly detail-oriented with excellent analysis and problem-solving skills
Experience with Microsoft Office suite and automated scheduling software
Ability to work effectively with minimal supervision
Proficiency with AI tools and technologies a plus
Certificate or training in Project Management a plus

What You Will Do At Cambia

Supports project management activities in accordance with Enterprise Project Management Office policies and standards
Develops and maintains project plans, including schedules, budgets, and plans for quality, resources, communications, and risks
Monitors and controls project execution, tracking costs, resources, issues, changes, and risks
Prepares and presents status reports and project information to leadership, project teams, and client groups
Coordinates, facilitates, and leads project meetings effectively
Maintains project documentation library ensuring compliance with standards and proper retention in project databases
May plan and manage small, well-defined projects or sub-projects under general direction of senior project managers

Oregon, Utah, and Idaho: The expected hiring range is for the Project Coordinator I is $25.90 to $32.40. The full range is $25.90 - $42.20 and the bonus target is 10%. North Dakota $23.91 - $31.08 and the full range is $23.91 - $38.25 depending on skills, experience, education, and training; relevant licensure / certifications; and performance history.

About Cambia

Working at Cambia means being part of a purpose-driven, award-winning culture built on trust and innovation anchored in our 100+ year history. Our caring and supportive colleagues are some of the best and brightest in the industry, innovating together toward sustainable, person-focused health care. Whether we're helping members, lending a hand to a colleague or volunteering in our communities, our compassion, empathy and team spirit always shine through.

Why Join the Cambia Team?

At Cambia, You Can

Work alongside diverse teams building cutting-edge solutions to transform health care.
Earn a competitive salary and enjoy generous benefits while doing work that changes lives.
Grow your career with a company committed to helping you succeed.
Give back to your community by participating in Cambia-supported outreach programs.
Connect with colleagues who share similar interests and backgrounds through our employee resource groups.

We believe a career at Cambia is more than just a paycheck – and your compensation should be too. Our compensation package includes competitive base pay as well as a market-leading 401(k) with a significant company match, bonus opportunities and more.

Benefits

In exchange for helping members live healthy lives, we offer benefits that empower you to do the same. Just a few highlights include:

Medical, dental and vision coverage for employees and their eligible family members, including mental health benefits.
Annual employer contribution to a health savings account.
Generous paid time off varying by role and tenure in addition to 10 company-paid holidays.
Market-leading retirement plan including a company match on employee 401(k) contributions, with a potential discretionary contribution based on company performance (no vesting period).
Up to 12 weeks of paid parental time off (eligibility requires 12 months of continuous service with Cambia immediately preceding leave).
Award-winning wellness programs that reward you for participation.
Employee Assistance Fund for those in need.
Commute and parking benefits.

Learn More About Our Benefits.

We are happy to offer work from home options for most of our roles. To take advantage of this flexible option, we require employees to have a wired internet connection that is not satellite or cellular and internet service with a minimum upload speed of 5Mb and a minimum download speed of 10 Mb.

We are an Equal Opportunity employer dedicated to a drug and tobacco-free workplace. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, national origin, religion, age, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability, protected veteran status or any other status protected by law. A background check is required.

If you need accommodation for any part of the application process because of a medical condition or disability, please email CambiaCareers@cambiahealth.com. Information about how Cambia Health Solutions collects, uses, and discloses information is available in our Privacy Policy.

jobs.description:
Position Summary

The Project Coordinator is responsible for supporting the regional office general operational work by maintaining and monitoring project plans, project schedules, work hours, budgets, and expenditures to ensure that all projects are completed on time.

Essential Job Duties And Responsibilities

Work closely with regional leadership to prepare comprehensive action plans, including resources and timeframes.
Collaborate with clients and internal teams to deliver results on deadlines.
Organize, attend, and participate in management and or project meetings
Document and follow up on important actions and decisions from meetings.
Assume project tasks as required, determining project changes and ensuring project deadlines are met.
Develop rigging strategies and coordinating tasks, and disseminate to appropriate personnel.
Ensure projects adhere to frameworks and all documentation is maintained and communicated appropriately for each project.
Evaluate project risks, issues and provide solutions where applicable.
Ensure MEI Rigging & Crating’s visions & values are maintained and managed.
Assist with material sourcing and product procurement.
Perform all other tasks as directed and/or necessary.

Minimum Qualifications (Experience, Skills, And Education)

High School Diploma or Equivalent needed.
Background in construction, rigging, or a related field helpful.

Intermediate computer skills using a Windows-based system. Good knowledge of Microsoft applications including Word, Excel, and Outlook.
Auto Cad experience is a plus but not required.
Excellent team building and project coordinator skills.
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills with internal leadership team.
Analytical skills with the ability to explain data-driven technical information to all levels of staff.
Goal orientated and deadline driven.
Demonstrated ability to positively interface with clients.
Organized, detail oriented, punctual, and highly self-motivated.

Physical Requirements And Working Conditions

The Project Coordinator will primarily work in a normal temperature-controlled office environment with frequent work using a computer, keyboard, mouse, telephone, and other standard office equipment. Work involves extended periods of sitting and use of the senses involving sight, touch, speech, and hearing. Occasional walking, bending/crouching, and lifting and/or moving items weighing up to 10 pounds. May need to perform work outdoors with exposure to inclement weather and uneven terrain, or in non-temperature-controlled environments.

This job description is intended to summarize the type and level of work performed by a Project Coordinator and is not an exhaustive list of all duties, responsibilities, and/or requirements. jobs.title: Senior Project Coordinator
jobs.description:
Senior Project Coordinators manage the execution of assigned projects through vendor coordination, documentation control, and system management, operating at a level approaching Project Manager execution while delegating all field responsibilities to installation vendors and service providers. They possess strong knowledge of equipment specifications, project sequencing, and Avanti operational systems, allowing them to maintain tight control of project scope, procurement timing, and documentation while coordinating installation activities remotely.
Senior Project Coordinators are responsible for full project ownership from the office, ensuring projects remain organized, technically accurate, and aligned with contract requirements and schedule.

Duties/Responsibilities

Lead project execution from the office by coordinating vendors, documentation, and internal teams to ensure projects remain organized and aligned with contract requirements.
Develop project execution plans based on equipment sequencing and construction schedules. Coordinate those requirements with field teams / installers.
Maintain strong understanding of project drawings, specifications, and equipment scope to ensure equipment selections align with contract documents.
Perform detailed AutoQuotes (AQ) review and cleanup to ensure accuracy before procurement / submittal
Manage submittals, RFIs, and change order documentation while ensuring project information is properly tracked and maintained in Airtable.
Coordinate procurement sequencing with Supply Chain and review purchase orders to ensure equipment aligns with approved submittals, scope, and schedule
Coordinate installation vendors and service agents by communicating delivery schedules, installation sequencing, and startup requirements.
Support billing coordination with Project Administration by ensuring change orders, deliveries, and project milestones align with billing requirements.
Manage project closeout coordination including startup scheduling, punch list tracking, warranty documentation, and final turnover to the client.
All other duties as assigned.

Required Qualifications

Strong understanding of commercial kitchen equipment specifications and sequencing.
Ability to interpret construction drawings and project specifications.
Strong organizational and documentation management skills.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
Proficiency with project management and operational systems including Bluebeam, AutoQuotes.
Ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously while maintaining attention to detail.
Ability to coordinate vendors and project stakeholders effectively.

Preferred Qualifications

Prior experience in commercial kitchen construction, foodservice equipment distribution, or construction project coordination.
Advanced experience with AutoQuotes and equipment specification management.
Familiarity with construction documentation including submittals, RFIs, and change orders.
Experience coordinating installation vendors and service agents.
Demonstrated ability to manage projects independently with minimal oversight.

Education/Experience

5 years of experience in either KEC Project Management / Kitchen Equipment Sales.
High school diploma required, but prefer undergraduate degree.

About Cambia

Working at Cambia means being part of a purpose-driven, award-winning culture built on trust and innovation anchored in our 100+ year history. Why Join the Cambia Team?

At Cambia, You Can

Work alongside diverse teams building cutting-edge solutions to transform health care.

Commute and parking benefits.

jobs.title: Project Coordinator
jobs.description:
Department Overview

The mission of the Information Technology Group (ITG) is to develop, implement and maintain technology-based services and solutions enabling OHSU to effectively manage information to accomplish its missions.

This position coordinates and assists the Strategic Healthcare Projects group in the management of assignments within scope, time, cost and quality; partners with Strategic Healthcare Projects' customers to assist them in the project intake process; assists with project management tool evaluation, development and refinement; assists Managers & Project Managers with the development and tracking of various project efforts and documents; coordinates, facilitates and/or executes assignments for projects within ITG, OHSU and OHSU Health.

Function/Duties of Position

Project Coordination

Responsibilities

Works with project managers and/or manager to organize and coordinate project assignments. Independently manage project work, as assigned. May be assigned to monitor project plans, schedules, resources, risk management and organization. Participate in project meetings, developing and conducting requirements analysis. Responsibilities include, not limited to:

Develop and execute communications to project members and stakeholders.
Follow-up with project team members to keep project activities on track.
Assist project managers to track meeting minutes, action items, decisions, follow-up items, etc.
Work with customers to clearly communicate task status, goals, timelines, and project deliverables.
Other duties as assigned.

Continued Professional Development

Participate In Appropriate Activities That Enhance General Knowledge Of Or Ability To Do Assigned Work. This Includes, But Is Not Limited To, The Following

Attend classes, seminars, and other training forums.
Attend staff meetings and other informational sessions.
Read vendor documentation, trade publications, or other sources appropriate to position.
Interact with peers and other employees to receive and disseminate information.

Required Qualifications

Four years of experience as an executive assistant, administrative analyst, researcher or administrative officer which included administrative duties, provision of technical assistance, or operations review.
Two of the required years must have included administration or oversight of an on-going project or program.
A Bachelor's degree in Business Administration, Management, Public Administration or a closely related field may be substituted for up to two years of the required experience.
Note: there is no substitution for the two years of administrative experience.

Skills And Abilities

Ability to coordinate complex meetings, discussions & agendas.
Advanced MS Office suite skills, including Project, SharePoint, Visio, Excel, Teams, OneNote, and PowerPoint.
Able to collaborate with technical partners on complex and nuanced topics to identify and resolve issues.
Presents professionally in meetings and other venues with colleagues and all levels of management.
Able to successfully communicate & build positive relationships among customers, co-workers and vendors.
Demonstrates the following core competencies: Leading Self, Leading Others, Leading Leaders, Leading Function.

Preferred Qualifications

Experience in an IT environment.
Experience in an Academic Medical Center environment.
Project management methodology and terminology.
Business analysis experience within an IT environment.
Smartsheet experience.
CAPM, PMP, or other project management certification.

Benefits

Additional Details

Healthcare for full-time employees covered 100% and 88% for dependents.
$50K of term life insurance provided at no cost to the employee.
Two separate above market pension plans to choose from.
Vacation - up to 200 hours per year dependent on length of service.
Sick Leave - up to 96 hours per year.
9 paid holidays per year.
Substantial Tri-Met and C-Tran discounts.
Employee Assistance Program.
Childcare service discounts.
Tuition reimbursement.
Employee discounts to local and national businesses.

Why apply to OHSU?

We are Oregon's only public academic health center.

In addition to caring for patients, we lead groundbreaking research. We also train the next generation of health care professionals. As Portland's largest employer, we give you opportunities to learn and advance in a system of hospitals and clinics across Oregon and Southwest Washington.

All are welcome.

OHSU welcomes people of all ages, ethnicities, genders, national origins, religions and sexual orientations. We are striving to build an anti-racist, multicultural institution and encourage people with diverse backgrounds to apply.

To request reasonable accommodation, contact askhr@ohsu.edu

jobs.description:
Location: Atlanta, GA 30354 (On-site)

Duration: 12 Months with possible extension

Pay Rate: $33/hour on W2 all inclusive

Top 3 Skills:

Advance skills in MS Office, Excel, and PowerPoint required. At least 2-4 years of direct professional-level experience. Must possess time management skills and be able to multi-task and manage multiple projects in a fast-paced environment.

Responsibilities:

Will assist in all aspects of substance testing program administration. The position will rely on strong analytical and business skills to assist with the completion of data requests/submissions and reporting analytics throughout the year as well as the development of various program functions. The position will also assist with the administration of various STP program compliance requirements.
• Analyze various DOT and Non-DOT programs, including random, follow-up, return to duty, reasonable suspicion, and pre-employment.
• Ensure compliant administration of the DOT drug and alcohol programs, as well as adherence to Client’s company drug and alcohol policies.
• Analyze various STP functions, including drug and alcohol history checks for prior DOT-covered employers, including tracking progress and final status.
• Audit and process drug and alcohol testing history checks for new hires and transfers into roles requiring safety-sensitive duties.
• Oversee and manage the collection of required DOT paperwork for all safety-sensitive new hires and transfers and ensure that all complete a timely DOT drug screen with a negative/pass result, as applicable, as well as that all three FAA mandated forms have been completed before the start date.
• Research and respond to requests from other airlines for drug and alcohol testing records and ensure responses are DOT compliant.
• Fulfill data and analytics requests for key STP projects including the annual MIS report, monthly STP trend reports, and other ad hoc projects.
• Analyze resource utilization, using web-based resources, and other program management tools to improve operational effectiveness.
• Partner with key stakeholders to complete and maintain standard monthly reports regarding STP activity.
• Ensure the maintenance of accurate and concise records and reports concerning all phases of the DOT and Non-DOT drug and alcohol programs.
• Interact with service agents who provide drug and alcohol testing services to Client’s including completing needed research to address Client’s record requirements.
• Complete mandated quality checks on services performed by service agents and that will become a part of Client’s program.
• Assist with training and education sessions, as needed.
• Assist with the DOT pre-employment testing for GSC training candidates, including providing and collecting DOT required forms, conducting drug and alcohol testing history checks from prior DOT covered employers, and updating HR database with required safety-sensitive status indicators.
• Maintain technical knowledge to enhance process improvements utilizing LMS and ad hoc reporting.
• Practices safety-conscious behaviors in all operational processes and procedures.

jobs.title: Program Coordinator
jobs.description:
Groove With Me is a free dance school for girls in Harlem, built on the belief that every child deserves access to excellent arts education. Our volunteer dance teachers, dedicated staff, and engaged parent community work together to create a joyful, rigorous, and inclusive program. We hold ourselves to high standards of customer service, equal inclusion, and mission-driven action.

We are looking for an organized, mission-driven Program Coordinator to help manage the day-to-day and seasonal operations of Groove With Me. This role sits at the intersection of student programming, community partnerships, and organizational operations. The ideal candidate is proactive, detail-oriented, and genuinely excited about expanding dance opportunities for girls in Harlem.

Programming & Performances

•        Actively seek out dance-related performance opportunities in the Harlem community and beyond, keeping the mission of equal participation central
•        Manage and coordinate all external performance opportunities, including logistics, communication with partner organizations, and student preparation
•        Co-lead preparation for the annual recital, including scheduling, rehearsals, and family communications
•        Co-lead preparation for summer camp programming, including planning, staffing, and enrollment
•        Sub dance classes as needed to ensure program continuity
After-School Programs

•        Oversee the day-to-day management of after-school programs, ensuring smooth operations and a positive experience for students and families
•        Serve as a point of contact for families and partner schools regarding after-school programming
Scholarships

•        Administer the dance school scholarship program, including application processes, eligibility review, and award communications
•        Administer camp scholarship applications and awards, ensuring equitable access for all families
Interns & Operations

•        Oversee interns, including onboarding, task assignment, supervision, and feedback
•        Maintain organization of the storage room, ensuring supplies and materials are accessible and well-managed
•        Take initiative to identify and resolve operational issues — if something needs fixing, either address it directly or ensure the right person is called in

What we're looking for
•        Commitment to Groove With Me's mission and values, including equal inclusion of all students
•        Strong organizational skills and ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously
•        Excellent communication skills with families, volunteers, partners, and students
•        A proactive mindset — you notice what needs to be done and do it
•        Experience working with children or youth programs preferred
•        Background or genuine interest in dance, arts education, or performing arts a plus
•        Ability to work some evenings and weekends for performances and events
What We Offer •        The opportunity to make a direct impact on the lives of girls in Harlem
•        A close-knit, mission-driven team of staff, volunteers, and families
•        Meaningful work at the intersection of arts, education, and community
How to Apply Please send a resume and a brief note about why this role resonates with you to jobs@groovewithme.org .  Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis.

Groove With Me is committed to equity and inclusion. We encourage candidates of all backgrounds to apply.

jobs.description:
The Fountain Group
is a national staffing firm and we are currently seeking a --------Project Coordinator II
for a prominent client of ours. This position is in Lake County, IL 60064.
Details for the position are as follows:

Project Coordinator II

Location:
Lake County, IL (Hybrid)

Duration:
Long-Term Contract

Pay Rate:
$30-$32/hr

Position Overview

Our client is seeking a Project Coordinator II to support the Operational Excellence team within Integrated Discovery Operations. This role will primarily focus on scientific publication coordination and compliance activities, while also supporting operational processes related to vendor payments, sponsorships, memberships, and training compliance.

The ideal candidate will have strong project coordination, document management, compliance, and database administration experience within a pharmaceutical, biotechnology, research, or highly regulated environment. This individual must be highly organized, detail-oriented, and capable of managing multiple priorities while collaborating with cross-functional stakeholders.

Key Responsibilities

Scientific Publications Coordination

Perform quality control reviews of scientific publications, manuscripts, abstracts, posters, and related documentation to ensure compliance with publication policies, intellectual property requirements, and submission standards.
Serve as the primary coordinator for publication requests, tracking publications from initiation through final approval.
Monitor publication timelines and proactively follow up with publication leads, legal reviewers, leadership, and other stakeholders to ensure deliverables are completed on schedule.
Partner with publication authors and contributors to collect, organize, and file required documentation within designated systems.
Maintain accurate publication records and ensure adherence to internal compliance and intellectual property review processes.
Support publication workflow management and provide status updates to key stakeholders.

Operational Excellence & Compliance Support

Manage operational compliance processes related to sponsorships, exhibits, memberships, and vendor payment requests.
Support established processes for maintaining R&D Discovery compliance training records and databases.
Assist with tracking, reporting, and maintaining compliance documentation across multiple operational functions.
Identify discrepancies, exceptions, and process issues within databases and documentation records and coordinate resolution efforts.
Maintain confidentiality and ensure compliance with all corporate policies, procedures, and regulatory requirements.

Required Qualifications

Bachelor's degree preferred or equivalent professional experience.
3-5 years of project coordination, operations, compliance, publications, scientific communications, or administrative support experience.
Experience managing documentation, workflows, databases, and tracking systems.
Strong attention to detail with the ability to perform quality control reviews and identify inconsistencies.
Excellent organizational, prioritization, and follow-up skills.
Ability to work independently while managing multiple deadlines and deliverables.
Strong written and verbal communication skills with the ability to interact effectively across all levels of an organization.
Proficiency with Microsoft Office Suite, including Excel, Word, Outlook, and PowerPoint.

Preferred Qualifications

Experience supporting scientific publications, medical affairs, research operations, or scientific communications.
Pharmaceutical, biotechnology, life sciences, healthcare, or research industry experience.
Experience supporting compliance, intellectual property review processes, or regulated documentation environments.
Familiarity with vendor payment processes, sponsorship management, and training compliance administration.
Experience working with document management systems, publication tracking tools, or compliance databases.

jobs.title: Foster Care Program Coordinator II - Pinal County, AZ (Intermountain Foster Care)
jobs.description:
Looking to build a lasting career? Join a team that is inclusive and embraces all individuals. Intermountain Centers is one of the largest statewide behavioral health and integrated care organizations in Arizona. What does building a lasting career look like?

Top-level compensation packages
Exceptional health, dental, and disability benefits
Career and compensation advancement programs
Student loan forgiveness programs
401k company match
Bilingual pay differential
Holiday, PTO and employer paid life insurance
Clinical licensure supervision and reimbursement
Evidence-based treatment approaches, training, and supervision.

Intermountain Centers and its statewide affiliates are currently recruiting career-minded individuals interested in opportunities within the largest adult and child service continuum in Arizona.

General Summary
: The Foster Care Program Coordinator II is an advancement of Foster Care Program Coordinator I. The position supports members and foster homes that require more therapeutic interventions in Therapeutic, Specialized and CDH foster homes ensuring safety, compliance and stability within a foster home environment. This role is critical in supporting the well-being of children/youth in foster care and ensuring that foster families are equipped to provide a nurturing and safe home.

Overview Of Primary Responsibilities

Home Visits:

In coordination with treatment team and foster family, monitor and record behavior problems identified in the home, school and/or community.
Provide guidance and support to foster parents on effective strategies to address behavior problems identified.
Document progress and response to interventions.
In coordination with treatment team refer foster child and/or family to behavioral health outpatient services.
Reports critical incidents within contractual time frames to Supervisor.
Document all required documentation (service planning, interventions and incidents) in the EHR.

Supervision Of Foster Parents

During home visit assess the living environment, safety issues, and ensure adherence to foster care regulations and organization goals.
Document all home visits and contacts in the EHR/OLR/DCS.
Train and assist foster parents in providing treatment strategies addressing member concerns/needs.
Train and assist foster parents on using provider portal to upload required documents.
Offer training and resources to foster parents, enhancing their skills in providing care and support to foster children.
Evaluate foster parent performance and provide constructive feedback.

Safety And Compliance

Ensure that all foster homes comply with state and federal regulations regarding safety, health, and welfare standards.
Assist foster parents in reporting required progress notes and other documents required.
Assist in re-licensure process of foster parents collecting and/or guiding foster parents through the process.
Respond promptly to any safety concerns or incidents, taking necessary actions to protect children in care.
Address immediate licensing violations to foster parent.

Collaboration And Communication

Work closely with treatment team, therapists, and other stakeholders to ensure a holistic approach to care.
Works with Supervisor/Director and Foster Care Compliance Manager assisting in re-licensure and maintenance of training records.
Participates in supervision with Supervisor and other team members providing updates on foster home.
Participate in team meetings and maintain professional relationships with internal and external stakeholders.

Other

Promote awareness of foster care needs and assist in the recruit of potential foster families as needed.
Performs other duties as assigned and in relation to foster care program.

Qualifications And Experience

Minimum Education Required: High School Diploma or Equivalent.
Preferred Education: Bachelors degree preferred
Minimum Experience Required: Minimum of 3-years’ experience working in foster care system and/or behavioral health care coordination.
BH Level: BHT

Company/Regulatory Requirements

Minimum 21years of age.
Able to pass a minimum 5 panel drug test & submit to regular screening.
DPS Level I fingerprint clearance (must possess upon hire and maintain throughout employment).
Maintain a clear Central Registry Background Check.
CPR, First Aid, (must possess upon hire and maintain throughout employment).
Current, valid Arizona Driver's License and 39-month Motor Vehicle Report and proof of vehicle registration liability insurance to meet insurance requirements

Questions about this position? Contact us at HR@ichd.net

Intermountain Centers is an equal opportunity employer. Intermountain Centers does not discriminate based on age, ethnicity, race, sex, gender, religion, national origin, creed, tribal affiliation, ancestry, gender identity, sexual orientation, marital status, genetic information, veteran status, socio-economic status, claims experience, medical history, physical or intellectual disability, ability to pay, source of payment, mental illness, and/or cultural and linguistic needs, as well as any other class protected by law.

in geotechnical or engineering geology

license

·        Experience with geologic mapping, fault study, seismicity

license or ability to obtain it.

jobs.title: Geochemist/Geologist/Environmental Engineer
jobs.description:
Job Type

Full-time

Description

About You

You are a skilled environmental scientist or engineer with extensive experience in the analysis of chemical fate and transport in the environment. You are a strategic thinker who thrives on the challenges of complex, interdisciplinary environment matters. You are experienced in conceptual site model development, data evaluation, groundwater and contaminant transport, forensics evaluations, allocation concepts, litigation support, and client communication .
A lifelong learner, you want to keep growing intellectually and professionally. You know how to prepare winning proposals and exceed clients’ expectations. Collaborating with colleagues with diverse expertise is exciting for you, and you are also comfortable working independently. People thrive under your coaching. Quality management and integrity are among your strongest principles.

About Integral

Integral Consulting Inc. is a science and engineering firm delivering sound technical solutions to meet today’s challenges in the health, environmental, and natural resource arenas. We are a values-driven collaborative team that is as passionate about company culture as we are about the work we do for our clients. Since our founding in 2002, we have operated with a demonstrated commitment to the well-being, diversity, and professional development of our staff and an equal commitment to strong partnerships with our clients and teaming partners. We are a company where people come to solve today’s toughest environmental challenges through innovative ideas, exceptional teamwork, and dedication to accuracy and technical quality.

About The Job

We are currently seeking a mid-to senior-level scientist or engineer for our Investigation and Remediation business area. The position will support multiple clients and projects, with the common theme of characterizing complex contaminated sites. The day-to-day work will vary and may involve data analysis, historical document review and synthesis, client presentations, developing scopes and budgets, field work, and writing. Career growth at Integral offers future opportunities to manage projects, develop technical strategy, increase client communications, and business development. Most assignments will require teamwork, though a few will be entirely your responsibility.

This position may offer in-office, remote, or hybrid opportunities based on the candidate’s location and the needs of the organization.

Requirements

Responsibilities

As a Geochemist, your job functions will include, but not be limited to:

Evaluating and synthesizing environmental data from multiple media to develop site conceptual models
Performing fate and transport and/or geochemical evaluations, potentially including modeling
Supporting litigation projects, potentially including allocation, forensics, PFAs, or emerging contaminants
Conducting project and task management
Communicating technical information to varied audiences (e.g., clients, staff, regulators, and stakeholders)
Writing technical reports

Qualifications

Candidates should hold an advanced degree in geochemistry, geology, hydrogeology, environmental or civil engineering, or similar field.

The Ideal Candidate Will Have

5+ years of professional experience
Strong quantitative and problem-solving skills
Demonstrated proficiency in geochemical evaluations
Excellent written and oral communication skills
A desire to grow both intellectually and professionally
Possess strong organizational abilities
Ability to work independently and within a multidisciplinary team of scientists and engineers

Compensation is commensurate with experience and internal equity, within the listed range. An employee’s total compensation package includes annual salary; company-subsidized (employee and family) medical, dental, and vision insurance; 401(k) participation with a company match of 1% to 4% of annual compensation; company-paid life and short- and long-term disability insurance; paid sick leave (10 days); paid vacation (15 days); paid holidays (9 days) per year, and the potential for an annual discretionary bonus based on company performance. Paid time off will be prorated to reflect part-time scheduled hours, if applicable.

Integral Consulting Inc. is proud to be an equal employment opportunity employer who provides equal opportunity in all of our employment practices to all qualified employees and applicants without regard to race, color, religion, creed, gender, pregnancy (including childbirth or related medical conditions), national origin or ancestry, age, sexual orientation, disability, marital status, military, veteran, or Vietnam Era Veterans’ Readjustment Assistance Act protected veteran status, gender identity or gender expression, genetic information (including testing or characteristics), or any other category protected by federal, state, and local laws. We believe that a diverse and inclusive environment drives innovation and excellence, and we are dedicated to building a team that reflects the diverse communities we live in and serve. If you require reasonable accommodations during the application or interview process, please contact us at integraljobs@integral-corp.com.

We are excited to connect with individuals who share our mission and values, and those who bring diverse contributions to our team. We value unique perspectives and skills, and encourage you to apply, even if you don’t meet every requirement.

All offers of employment are contingent on candidates successfully passing an employment history screening, degree verification, and a federal, state, and county criminal background and Watch List screening. Candidates will receive a written notice of this requirement. All screenings will be done in accordance with all local, state, and federal laws.

Senior

Salary Description
