jobs.title: Data Analyst
jobs.description:
About The Company

My3Tech is a leading technology solutions provider dedicated to delivering innovative and reliable IT services to a diverse range of clients. With a focus on cutting-edge technology and customer-centric approaches, we strive to empower businesses by providing tailored solutions that enhance efficiency, security, and growth. Our team of highly skilled professionals is committed to maintaining the highest standards of quality and excellence, ensuring that our clients stay ahead in an ever-evolving digital landscape.

About The Role

We are seeking a highly motivated and detail-oriented IT Project Manager to join our dynamic team at My3Tech. In this role, you will be responsible for planning, executing, and overseeing various technology projects from initiation to completion. The ideal candidate will possess excellent leadership skills, a strong technical background, and the ability to coordinate cross-functional teams effectively. You will serve as the primary point of contact for clients and internal stakeholders, ensuring project deliverables are met on time, within scope, and within budget. This position offers an exciting opportunity to work on innovative projects that make a tangible impact on our clients' businesses.

Qualifications

The ideal candidate should have a bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field. A minimum of 5 years of experience in project management within the IT industry is required. Certifications such as PMP, PRINCE2, or Agile Scrum are highly desirable. Strong understanding of software development lifecycle, infrastructure, and cybersecurity is essential. Excellent communication, organizational, and problem-solving skills are a must. Experience working with cross-functional teams and managing multiple projects simultaneously is preferred. A proactive attitude and the ability to adapt to changing priorities are key attributes for success in this role.

Responsibilities

Develop comprehensive project plans, including scope, objectives, timelines, and resource allocation to ensure successful project delivery.

Coordinate internal resources and third-party vendors for the flawless execution of projects.

Monitor project progress and make adjustments as needed to ensure timely completion.

Communicate project status, issues, and risks effectively to stakeholders and senior management.

Ensure all projects adhere to quality standards and compliance requirements.

Identify and mitigate potential project risks through proactive planning and problem resolution.

Facilitate regular project meetings to track progress and address any challenges.

Maintain comprehensive documentation related to project activities, deliverables, and lessons learned.

Support continuous improvement initiatives by analyzing project outcomes and implementing best practices.

Assist in resource planning and capacity management to optimize project execution.

Benefits

My3Tech offers a competitive salary package aligned with industry standards, along with performance-based incentives. We provide comprehensive health insurance coverage, including medical, dental, and vision plans, to support the well-being of our employees and their families. Our company also offers generous paid time off, including holidays, vacation, and personal days, to promote work-life balance. Employees have access to ongoing professional development opportunities, including training programs, certifications, and workshops, to foster career growth. Additionally, we promote a collaborative and inclusive work environment that values diversity, innovation, and teamwork. Our employees enjoy flexible working arrangements to accommodate their personal and professional needs, along with various employee engagement activities throughout the year.

Equal Opportunity

My3Tech is an equal opportunity employer committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. We do not discriminate based on race, ethnicity, gender, age, religion, sexual orientation, disability, or any other protected characteristic. We believe in fostering a diverse workforce that reflects the communities we serve and are dedicated to providing equal employment opportunities to all qualified candidates. Our commitment to diversity and inclusion is integral to our company culture and success.

jobs.title: Data Analyst - Procurement
jobs.description:
Job description:

Basic Function
Supports the procurement organization’s goals and objectives by leveraging advanced analytical techniques and tools to transform raw data into strategic insights that enhance decision-making and drive operational success. Manage complex datasets, develop sophisticated analytics solutions, and collaborate with cross-functional teams to identify opportunities, optimize processes, and implement impactful improvements. Ensures data accuracy and integrity while communicating findings effectively to diverse stakeholders, influencing organizational strategies and fostering data-driven innovation. Job Duties
ESSENTIAL JOB DUTIES/RESPONSIBILITIES: (functions considered essential as defined by ADA)
Data Collection & Management:
Gather, organize, and manage complex datasets from various internal and external sources.
Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and integrity through rigorous audits, validation, and quality control measures.
Design and maintain advanced data models to support seamless data processing and automated reporting.

Data Analysis & Insights:
Conduct sophisticated analyses using statistical, predictive, and machine learning techniques to solve complex problems and drive business value.
Identify trends, correlations, and opportunities through deep-dive data exploration and advanced modeling.
Develop actionable recommendations to inform strategies and improve business outcomes.

Reporting & Visualization:
Design and implement dynamic dashboards, reports, and visualizations to clearly communicate complex insights.
Tailor presentations to diverse audiences, ensuring both technical and non-technical stakeholders can understand and utilize insights effectively.
Provide proactive and timely responses to ad-hoc reporting needs.

Collaboration & Stakeholder Engagement:
Partner with cross-functional teams and leadership to understand their data needs and provide expert-level analytical support.
Act as a key liaison between business units and technical teams to ensure alignment on project goals, metrics, and deliverables.
Mentor and train team members on data tools, systems, and best practices to elevate organizational analytics capabilities.

Process Improvement & Innovation:
Identify and lead initiatives to improve data collection, storage, and analysis.
Develop strategies to automate workflows, enhance efficiency, and support scalable growth.
Lead or contribute to cross-functional projects that leverage data to innovate and create new business opportunities.

Data Quality & Governance:
Establish and uphold best practices for data governance, ensuring compliance with organizational policies and standards.
Oversee data cleansing, enrichment, and transformation processes to ensure high-quality, reliable datasets.
Advocate for and support policies that promote data integrity and effective data management.

Leadership & Special Projects:
Drive high-impact projects that align with organizational goals and deliver measurable outcomes.
Provide guidance based on data-driven insights to influence decision-making at the leadership level.
Foster a culture of continuous improvement and innovation.
Lead or contribute to special assignments or organizational initiatives as required.

Special assignments or tasks assigned to the employee by their supervisor, as determined from time to time in their sole and complete discretion.
Experience
A minimum of 0- 2 years related experience is required.

PREFERRED EXPERIENCE:
Refinery experience and/or experience in procurement, accounting, and inventory management are preferred.
Education Level
A minimum of a Bachelor’s Degree, in a technical or business related field. Each year of experience over five in procurement or inventory control may be substituted for one year of education (nine years total experience required w/o education).
Required Skills
Strong analytical skills with the ability to collect, organize analyze and disseminate significant quantities of information with attention to detail and accuracy; knowledge of the procurement / contracting business and ability to apply this knowledge in providing supporting analytics and reporting; Advanced reading and writing skills; strong background and extensive knowledge of Excel (focus on Power Query/Power Pivot for data transformations); Power BI, SAP tables and QuickViewer query builder, SAP scripting and Microsoft VBA to automate routine manual tasks; familiarity with SQL; proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite; experience with requirements gathering and process documentation; ability to analyze and reconcile data across multiple data sets and systems, interpret data into insights, and develop actionable recommendations; experience with manual bulk data processing using reports/queries and uploads; experience in data analysis methods and using a variety of tools to develop visualizations; business process analysis and change management; extracting data from tables, building queries in SAP; preparing and using large data sets to create dashboards.;strong communication skills to present data-driven insights to senior leadership.
Supervisory/Managerial Responsibility
May have direct reports (contractor and/or HF Sinclair staff).
Work Conditions
Office based with occasional refinery work and with up to 25% travel required by land or air. May include petroleum refinery, warehouse/plant environment, and/or out-of-doors environment.
Benefits
HF Sinclair offers a comprehensive benefits package designed to support the well-being of our employees and their families. Our benefits include, but are not limited to, the following: Medical Insurance
Vision Insurance
Dental Insurance
Paid Time-Off
401(k) Retirement Plan with match
Educational Reimbursement
Parental Bonding Time
Employee Discounts

We are committed to fostering a supportive and inclusive work environment, ensuring our employees have the resources needed to thrive professionally and personally. Benefit eligibility is governed by official plan documents, for more details visit Total Rewards. Physical Requirements
Job conditions require, walking, sitting, twisting, stooping, crouching, kneeling, talking or hearing, making precise hand and finger movements, and perceiving color differences, the ability to wear personal protective equipment (beards not permitted), and the ability to operate and drive all assigned company vehicles at company standard insurance rates is essential, valid State driver’s license and proof of insurance required.  Job conditions may require reaching or grasping, lifting and/or carrying up to 20lbs, pushing and/or pulling up to 20 lbs, and climbing up to 15ft. 
Our One HF Sinclair Culture:
At HF Sinclair, we are united through our One HF Sinclair Culture, which is underpinned by our five core values of Safety, Integrity, Teamwork, Ownership and Inclusion. Developed to empower our people, our five core cultural values are at the heart of everything we do and extend to how we engage our stakeholders. These values influence our decisions, shape our behaviors and keep us connected across the entire organization. We maintain a true Safety culture for our employees, communities, environments and customers. Our goal is to make sure everyone returns home safely each day. We have a long-standing commitment to Integrity and ethical behavior and do what is right for our employees, investors, communities and the environment. We encourage employees to Step Up and Stand Out by championing a culture of Teamwork and Ownership. We foster a culture of Inclusion by encouraging diversity of experiences, viewpoints and backgrounds. What makes each of us different, together makes us stronger. About HF Sinclair Corporation
HF Sinclair Corporation, headquartered in Dallas, Texas, is an independent energy company that produces and markets high-value light products such as gasoline, diesel fuel, jet fuel, renewable diesel and other specialty products. HF Sinclair owns and operates refineries located in Kansas, Oklahoma, New Mexico, Wyoming, Washington and Utah and markets its refined products principally in the Southwest U.S., the Rocky Mountains extending into the Pacific Northwest and in other neighboring Plains states. HF Sinclair supplies high-quality fuels to more than 1,500 branded stations and licenses the use of the Sinclair brand at more than 300 additional locations throughout the country. In addition, subsidiaries of HF Sinclair produce and market base oils and other specialized lubricants in the U.S., Canada and the Netherlands, and export products to more than 80 countries. Through its subsidiaries, HF Sinclair produces renewable diesel at two of its facilities in Wyoming and also at its facility in Artesia, New Mexico. HF Sinclair provides petroleum product and crude oil transportation, terminalling, storage and throughput services to its refineries and the petroleum industry. Equal Opportunity Employer
HF Sinclair Corporation is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, veteran status or any other prohibited ground of discrimination.

jobs.title: Project Coordinator
jobs.description:
Position Summary

The Project Coordinator provides administrative and operational support to Project Managers by assisting with project setup, documentation, coordination, and execution activities. This role helps ensure projects move efficiently through internal workflows by supporting communication, maintaining accurate records, and assisting with scheduling, permitting, and invoicing preparation. The Project Coordinator plays a key role in ensuring accuracy, organization, and follow-through while developing foundational skills in project management.

Essential Job Duties

Project Setup & Coordination
· Assist with project setup activities, including surveys, code checks, and documentation collection.
Support permitting and landlord approval processes by gathering required information and tracking status.
Prepare and organize project files and documentation in ERP/project management systems.
Assist Project Managers with developing and maintaining project timelines and schedules.

Design & Approval Support
Submit design requests and track revisions in coordination with the Design team.
Confirm approved designs are documented and ready for Project Manager review before production release.

Production & Installation Support
Assist with releasing projects to production once approvals and documentation are complete.
Help coordinate installation schedules and subcontractor availability.
Support quality control efforts by ensuring installation documentation is complete and accurate.

Financial & Administrative Support
Assist with issuing purchase orders (POs) for installers and materials as directed by the Project Manager.
Support the Project Manager with cost-related closeout activities by gathering required documentation, including final permit costs, freight invoices, and vendor paperwork.
Prepare and compile project closeout documentation to support Project Manager submission to Accounts Receivable for invoicing.
Assist with resolving invoicing discrepancies by gathering supporting documentation and coordinating with internal teams as needed.

Communication & Team Collaboration
Support Project Managers with internal and external communication as needed.
Interact with Estimating, Design, Production, and Service teams as needed to support assigned project tasks.
Document meeting notes, decisions, and follow-up actions as required.
Escalate issues or questions to the Project Manager in a timely manner.

Reporting & Continuous Improvement
Maintain accurate project records and data integrity in ERP systems.
Assist with post-project debriefs and lessons-learned documentation.
Support process improvement initiatives and standardization efforts.
Participate in training and development activities to build project management skills.

Skill and Experience and Education Requirements

Associate’s or Bachelor’s degree preferred, or equivalent work experience.
1–2 years of experience in a project coordination, administrative, or support role preferred.
Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, Outlook).
Strong organizational and documentation skills.
Ability to manage multiple tasks and priorities in a fast-paced environment.
Basic understanding of construction, signage, or service operations is a plus.
Strong written and verbal communication skills.
Willingness to learn project management processes and systems.

Qualifications

Competency Statement(s)

Attention to Detail – Consistently maintains accurate and complete documentation.
Organization & Time Management – Effectively manages multiple tasks and deadlines.
Communication – Clearly communicates information and follows through on assigned actions.
Collaboration & Teamwork – Supports Project Managers and works cooperatively across departments.
Problem Awareness – Identifies issues early and escalates appropriately.
Adaptability – Adjusts effectively to changing priorities and workloads.
Decision-Making – Exercises sound judgment and makes critical decisions aligned with company procedures and best practices.
Customer Focus – Supports internal and external customer needs with professionalism.
Relationship Building – Develops and maintains positive, productive relationships with clients, colleagues, and stakeholders.
Adaptability & Change Management – Embraces change with a positive attitude, adjusts effectively to shifting priorities, and supports process improvements, system implementations, and organizational growth initiatives.
Safety Awareness – Identifies, communicates, and addresses safety risks to ensure a safe working environment for all employees.

Work Environment / Physical Requirements:

Office-based role with occasional travel within the building or to nearby locations.

jobs.title: Project Coordinator
jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

We are supporting our client on a search for a Project Coordinator who will help keep work moving across a fast-paced, fully remote team. This is a launchpad role for someone early in their career who wants real exposure to operations, leadership, and cross-functional work from day one.
You will partner with operations, product, marketing, and leadership on the projects that matter most. You will own timelines, follow-ups, documentation, and the small details that make big things possible. Mentorship is a built-in part of the seat.
We are intentionally open to recent grads, internship grads, bootcamp grads, and career changers. If you love organization, calendars, and helping smart people do their best work, we want to hear from you.

COMPENSATION

$50,000 - $75,000 per year, depending on experience. Comp reviewed annually. Performance bonus eligibility after 6 months.

WHAT YOU'LL DO

- Help plan, schedule, and track projects from kickoff to close across multiple teams
- Coordinate across engineering, product, design, marketing, and operations to keep work moving on time
- Maintain project documentation, timelines, status updates, and shared trackers
- Run weekly team standups, capture action items, and follow up on commitments
- Track risks and dependencies and flag blockers early so leaders can unblock fast
- Support meeting logistics, agendas, notes, and post-meeting follow-ups
- Build templates and lightweight processes that scale across teams
- Help prepare leadership updates, weekly digests, and quarterly review materials
- Manage vendor coordination, contract intake, and basic procurement
- Be the connective tissue across the company, the person who always knows the status

WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

- 0 to 2 years of project coordination, administrative, operations, or chief-of-staff style experience (internships count)
- Strong organization, follow-through, and attention to detail
- Clear written communication and a knack for summarizing complex situations
- Comfort with tools like Notion, Slack, Asana, Linear, Airtable, or Google Workspace
- A natural sense of urgency and a bias toward action
- Open to recent grads, internship grads, bootcamp grads, and career changers
- Bachelors degree preferred but not required, especially if you can point to relevant work

NICE TO HAVE

- Prior internship or part-time experience at a startup, agency, or consultancy
- Exposure to project management methodologies like Agile, Scrum, or Kanban
- Light familiarity with reporting tools like Looker, Mode, or Google Sheets pivot tables
- Experience supporting an executive, founder, or senior leader
- Comfort writing weekly recaps, status updates, or newsletters

BENEFITS & PERKS

- Fully remote work environment, with flexible hours across US time zones
- Competitive base salary with annual reviews and performance bonus eligibility
- Medical, dental, and vision insurance
- 401(k) plan with employer match
- Generous paid time off, paid holidays, and paid sick leave
- Paid parental leave
- Annual learning and development stipend
- Home office setup support and monthly tech stipend
- Two paid team offsites per year

WHY APPLY

This is one of the highest-leverage seats you can take early in your career. You will work directly with senior operators, see how the company runs, and leave this seat with a portfolio of real work, real impact, and a strong professional network.

ABOUT CAREERSCAPE

Careerscape is a staffing firm matching exceptional talent with growing companies across industries. We work with founders and hiring leaders to fill the seats that matter most, and we work with candidates to find roles where they can actually do their best work.

APPLICATION NOTICE

Apply via the external link to be considered. We review every application and respond to qualified candidates within one week.

EQUAL OPPORTUNITY STATEMENT

Careerscape is an Equal Opportunity Employer. M/F/Disability/Veterans. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, or veteran status.

jobs.title: Hydrogeologist/Environmental Scientist
jobs.description:
Description

About You

You are an experienced hydrogeologist or environmental scientist with practical experience working on environmental investigation and remediation projects. Collaborating with colleagues with diverse expertise is exciting for you, and you are also comfortable working independently. You effectively manage tasks and projects, overseeing and delegating technical tasks, producing high quality deliverables, and exceeding internal and external client expectations. You are highly detail-oriented, organized, and timely. You have broad experience communicating key concepts orally and in written form to a lay audience, regulators, attorneys, and clients. Quality management and integrity are among your strongest principles. You are looking for opportunities to grow in your technical area and in the firm as a whole. A lifelong learner, you want to keep growing intellectually and professionally. You know how to exceed clients’ expectations. Collaborating with colleagues with diverse expertise is exciting for you, and you are also comfortable working independently. People thrive under your coaching. Quality management and integrity are among your strongest principles.

About Integral

Integral Consulting Inc. is a science and engineering firm delivering sound technical solutions to meet today’s challenges in the health, environmental, and natural resource arenas. We are a values-driven collaborative team that is as passionate about company culture as we are about the work we do for our clients. Since our founding in 2002, we have operated with a demonstrated commitment to the well-being, diversity, and professional development of our staff and an equal commitment to strong partnerships with our clients and teaming partners. We are a company where people come to solve today’s toughest environmental challenges through innovative ideas, exceptional teamwork, and dedication to accuracy and technical quality.

About The Job

We are currently seeking a mid- to senior-level scientist for our Investigation and Remediation business area. The position will support multiple clients and projects, with the common theme of supporting site characterization and remedial investigation activities under federal and state-led regulatory programs. The day-to-day work will vary and may involve project and task management, data analysis, literature review, field work, and writing. Career growth at Integral offers future opportunities to manage tasks and projects, communicate with clients, and present and/or publish your work through science conferences and peer-reviewed journals. Most assignments will require teamwork, though a few will be entirely your responsibility.

This position may offer in-office, remote, or hybrid opportunities based on the candidate’s location and the needs of the organization.

Requirements

Responsibilities

As a mid-level environmental science professional, your job functions will include, but not be limited to:

Working safely and actively participating in our safe work culture.
Designing/supporting site and remedial investigation activities under federal and state lead regulatory programs (initially with an Oregon focus) through proposal and work plan preparation
Leading field investigation efforts by providing in-office or in-field support as appropriate based on project and program complexity.
Developing and refining conceptual site models and contaminant fate and transport by analyzing, evaluating, and combining geologic, hydrogeologic, chemical, and other data and information.
Supporting the development and refinement of remedial alternatives and remedial design by providing analysis and interpretation of existing and hypothetical future geologic/hydrogeologic conditions as part of multidisciplinary teams.
Conducting project and task management
Managing subcontractors and performing basic contract management (preparing work orders)
Communicating technical information to varied audiences (e.g., clients, staff)
Providing a high quality and consistent work product including technical reports/documents for submittal to internal and external clients/regulators
Leading and mentoring staff.

Qualifications

Candidates should hold a B.S. or M.S. degree in hydrogeology, geology, environmental science, or related technical field.

The Ideal Candidate Will Have

Experience with leading and completing field investigations and contractor oversight
5+ years of professional experience
Experience in project management
Experience using and applying a variety of analysis and/or modeling tools (e.g. GIS, Leapfrog, EVS, or other similar software tools)
Strong quantitative and problem-solving skills
Demonstrated ability to work successfully within multidisciplinary and diverse teams
Excellent written and oral communication skills
A desire to grow both intellectually and professionally
Possess strong organizational abilities
Ability to work independently and within a multidisciplinary team of scientists and engineers

Compensation is commensurate with experience and internal equity, within the listed range. An employee’s total compensation package includes annual salary; company-subsidized (employee and family) medical, dental, and vision insurance; 401(k) participation with a company match of 1% to 4% of annual compensation; company-paid life and short-and long-term disability insurance; paid sick leave (10 days); paid vacation (15 days); paid holidays (9 days) per year; and the potential for an annual discretionary bonus based on company performance. Paid time off will be prorated to reflect part-time scheduled hours, if applicable.

Integral Consulting Inc. is proud to be an equal employment opportunity employer who provides equal opportunity in all of our employment practices to all qualified employees and applicants without regard to race, color, religion, creed, gender, pregnancy (including childbirth or related medical conditions), national origin or ancestry, age, sexual orientation, disability, marital status, military, veteran, or Vietnam Era Veterans’ Readjustment Assistance Act protected veteran status, gender identity or gender expression, genetic information (including testing or characteristics), or any other category protected by federal, state, and local laws. We believe that a diverse and inclusive environment drives innovation and excellence, and we are dedicated to building a team that reflects the diverse communities we live in and serve. If you require reasonable accommodations during the application or interview process, please contact us at integraljobs@integral-corp.com.

We are excited to connect with individuals who share our mission and values, and those who bring diverse contributions to our team. We value unique perspectives and skills, and encourage you to apply, even if you don’t meet every requirement.

All offers of employment are contingent on candidates successfully passing an employment history screening, degree verification, and a federal, state, and county criminal background and Watch List screening. Candidates will receive a written notice of this requirement. All screenings will be done in accordance with all local, state, and federal laws.

jobs.title: GIS Analyst, (A264111-3), 208, G.I.S.
jobs.description:
Job Description

Coordinates the management of the Utilities geographic information system, develops the Geographic Information System (GIS) database, reviews data sources/inputs, and the associated publishing of data for the Department. Serves as Subject Matter Expert (SME) for all GIS data related to pumped and gravity flow infrastructure.

Essential Functions/ Job Competencies/ Physical Requirements

ESSENTIAL DUTIES & RESPONSIBILITIES

The intent of this job description is to provide a representative summary of the major duties and responsibilities performed by employees in this job. Employees may be requested to perform job-related tasks other than those specifically presented in this description.

Uses the ARCGIS 10.x platform to perform editing, spatial analysis, and produce maps.
Configures mobile devices including GPS equipment.
Completes associated computer-related technical work.
Performs aerial imagery analysis and digitizes features using imagery.
Uses imagery analysis to identify project sites and locations.
Translates information on maps, surveys, and various other instruments used by various professions.
Researches, locates, analyzes various types of data within databases, maps, as well as local, state and national references.
Provides statistical analysis based on spatial data.
Develops spatial relationships with various types of data and formats.
Coordinates and prioritizes inflow of geographic and tabular data.
Will be required to drive a City vehicle for City business use.
Performs other related work as required.

Knowledge Of:

COMPETENCIES FOR SUCCESSFUL PERFORMANCE OF JOB DUTIES

Knowledge of GIS computers and applications.
Knowledge of spatial and data transformations.
Knowledge of versioning within ArcGIS.
Knowledge of principles of data collection.
Knowledge of Topology.
Knowledge of business English, spelling, and arithmetic.
Knowledge of modern office practices, procedures, methods, and equipment.

Skills and Abilities:

Ability to read and digitize metes and bounds.
Ability to keep moderately complex records, to assemble and organize data, and to prepare reports from such records.
Ability to provide effective leadership and coordination in developing solutions and recommending new techniques.
Ability to make independent decisions in accordance with established policies and procedures.
Ability to maintain good planning and organizational skills.
Ability to establish and maintain effective working relationships with elected officials, the general public, and others.
Ability to give directions to and gain compliance from database technicians.
Ability to maintain valid Texas Driver License and a good driving record.
Ability to project a positive and professional image of the City of Laredo.
Ability to effectively communicate both orally and in writing in the English and Spanish language.
Ability to maintain good health and physical condition sufficient to permit the full performance of the duties of the position.
Ability to comply with all City of Laredo's policies and procedures.
Ability to perform other duties as assigned.

WORK ENVIRONMENT/CONDITIONS AND PHYSICAL DEMANDS

Physical Effort Requirements

Position will have the following exposures

Office or similar indoor environment, relatively free from unpleasant environmental conditions or hazards.
Occasional exposure to unpleasant environmental conditions and/or hazards. Occasional outside work.
Exerting up to 10 pounds of force occasionally, a negligible amount of force frequently, and/or constantly having to lift, carry, push, pull, or otherwise move objects, including the human body. Sedentary work involves sitting most of the time. Jobs are sedentary if walking and standing are required only occasionally, and all other sedentary criteria are met.
Exerting up to 35 pounds of force occasionally, up to 20 pounds of force frequently, and/or up to 20 pounds of force constantly having to move objects.

Work Environment Locations

Position will require the employee to work in the following types of work environments

Seldom:

Confined space

Sometimes:

Outdoor environment
Street environment (near moving traffic)
Construction site
Warehouse environment

Frequently:

Vehicles

Often:

Office or similar indoor environment

Exposures

Position will require the employee to be exposed to the following environmental elements

Seldom:

Individuals who are hostile or irate
Individuals with known violent backgrounds
Extreme cold (below 32 degrees)
Communicable diseases
Fumes or airborne particles
Toxic or caustic chemicals or substances
Loud noises (85+ decibels such as heavy trucks, jack hammers, construction)

Sometimes:

Moving mechanical parts

Frequently:

Extreme heat (above 100 degrees)

Travel

Position will require the employee to travel

Never:

International travel

Seldom

Regional travel
National travel

Never:

Local travel

Minimum Qualifications

Required Education and Experience

Bachelor's degree from an accredited* college or university in GIS, Computer Science, Environmental Science, Engineering, Management Information Systems, or related field.
At least one (1) year of experience in coordinating the management of geographic information system, associated mapping of the data, GIS database design, and data entry.
Council of Higher Education Accreditation (C.H.E.A)

Valid Licenses and Certifications

Valid Licenses or Certifications

Valid Texas Driver License. If applicant holds an out-of-state license, a State of Texas Driver License must be obtained within 6 months of employment.

As part of the minimum requirements for all positions with the City of Laredo, a thorough background check and certified school transcripts will be required from all applicants who are offered employment.

Effective February 24, 1997, all selected applicants are required to undergo a drug and alcohol test prior to employment.

Effective January 7, 2008, all persons in safety-sensitive functions/positions, as per City of Laredo Drug and Alcohol Policy definition, shall be subject to random drug and alcohol testing.

A person with a disability who needs a reasonable accommodation related to a selection process is requested to contact the Human Resources Department at (956) 791-7480 two days prior to the scheduled test and/or interview if applicable.

City of Laredo is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action/Pro Disabled and Veteran Employer. We welcome differences in the form of gender, race, ethnicity, disability, geography, socioeconomic status, age, politics, religion, philosophy, sexual orientation, or gender identity. All applicants who share this goal are encouraged to apply. Must be able to function in a principle-based organization that has a culture built on character and core values.

jobs.description:
About The Company

University Health is a leading healthcare organization committed to providing exceptional medical services and innovative healthcare solutions. With a strong focus on patient-centered care, cutting-edge research, and community engagement, University Health strives to improve health outcomes and enhance the quality of life for the communities it serves. Our organization boasts a diverse team of healthcare professionals dedicated to excellence, integrity, and continuous learning. As a pioneer in the healthcare industry, University Health invests in advanced technology and infrastructure to ensure the delivery of top-tier medical services across various specialties.

About The Role

We are seeking a highly motivated and experienced Healthcare Administrator to join our dynamic team at University Health. The successful candidate will play a crucial role in managing daily operations, overseeing administrative functions, and supporting clinical staff to ensure seamless delivery of healthcare services. This position requires strong leadership skills, excellent organizational abilities, and a comprehensive understanding of healthcare policies and procedures. The Healthcare Administrator will act as a liaison between medical staff, patients, and administrative personnel, fostering a collaborative environment that promotes efficiency, compliance, and exceptional patient care.

Qualifications

The ideal candidate should possess a bachelor’s degree in Healthcare Administration, Business Administration, or a related field; a master’s degree is preferred. Proven experience in healthcare management or administration, ideally within a hospital or large clinical setting, is essential. Strong knowledge of healthcare laws, regulations, and accreditation standards is required. Excellent communication, leadership, and problem-solving skills are vital for success in this role. Familiarity with healthcare management software and electronic health records (EHR) systems is also necessary. Candidates should demonstrate a commitment to ethical practices, patient confidentiality, and continuous improvement.

Responsibilities

The Healthcare Administrator will be responsible for overseeing the day-to-day administrative operations of the healthcare facility, including staff management, budgeting, and resource allocation. They will ensure compliance with healthcare regulations and accreditation standards, maintaining high standards of quality and safety. The role involves coordinating with clinical teams to optimize workflow, improve patient experience, and support clinical excellence. The administrator will also handle patient inquiries, resolve administrative issues, and implement policies to enhance operational efficiency. Additionally, they will contribute to strategic planning initiatives, participate in performance evaluations, and foster a positive work environment that encourages professional development.

Benefits

University Health offers a comprehensive benefits package designed to support our employees’ well-being and professional growth. Benefits include competitive salary packages, health insurance coverage, retirement plans, paid time off, and professional development opportunities. We also provide a supportive work environment that values diversity, inclusion, and work-life balance. Employees have access to wellness programs, employee assistance programs, and ongoing training to enhance their skills and career progression within the organization.

Equal Opportunity

University Health is an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to fostering an inclusive workplace that values diversity and promotes fairness in all employment practices. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, age, disability, or any other protected status. We believe that a diverse workforce enhances our ability to provide outstanding healthcare services and innovate for the future.

The organization operates within the academic healthcare sector, addressing the critical need for accurate clinical data management in medical records. It focuses on advancing medical innovation and serving community health needs through precise coding and documentation. Collaborate with providers to clarify documentation and provide education

Maintain continuing education credits as required by professional standards

Certification as Registered Health Information Technician (RHIT), Registered Health Information Administrator (RHIA), or Certified Coding Specialist (CCS)

Experience with personal computers

We are seeking a motivated Project Geologist, Project Scientist, or Project Engineer with a strong background in environmental remediation to join our Technical Services Organization. This mid-career role offers the opportunity to contribute to high-profile projects, develop technical leadership skills, and grow into a Technical Lead role on small to moderately complex assignments. This position must report to the CAPE office in Honolulu, Hawaii.

Responsibilities

Provide support for project planning, field sampling, quality control oversight, design evaluations, data analysis, and reporting.
Develop technical plans and reports including sampling and analysis plans, quality control plans, and construction completion reports.
Schedule and oversee field activities such as soil, surface water, and groundwater sampling, drilling, subcontractor management, and remediation system operation.
Support proposal development, cost estimating, and preparation of regulatory submittals.
Mentor junior staff and contribute to CAPE’s technical quality standards and procedures.
Collaborate with project and senior management to deliver high-quality results that meet budget, schedule, and client expectations.

Qualifications

Education: Bachelor’s degree in engineering, geology, or environmental science; advanced degree preferred.
Experience: Minimum 5 years in environmental remediation. Experience working in Hawaii/Tropical Pacific preferred.
Certifications/Training:
Preferred: Licensed Engineer or Geologist in Training (E.I.T. or G.I.T.) or able to obtain Professional Registration within six months of hire
HAZWOPER 40-hour training (and annual refresher).
Adult First Aid/CPR.
Valid U.S. driver’s license with satisfactory record.
U.S. citizenship required (due to federal contracting requirements).

Strong technical writing, analytical, and communication skills.
Familiarity with federal environmental regulatory programs (CERCLA, RCRA).
Proficiency with Microsoft Office and industry-standard tools (CAD, ArcGIS, modeling software).

Work Environment & Travel

Position requires up to 50% travel annually for project support.
Field conditions may involve exposure to varying weather, hazardous materials, and physical demands.

Salary

$80K - $102K commensurate with experience

WHY CAPE?

For 35 years, CAPE has delivered construction and environmental remediation solutions to governmental and private organizations nationwide. Our projects are high-profile environmental remediation projects involving cleanup of contaminated soil, groundwater, and sediment. The culture of our company is one of working together to achieve our clients’ objectives, company goals, and employees’

professional goals, and is succinctly and accurately defined as “We, Not Me” behavior. To ensure long-term, sustainable growth, we tailor our 3-year vision to be ahead of our industry in delivering our projects, align with our clients and team partners to improve our existing market share, and develop new services to support smart growth.

CAPE offers competitive benefits including Medical/Dental/Vision insurance, life insurance, ADP retirement savings plan including both traditional and Roth 401(k) with up to 4% match and lowest in class fees, health savings account (HSA) with investment option, flexible spending account (FSA), paid annual leave, paid maternity/paternity leave, and tuition reimbursement. CAPE also offers staff several other voluntary benefits options, as well as the option to work from home. Straight time overtime may be offered on certain field assignments.

CAPE is an Equal Employment Opportunity Employer. We invite all qualified applicants to apply to our open positions.

Communication

Any communication regarding this position will come from an email address ending in cape-inc.com. Scammers may try to imitate our HR team. Please use caution when replying to emails that are from other domains. To ensure you are applying for a legitimate job, please only complete a job application that originates from https://www.cape-inc.com/environmental-jobs.

jobs.description:
1. About Our Client:
The organization operates within the healthcare industry, focusing on improving productivity for physicians and enhancing patient care. It manages the largest network of medical professionals in the United States, facilitating collaboration among verified clinicians, access to medical news and research, career management, and virtual patient visits. This platform includes over 80% of U.S. physicians, 50% of nurse practitioners and physician assistants, and 90% of graduating medical students as members. By connecting healthcare providers, the organization aims to address inefficiencies in the U.S. healthcare system.

2. About the Opportunity:
The Data Analys role involves working within cross-functional teams to analyze extensive healthcare datasets and generate insights that improve healthcare outcomes. The position contributes to both product development and client-facing analytics, influences data team strategies in collaboration with product leaders, and leads data projects from collection through to recommendation stages. This role is pivotal in turning data into actionable intelligence that guides organizational decisions.

• Develop product and client-facing analytical reports.
• Collaborate with product managers, analysts, and developers on data projects.

• Demonstrated curiosity and passion for data, and ability to learn quickly.

Job Posting: Job File Coordinator

Are you highly organized, detail-oriented, and passionate about providing outstanding customer service? Do you thrive in a fast-paced environment with opportunities for growth? Join our team as a Project Coordinator/Job File Coordinator
and become a vital part of a dynamic workplace where your contributions will make a difference!

What We Offer

Competitive Compensation
Superior Benefits
Paid Training
Career Progression Opportunities
Personal and Professional Development
And more!

About The Role

As a Job File Coordinator, you will act as a key liaison between customers and project stakeholders, ensuring projects run smoothly from start to finish. You’ll monitor project progress, maintain accurate documentation, and collaborate across teams to meet customer needs efficiently. If you’re self-motivated, organized, and ready to take on challenges, we want you on our team!

Key Responsibilities

Oversee and maintain accurate job file documentation from lead conversion to project completion.
Communicate clearly and efficiently with customers, production crews, and stakeholders to ensure project success.
Create and review estimates based on project scope and supporting documentation.
Monitor active projects to ensure compliance with client requirements and internal standards.
Audit job files daily to confirm all work is properly documented and invoiced.
Maintain an up-to-date Work in Progress board to track project statuses.
Assist in the final review and audit of completed job files before invoicing.
Support cross-departmental collaboration as needed.

Position Requirements

High school diploma or GED (required).
At least 1 year of customer service and/or office-related experience (preferred).
Strong administrative and multitasking skills with keen attention to detail.
Proficiency in relevant computer applications and digital tools.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
Experience in the restoration, construction, or insurance industries is a plus.

Work Environment And Physical Demands

Predominantly a sedentary office role in a fast-paced environment.
Occasional filing tasks may require lifting, bending, or standing.
Ability to work varying hours (Monday–Friday, 7:00 a.m. – 6:00 p.m.) with flexibility as needed.

Why Join Us?

This role offers the opportunity to grow your career while making meaningful contributions to a team-focused environment. From ensuring projects are managed seamlessly to interacting with customers and stakeholders, this position is perfect for someone looking to build a rewarding career while honing their skills.

Take the next step in your career— apply now
to join a team where your dedication and expertise are truly valued!

Picture yourself here fulfilling your potential.

At SERVPRO®, you can make a positive difference in people’s lives each and every day! We’re seeking self-motivated, proactive, responsible, and service-oriented teammates to join us in our mission of helping customers in their greatest moments of need by repairing and restoring homes and businesses with an industry-leading level of service. With nearly 2,000 franchises all over the country, finding exciting and rewarding SERVPRO® career opportunities near you is easy! We look forward to hearing from you.

All employees of a SERVPRO® Franchise are hired by, employed by, and under the sole supervision and control of an independently owned and operated SERVPRO® Franchise. SERVPRO® Franchise employees are not employed by, jointly employed by, agents of, or under the supervision or control of Servpro Franchisor, LLC, in any manner whatsoever.

jobs.title: Project Coordinator
jobs.description:
About Our Client
The organization operates within the professional services sector, focusing on delivering coordinated project execution to its clients. It addresses challenges related to managing multiple concurrent projects by structuring project delivery processes, ensuring efficient progression from planning to closure, and maintaining clear communication with both customers and internal stakeholders.
About the Opportunity
The Project Coordinator
role is central to the successful delivery of projects within the professional services group. This position ensures that project activities, resources, communications, and schedules are effectively managed to maintain momentum and accountability. The coordinator supports engineers, clients, and internal teams by organizing project documentation, facilitating communication, and aligning efforts with business objectives.
Responsibilities
Project Lifecycle Execution & Scheduling
Milestone Coordination:
Coordinate and manage detailed project schedules, resource allocations, and critical performance milestones.
Stakeholder Communication:
Serve as the primary operational point of contact to manage communications with customers, external vendors, and internal teams.
Meeting Facilitation:
Structure and facilitate critical project milestones, including project kickoffs, routine status meetings, and post-project reviews.
Health Monitoring:
Actively monitor project timelines, budget allocations, and overall portfolio health metrics to ensure on-time delivery.

Administrative Governance & Risk Management
Documentation Maintenance:
Maintain clean and structured project records, baseline documentation, and necessary administrative tracking tasks.
Scope & Change Control:
Manage incoming project change requests and ensure all adjustments to project scope are thoroughly documented and approved.
Risk Identification:
Proactively identify project dependencies, operational risks, and bottlenecks that could threaten delivery timelines.
Engineering Support:
Reduce administrative and operational coordination burdens for core engineering teams, allowing them to focus on technical delivery.
Systems & Reporting:
Produce detailed project performance reports, update tracking dashboards, and systematically enter and maintain records within internal PSA (Professional Services Automation) and project management systems.

Qualifications
Experience & Systems
Tenure:
3 to 5 years of professional experience in project coordination, service delivery, or closely related operations roles (experience working within a Managed Service Provider (MSP) environment is highly preferred).
Technical Tooling:
Direct experience utilizing enterprise PSA platforms and specialized project management tools.
Productivity Stack:
Highly proficient navigating the Microsoft 365 ecosystem.

Core Competencies & Education
Project Toolset:
Advanced skills in comprehensive project coordination, structural scheduling, customer relationship communication, change management, and risk identification.
Education:
An Associate's or Bachelor's degree in Business, Information Technology, Project Management, or a related academic discipline is preferred.
Certifications:
Professional project management certifications—such as Certified Associate in Project Management (CAPM), Project Management Professional (PMP), Agile, Scrum, or ITIL—are considered highly beneficial but are not strictly required.

Compensation and Benefits
Compensation
Base Hourly Rate:
$25.00 to $33.00 per hour.

Eligible for a targeted annual incentive bonus structured around overall performance achievements.

jobs.title: Manager, Solutions Engineering
jobs.description:
About Mixpanel

Mixpanel turns data clarity into innovation. Trusted by more than 29,000 companies, including Workday, Pinterest, LG, and Rakuten Viber, Mixpanel’s AI-first digital analytics help teams accelerate adoption, improve retention, and ship with confidence. Powering this is an industry-leading platform that combines product and web analytics, session replay, experimentation, feature flags, and metric trees. Mixpanel delivers insights that customers trust. Visit mixpanel.com to learn more.

About The Role

As a Manager on the Solutions Engineering team at Mixpanel, you will lead a talented group of analytics consultants who are pivotal to our success. You will be at the forefront of driving customer value, guiding your team as they serve as the primary technical resources for our Sales organization.

This role is a unique blend of leadership, technical expertise, and business strategy. You will be responsible for coaching and developing your team, refining our technical sales processes, and collaborating across departments to ensure our customers achieve their business outcomes with Mixpanel.

You will also play a key role in hiring, onboarding, and managing the next wave of Solutions Engineers. By fostering a culture of excellence and deep customer empathy, you will empower your team to architect innovative solutions, drive product adoption, and ultimately secure the technical win with prospects and customers.

Responsibilities

Develop & Mentor: Lead, coach, and grow a high-performing and inclusive team of Solutions Engineers, actively investing in their career development and upholding a high standard of performance.
Drive Results: Partner closely with Sales leadership and Account Executives to provide technical expertise that drives new, retained, and expansion ARR. You will ensure your team's activities are directly contributing to the company's bottom line.
Prioritize & Problem Solve: Guide your team through complex customer evaluations and technical challenges. You will manage team resources effectively, aligning the right skills to customer needs to achieve productivity targets and successful outcomes.
Cross-Functional Partnership: Act as a key technical liaison, collaborating with peer managers across Sales, Product, and Engineering. You will gather and synthesize customer feedback from your team to influence product strategy and solve problems at scale.
Communicate & Manage Change: Effectively translate broader company and departmental strategy into clear, actionable goals for your team. You will guide your direct reports through evolving business priorities with empathy and clarity.
Hire the Best: Actively assess the needs of the team, build a pipeline of top talent, and hire outstanding individuals who elevate the team's capabilities and contribute to our inclusive culture.
Innovate & Raise the Bar: Relentlessly seek to improve how your team operates, from refining demo strategies and proof-of-concept methodologies to adopting new tools and processes that increase effectiveness and celebrate success.

We’re Looking For Someone Who

Has progressive experience in a B2B SaaS environment, including 3+ years of people management experience leading a technical pre-sales, solutions engineering, or professional services team.
Exhibits a "player-coach" mentality with deep knowledge in the data and analytics space. You are an expert on how data products (like CDPs, data warehouses, and analytics tools) are implemented and adopted by customers.
Is a proven cross-functional partner with a track record of successfully working with sales teams to navigate complex deals and drive revenue.
Demonstrates expertise in communicating complex technical concepts clearly and effectively to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Is skilled at prioritizing team activities and managing workload in a dynamic environment, balancing customer needs with efficiency goals.
Is a natural mentor and developer of talent, with a passion for coaching and a history of building inclusive, high-achieving teams.
Handles ambiguity with ease, demonstrating flexibility and a proactive, problem-solving mindset when adapting to new challenges and business priorities.
Actively seeks feedback and is humble to learn, consistently looking for ways to improve themselves and their team.

Bonus Points

Previous experience in management consulting, strategic operations, or a similar role focused on go-to-market strategy.
Direct, hands-on experience with Mixpanel or other product analytics tools like Amplitude, Pendo, or Contentsquare.
Strong familiarity with the modern data stack, including tools like Snowflake, Google BigQuery, Segment, or Hightouch.

Compensation

The amount listed below is the total target cash compensation (TTCC) and includes base compensation and variable compensation in the form of either a company bonus or commissions. Variable compensation type is determined by your role and level. In addition to the cash compensation provided, this position is also eligible for equity consideration and other benefits including medical, vision, and dental insurance coverage. You can view our benefits offerings here.

Our salary ranges are determined by role and level and are benchmarked to the SF Bay Area Technology data cut released by Radford, a global compensation database. The range displayed represents the minimum and maximum TTCC for new hire salaries for the position across all of our US locations. To stay on top of market conditions, we refresh our salary ranges twice a year so these ranges may change in the future. Within the range, individual pay is determined by experience, job-related skills, qualifications, and other factors. If you have questions about the specific range, your recruiter can share this information.

Mixpanel Compensation Range

$238,300 - $321,705 USD

Benefits And Perks

Comprehensive Medical, Vision, and Dental Care
Mental Wellness Benefit
Generous Vacation Policy & Additional Company Holidays
Enhanced Parental Leave
Volunteer Time Off
Additional US Benefits: Pre-Tax Benefits including 401(K), Wellness Benefit, Holiday Break
please note that benefits and perks for contract positions will vary*

Culture Values

Make Bold Bets: We choose courageous action over comfortable progress.
Innovate with Insight: We tackle decisions with rigor and judgment - combining data, experience and collective wisdom to drive powerful outcomes.
One Team: We collaborate across boundaries to achieve far greater impact than any of us could accomplish alone.
Candor with Connection: We build meaningful relationships that enable honest feedback and direct conversations.
Champion the Customer: We seek to deeply understand our customers’ needs, ensuring their success is our north star.
Powerful Simplicity: We find elegant solutions to complex problems, making sophisticated things accessible.

Why choose Mixpanel?

We’re a leader in analytics with over 9,000 customers and $277M raised from prominent investors: like Andreessen-Horowitz, Sequoia, YC, and, most recently, Bain Capital. Mixpanel’s pioneering event-based data analytics platform offers a powerful yet simple solution for companies to understand user behaviors and easily track overarching company success metrics. Our accomplished teams continuously facilitate our expansion by tackling the ever-evolving challenges tied to scaling, reliability, design, and service. Choosing to work at Mixpanel means you’ll be helping the world’s most innovative companies learn from their data so they can make better decisions.

Mixpanel is an equal opportunity employer supporting workforce diversity. At Mixpanel, we are focused on things that really matter—our people, our customers, our partners—out of a recognition that those relationships are the most valuable assets we have. We actively encourage women, people with disabilities, veterans, underrepresented minorities, and LGBTQ+ people to apply. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status. Pursuant to the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance or other similar laws that may be applicable, we will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records. We’ve immersed ourselves in our Culture and Values as our guiding principles for the impact we want to have and the future we are building.

jobs.title: Data Analyst
jobs.description:
Data Analyst – Role Description & Qualifications
Role Overview
The Data Analyst is responsible for transforming raw data into meaningful insights that support business decisions and operational efficiency. This role focuses on data collection, analysis, reporting, and visualization to identify trends, measure performance, and uncover opportunities for improvement. The Data Analyst works closely with stakeholders across departments to ensure data-driven strategies align with organizational goals.
Key Responsibilities
• Collect, process, and analyze data from multiple internal and external sources.
• Validate, clean, and structure datasets to ensure accuracy and consistency.
• Develop reports, dashboards, and visualizations to communicate insights effectively.
• Analyze trends, patterns, and key performance indicators to support decision-making.
• Collaborate with business teams to understand analytical requirements and objectives.
• Support ad hoc analysis requests and present findings to stakeholders.
• Assist in defining data standards, metrics, and documentation practices.
• Identify data quality issues and recommend corrective actions.
• Contribute to automation and optimization of data analysis and reporting processes.
• Stay informed about analytical tools, techniques, and best practices.
Qualifications and Requirements
• Bachelor’s degree in Data Analytics, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Economics, or a related field.
• Strong understanding of data analysis techniques, statistical concepts, and data structures.
• Experience working with datasets, databases, and reporting tools.
• Proficiency in spreadsheets and data visualization platforms; familiarity with query languages or analytical scripting is a plus.
• Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving skills.
• High attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy.
• Ability to translate complex data into clear, actionable insights.
• Strong communication and collaboration skills.
• Ability to manage multiple projects and meet deadlines effectively.
• Experience gained through professional roles, academic projects, or internships is advantageous.
Summary
The Data Analyst plays a vital role in enabling data-driven decision-making by delivering accurate analysis and actionable insights. This position offers opportunities to work with diverse datasets, collaborate across teams, and contribute directly to strategic and operational improvements within the organization.

jobs.title: Technical Solutions Engineer
jobs.description:
Please note that this position is based on our campus in Madison, WI, and requires relocation to the area. We recruit nationally and provide financial relocation assistance.

Problem-solving with a purpose.

As a Technical Solutions Engineer at Epic, you’ll work on software that impacts 305 million patients around the world. Alongside customer counterparts, you’ll tackle mission-critical and complex problems – we’re way beyond “turn it off and on again” issues. You’ll need to diagnose the problem, identify a solution, and manage the implementation of the solution, sometimes across multiple physical locations and stakeholders. Learn more about our team at https://careers.epic.com/Jobs/Technical-Solutions-Engineer.

Test the limits of your brain for the most innovative health systems on the planet.

The top-ranked health systems in U.S. News and World Report are Epic customers. Our community includes major systems like the Mayo Clinic, Johns Hopkins, Cleveland Clinic, and Kaiser Permanente, as well as leading academic medical centers at the University of Wisconsin, University of Michigan, University of California, University of Texas, The Ohio State University, and many more.

Live affordably in a city known for its rising tech talent.

Epic is located just outside Madison, Wisconsin, the second fastest growing market for tech talent in the United States and home to the state capital and the University of Wisconsin. Madison, a city surrounded by water, has received accolades for being the greenest city in America (NerdWallet), the best city for renters (SmartAsset), the fittest city in America (Fitbit), and the third best metro in the US for young professionals (Forbes Advisor).

More than just important work
.

Our uniquely themed campus was designed to heighten your ability to get stuff done in your office, a conference room, or by the fireplace in a comfy chair. All meals are restaurant-quality but cost only a few dollars, and they’re prepared by a team comprised of kitchen talent from restaurants around the country. And, after five years here, you’ll earn a four-week sabbatical anywhere in the world. Staff have kayaked in Patagonia, attended a Beyoncé concert in Ireland, built a library in Tanzania, and run a marathon in Antarctica.

We offer comprehensive benefits to keep you healthy and happy as you grow in your life and career, and your merit-based compensation will reflect the impact your work has on the company and our customers. You'll also be eligible for annual raises and bonuses, as well as stock grants, which give you an even greater stake in the success of Epic and our customers. Healthcare is global, and building the best ideas from around the world into Epic software is a point of pride. As an Equal Opportunity Employer, we know that inclusive teams design software that supports the delivery of quality care for all patients, so diversity, equity, and inclusion are written into our principles. Please see our full non-discrimination statement at https://careers.epic.com/EEO.

Bachelor's degree or greater
A history of academic excellence or professional success
Willingness to travel occasionally to customer locations
Eligible to work in the U.S. without visa sponsorship
Relocation to the Madison, WI area (reimbursed)
COVID-19 vaccination

jobs.title: Operations Data Analyst
jobs.description:
Job Summary

Our client is seeking a Operations Data Analyst who will provide end to end support for MDMS operations, including ingestion, VEE processing, aggregation, and data publishing.

Core Responsibilities

Analysis and resolution of complex data quality issues, system defects, and integration failures
Configure, and optimize VEE rules, exception handling, and performance tuning
Monitor, and troubleshoot MDMS batch jobs, workflows, dependencies, and calendars
Investigation and resolution of complex MDMS and scheduling failures, including missed runs, data delays, and downstream impacts
Oversee and support integrations between MDMS and other enterprise systems (CIS, AMI, Analytics, Data Warehouse, etc.)
Execute comprehensive test cycles functional, performance and ORT for system changes, defect fixes, and new functionality
Develop and maintain system documentation, runbooks, and operational standards
Participate in incident management, root cause analysis, and continuous improvement initiatives

Required Skills/Experience (Must-Haves)

Bachelor’s degree in Information Systems, Computer Science, Engineering, or related field
5+ years of hands on experience in Unix and Oracle applications including 24/7 application support
Experience supporting MDMS and AMI systems within a utility environment
Understanding of Smart Meter technologies, interval data, and utility business processes
Experience with at least one enterprise MDMS platform (e.g., Landis+Gyr, Itron, Oracle, Siemens)
Expertise in SQL and advanced data analysis for troubleshooting, reporting, and validation
Experience supporting SOX regulated systems, including compliance with access controls, audit logging, and change management requirements
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, including executive level communication

Preferred Skills/Experience (Nice-to-Haves)

Experience supporting Landis+Gyr MDMS platform
Experience supporting both electric and gas MDMS implementations
Experience working IBM power series infrastructure
Familiarity with AMI head?end systems and communication networks
Experience with large?scale AMI deployments or enterprise transformations
Knowledge of regulatory reporting, audits, and compliance requirements
Experience supporting time?of?use, net metering, demand response, or EV programs

Key Competencies

Strong hands on experience with Oracle databases (SQL tuning, PL/SQL programming, data validation, performance troubleshooting)
Experience supporting applications on AIX/Linux platforms, including job monitoring and log analysis
Experience working in Oracle WebLogic platform
Proficient in Python/UNIX scripting for automation, data analysis, operational reporting, and problem resolution
Experience troubleshooting application, database, and OS level issues in a production environment
Hands on experience with enterprise scheduling/workload automation tools, such as UC4
Experience supporting mission critical batch processing and time sensitive data flows

Work Environment

Location: Remote but must be local to San Ramon, CA

Compensation & Benefits

Pay Range: The approximate pay range for this position is between $50.00
and $69.00
. Please note that the pay range provided is a good faith estimate. Final compensation may vary based on factors including but not limited to background, knowledge, skills, and location. We comply with local wage minimums.
Medical, Dental, & Vision Insurance Plans
Employee-Owned Profit Sharing (ESOP)
401K offered

About KellyMitchell

At KellyMitchell, our culture is world class. We’re movers and shakers! We don’t mind a bit of friendly competition, and we reward hard work with unlimited potential for growth. This is an exciting opportunity to join a company known for innovative solutions and unsurpassed customer service. We're passionate about helping companies solve their biggest IT staffing & project solutions challenges. As an employee-owned, women-led organization serving Fortune 500 companies nationwide, we deliver expert service at a moment's notice.

Marketing Disclosure

By applying for this job, you agree to receive calls, AI-generated calls, text messages, or emails from KellyMitchell and its affiliates, and contracted partners. Frequency varies for text messages. Message and data rates may apply. Carriers are not liable for delayed or undelivered messages. You can reply STOP to cancel and HELP for help. You can access our privacy policy at https://www.careers.kellymitchell.com/privacy-policy

jobs.title: Project Coordinator
jobs.description:
Job Description

This Opportunity

WSP is seeking Project Coordinators to join our Project Controls team. These roles are out of our offices in Ft. Worth TX, Meridian ID, San Diego CA, and Phoenix AZ.

Your Impact

Roles & Responsibilities

Working closely with all levels of management and disciplines/departments; and coordinating & communicating relevant project information to the team
Providing high-level coordination/administrative support to the client
Provide proposal & opportunity support in ERP
Provide pricing tool and change order support; may also include tracking
Acting as liaison between WSP and clients as required
Scheduling and attending project meetings (internal or with client)
Documenting and following up on important actions and decisions from meetings
Assist with the management of project/program documents utilizing platforms such as SharePoint, ProjectWise, etc. (organizing, uploading, and maintaining documentation)
Other administrative tasks as assigned

Who You Are

Required Education/Experience:

High School diploma
7 years of project support/administrative experience
Experience supporting a high volume of projects
Ability to handle a fast-paced environment
Intermediate analytical and problem-solving skills
Intermediate to Advanced Microsoft Office Suite skills
Strong attention to detail and a good communicator
Ability to work independently and as part of a team

Preferred Qualifications:

Bachelor’s degree or equivalent industry-related experience
Experience with MS Project, MS Visio, Power BI, Oracle (ERP) , and ProjectWise

WSP Benefits:

WSP provides a comprehensive suite of benefits focused on providing health and financial stability throughout the employee’s career. These benefits include coverage related to medical, dental, vision, disability, and life; retirement savings; paid sick leave; paid vacation (or other personal time); paid parental leave; and paid time off for purposes of bereavement, voting, and/or attendance at naturalization proceedings.

Compensation:

Expected Salary (all locations): $30-$42/hour

WSP USA is providing the compensation range that the company in good faith believes it might pay and offer for this position, based on the successful applicant’s education, experience, knowledge, skills, abilities in addition to internal equity and specific geographic location. WSP USA reserves the right to ultimately pay more or less than the posted range and offer additional benefits and other compensation, depending on circumstances not related to an applicant’s sex or other status protected by local, state, and/or federal law.

About WSP

ABOUT US

WSP USA is the U.S. operating company of WSP, one of the world's leading engineering and professional services firms. Dedicated to serving local communities, we are engineers, planners, technical experts, strategic advisors and construction management professionals. WSP USA designs lasting solutions in the buildings, transportation, energy, water and environment markets. With more than 15,000 employees in over 300 offices across the U.S., we partner with our clients to help communities prosper.

www.wsp.com

WSP provides a flexible and agile workplace model while meeting client needs. Employees are also afforded a comprehensive suite of benefits including medical, dental, vision, disability, life, and retirement savings focused on providing health and financial stability throughout the employee’s career.

At WSP, we want to give our employees the challenges they seek to grow their careers and knowledge base. Your daily contributions to your team will be essential in meeting client objectives, goals and challenges. Are you ready to get started?

WSP USA (and all of its U.S. companies) is an Equal Opportunity Employer Race/Age/Color/Religion/Sex/Sexual Orientation/Gender Identity/National Origin/Disability or Protected Veteran Status.

The selected candidate must be authorized to work in the United States.

NOTICE TO THIRD PARTY AGENCIES:

WSP does not accept unsolicited resumes from recruiters, employment agencies, or other staffing services. Unsolicited resumes include any resume or hiring document sent to WSP in the absence of a signed Service Agreement where WSP has expressly requested recruitment/staffing services specific to the position at hand. Any unsolicited resumes, including those submitted to hiring managers or other business leaders, will become the property of WSP and WSP will have the right to hire that candidate without reservation – no fee or other compensation will be owed or paid to the recruiter, employment agency, or other staffing service.

Madison, a city surrounded by water, has received accolades for being the greenest city in America (NerdWallet), the best city for renters (SmartAsset), the fittest city in America (Fitbit), and the third best metro in the US for young professionals (Forbes Advisor).

More than just important work
.

Our uniquely themed campus was designed to heighten your ability to get stuff done in your office, a conference room, or by the fireplace in a comfy chair. All meals are restaurant-quality but cost only a few dollars, and they’re prepared by a team comprised of kitchen talent from restaurants around the country. And, after five years here, you’ll earn a four-week sabbatical anywhere in the world. Staff have kayaked in Patagonia, attended a Beyoncé concert in Ireland, built a library in Tanzania, and run a marathon in Antarctica.

We offer comprehensive benefits to keep you healthy and happy as you grow in your life and career, and your merit-based compensation will reflect the impact your work has on the company and our customers. You'll also be eligible for annual raises and bonuses, as well as stock grants, which give you an even greater stake in the success of Epic and our customers. Healthcare is global, and building the best ideas from around the world into Epic software is a point of pride. As an Equal Opportunity Employer, we know that inclusive teams design software that supports the delivery of quality care for all patients, so diversity, equity, and inclusion are written into our principles. Please see our full non-discrimination statement at https://careers.epic.com/EEO.

Bachelor's degree or greater
A history of academic excellence or professional success
Willingness to travel occasionally to customer locations
Eligible to work in the U.S. without visa sponsorship
Relocation to the Madison, WI area (reimbursed)
COVID-19 vaccination
